It is 701 meters away in a straight line. == Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Annotations === {{Reflist|group="Note"}} === References === {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|title=Upper Volume of the Takamori Town History|author=Takamori Town History Compilation Committee|publisher=Takamori Town History Publication Committee|year=1972|ref=harv}} == Related Topics == {{Commonscat}} * {{ill|Yamabuki Jin'ya|ja|山吹陣屋}} [[Category:Shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Takamori, Nagano]] [[Category:History of Takamori, Nagano]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Taisan Shrine-qq]] [[ja:泰山神社]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Taisan Shrine-qq]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Taisan Shrine-qq]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Taisan Shrine-qq]] [[Category:dd]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:Taisan Shrine]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:Taisan Shrine]] [[Category:subst:NAMESPACE]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:subst:MAINSPACE]][[sn:Taisan Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Taisan Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Taisan Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Taisan Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:subst:MAINSPACE]][[sn:Taisan Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Taisan Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Taisan Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Taisan Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:泰山神社]]

{{Shrine |name = Taizan Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image =[[File:Taizanjinnja2.jpg|300px]]
Taizan Shrine's Torii Gate and Grand Shrine Inscription |location = {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}{{ill|Shimoina District, Nagano|ja|下伊那郡}}{{ill|Takamori, Nagano|ja|高森町 (長野県)}} Oaza Yamabuki Azamaruyama 1048{{Cite web|title=Shrine Introduction Iii Branch|url=https://www.nagano-jinjacho.jp/shibu/hani.htm|publisher=Nagano Shrine Association|accessdate=2021-12-04}} | Latitude = 35 | Lat_min = 34 | Lat_sec = 28.5 | Longitude = 137 | Lon_min = 53 | Lon_sec = 18.4 |ISO = JP-20 |Label_Position = right |Map = Japan Nagano |deity = {{ill|Homuda Wake no Mikoto|ja|応神天皇}}・{{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}}・{{ill|Susanoo|ja|スサノオ}}・Kodama Hikonomikoto・{{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方命}} {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1120 }} |Status = |consecration_year = {{ill|Kan'ei|ja|寛永}} Period {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1118 }} |Main_Hall = |Alias = |Festival = |Ritual = }} [[File:Taizanjinnja1.jpg|thumb|Main Hall]] '''Taizan Shrine''' (泰山神社) is a {{ill|shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}, {{ill|Shimoina District|ja|下伊那郡}}, {{ill|Takamori, Nagano|ja|高森町}}{{Cite web|title=Mapion Directory Taizan Shrine|url=https://www.mapion.co.jp/phonebook/M06005/20403/ILSP0061155717_ipclm/|publisher=ONE COMPATH|accessdate=2021-12-04}}. On registry records, it is listed as "𣳾{{Refnest|group="Note"|"券" written with the top half as "氺".}}"{{Cite web|title=Information about Taizan Shrine|url=https://www.houjin-bangou.nta.go.jp/henkorireki-johoto.html?selHouzinNo=4100005010121|website=Corporate Number Publication Site|publisher=National Tax Agency|date=2015-10-05|accessdate=2021-12-04}}. == Deities == According to sources {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1120 }}: * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|lt=Homuda Wake no Mikoto|ja|応神天皇}} * {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}} * {{ill|Susanoo|ja|スサノオ}} * Kodama Hikonomikoto * {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方命}} == History == The shrine was historically known as "Hachiman Shrine," "Ōmiya," or "Ōmiya Sansha," and was highly revered by the {{ill|Zakōji clan|ja|座光寺氏}} {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |pp=1118–1119 }}. After the {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} period, it merged with other shrines in {{ill|Yamabuki Village|ja|山吹村}}, forming its current structure and name {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1120 }}. It is a shrine closely tied to the Yamabuki Kamihira, Yamabuki Naka, and Shimohei districts of Takamori{{Cite web|title=Taizan Shrine|url=https://yamabukiku.main.jp/taizan.html|publisher=Yamabuki Ward of Takamori Town|accessdate=2021-12-07}}. === Chronology === * {{ill|Kan'ei|ja|寛永}} Period: Shrine established (as recorded in the shrine's detailed ledger) {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1118 }}. * {{ill|An'ei|ja|安永}} 1 (1772): Ōmiya Sansha was rebuilt and relocated {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1119 }}. * 1881 (Meiji 14): Kodama Shrine was merged and renamed "Yamabuki Shrine" {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1120 }}. * 1911 (Meiji 44): Suwa Shrine was merged, and the name was changed to the current Taizan Shrine {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1120 }}. * 2009 (Heisei 21), April 3: The shrine's main hall, kagura hall, office, and cultural facilities were destroyed by fire{{Cite news|title=Spring Festival at Taizan Shrine in Takamori Town|url=https://minamishinshu.jp/news/culture/高森町の泰山神社で春祭り.html|newspaper=Minamishinshu Shimbun|publisher=Minamishinshu Shimbunsha|date=2013-04-18|accessdate=2021-12-05}}. * 2012 (Heisei 24), June: The shrine was rebuilt. * 2015 (Heisei 27), October 5: Designated {{ill|corporate number|ja|法人番号}} 4100005010121. == Grounds == According to sources {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |pp=1121–1122 }}: * {{ill|Garden|ja|庭園}}: A garden with a pond is arranged at the front of the precincts. * {{ill|Path|ja|参道}}: The garden path leads alongside the garden. A pair of stone lanterns (dated Man'ei era) stand here. * {{ill|Torii|ja|鳥居}}: Built in 1901 (Meiji 34), the torii is a stone Myōjin-style gate. * Grand Shrine Inscription Monument: Erected on April 10, 1923 (Taishō 12). Located to the right of the above torii, with inscriptions by {{ill|Kameo Mitsutomi|ja|神尾光臣}}. * {{ill|Komainu|ja|狛犬}}: Positioned beyond the torii. Built in 1940 (Shōwa 15) through offerings from parishioners. * Second Torii: Relocated from the merged Kodama Shrine in 1881. * {{ill|Stone lanterns|ja|石灯籠}}: Present on either side of the second torii (date unknown). They are rare four-legged Miyadachi-style lanterns. * Stele of {{ill|Konpira Gongen|ja|金毘羅大権現}} and {{ill|Akiha Gongen|ja|秋葉権現}}: Positioned to the left of the main hall. == Auxiliary Shrines == According to sources. Parentheses indicate deities {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1120 }}: * Shinmei Shrine ({{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}}) * Suwa Shrine (Takeminakata) * Inari Shrine ({{ill|Toyoukehime|ja|トヨウケビメ}}) * Asama Shrine ({{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|コノハナノサクヤビメ}}) * Mountain God Shrine ({{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|オオヤマツミ}}) == Festivals == * Spring Festival: Features flower dances, Okame dances, and {{ill|Shishi-mai|ja|獅子舞}}. == Cultural Properties == * Sword marked "Made by Nagachika of Shinano Province": A sword by the Shinano swordsmith Nagachika (Tenbun–Keichō eras). Length: 2 shaku, 3 sun, 4 bu. Originally belonging to the Zakōji family, it was bought back through parishioners' donations and enshrined {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |pp=1120–1121 }}. == Access == {{Maplink2|frame=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-width=220|frame-height=240|text=Map. Shimodaira Station is in the lower right.}} ;[[Public Transport]] :The nearest station is {{ill|Shimodaira Station|ja|下平駅}} on the {{ill|Iida Line|ja|飯田線}} operated by {{ill|JR Central|ja|東海旅客鉄道}}. It is 701 meters away in a straight line. == Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Annotations === {{Reflist|group="Note"}} === References === {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|title=Upper Volume of the Takamori Town History|author=Takamori Town History Compilation Committee|publisher=Takamori Town History Publication Committee|year=1972|ref=harv}} == Related Topics == {{Commonscat}} * {{ill|Yamabuki Jin'ya|ja|山吹陣屋}} [[Category:Shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Takamori, Nagano]] [[Category:History of Takamori, Nagano]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Taisan Shrine-qq]] [[ja:泰山神社]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Taisan Shrine-qq]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Taisan Shrine-qq]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Taisan Shrine-qq]] [[Category:dd]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:Taisan Shrine]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:Taisan Shrine]] [[Category:subst:NAMESPACE]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:subst:NAMESPACE]][[sn:Taisan Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Taisan Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Taisan Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Taisan Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:subst:NAMESPACE]][[sn:Taisan Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Taisan Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Taisan Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Taisan Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:泰山神社]]

Parentheses indicate deities {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1120 }}: * Shinmei Shrine ({{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}}) * Suwa Shrine (Takeminakata) * Inari Shrine ({{ill|Toyoukehime|ja|トヨウケビメ}}) * Asama Shrine ({{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|コノハナノサクヤビメ}}) * Mountain God Shrine ({{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|オオヤマツミ}}) == Festivals == * Spring Festival: Features flower dances, Okame dances, and {{ill|Shishi-mai|ja|獅子舞}}. == Cultural Properties == * Sword marked "Made by Nagachika of Shinano Province": A sword by the Shinano swordsmith Nagachika (Tenbun–Keichō eras). Length: 2 shaku, 3 sun, 4 bu. Originally belonging to the Zakōji family, it was bought back through parishioners' donations and enshrined {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |pp=1120–1121 }}. == Access == {{Maplink2|frame=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-width=220|frame-height=240|text=Map. Shimodaira Station is in the lower right.}} ;[[Public Transport]] :The nearest station is {{ill|Shimodaira Station|ja|下平駅}} on the {{ill|Iida Line|ja|飯田線}} operated by {{ill|JR Central|ja|東海旅客鉄道}}. It is 701 meters away in a straight line. == Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Annotations === {{Reflist|group="Note"}} === References === {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|title=Upper Volume of the Takamori Town History|author=Takamori Town History Compilation Committee|publisher=Takamori Town History Publication Committee|year=1972|ref=harv}} == Related Topics == {{Commonscat}} * {{ill|Yamabuki Jin'ya|ja|山吹陣屋}} [[Category:Shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Takamori, Nagano]] [[Category:History of Takamori, Nagano]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Taisan Shrine-qq]] [[ja:泰山神社]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Taisan Shrine-qq]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Taisan Shrine-qq]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Taisan Shrine-qq]] [[Category:dd]] [[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:Taisan Shrine]][[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:new pages]] [[Category:Taisan Shrine]] [[Category:q--q]] [[sn:Taisan Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Taisan Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Taisan Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Taisan Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Taisan Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Taisan Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Taisan Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Taisan Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:泰山神社]]

{{Shrine |name = Taizan Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image =[[File:Taizanjinnja2.jpg|300px]]
Taizan Shrine's Torii Gate and Grand Shrine Inscription |location = {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}{{ill|Shimoina District, Nagano|ja|下伊那郡}}{{ill|Takamori, Nagano|ja|高森町 (長野県)}} Oaza Yamabuki Azamaruyama 1048{{Cite web|title=Shrine Introduction Iii Branch|url=https://www.nagano-jinjacho.jp/shibu/hani.htm|publisher=Nagano Shrine Association|accessdate=2021-12-04}} | Latitude = 35 | Lat_min = 34 | Lat_sec = 28.5 | Longitude = 137 | Lon_min = 53 | Lon_sec = 18.4 |ISO = JP-20 |Label_Position = right |Map = Japan Nagano |deity = {{ill|Homuda Wake no Mikoto|ja|応神天皇}}・{{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}}・{{ill|Susanoo|ja|スサノオ}}・Kodama Hikonomikoto・{{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方命}} {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1120 }} |Status = |consecration_year = {{ill|Kan'ei|ja|寛永}} Period {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1118 }} |Main_Hall = |Alias = |Festival = |Ritual = }} [[File:Taizanjinnja1.jpg|thumb|Main Hall]] '''Taizan Shrine''' (泰山神社) is a {{ill|shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}, {{ill|Shimoina District|ja|下伊那郡}}, {{ill|Takamori, Nagano|ja|高森町}}{{Cite web|title=Mapion Directory Taizan Shrine|url=https://www.mapion.co.jp/phonebook/M06005/20403/ILSP0061155717_ipclm/|publisher=ONE COMPATH|accessdate=2021-12-04}}. On registry records, it is listed as "𣳾{{Refnest|group="Note"|"券" written with the top half as "氺".}}"{{Cite web|title=Information about Taizan Shrine|url=https://www.houjin-bangou.nta.go.jp/henkorireki-johoto.html?selHouzinNo=4100005010121|website=Corporate Number Publication Site|publisher=National Tax Agency|date=2015-10-05|accessdate=2021-12-04}}. == Deities == According to sources {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1120 }}: * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|lt=Homuda Wake no Mikoto|ja|応神天皇}} * {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}} * {{ill|Susanoo|ja|スサノオ}} * Kodama Hikonomikoto * {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方命}} == History == The shrine was historically known as "Hachiman Shrine," "Ōmiya," or "Ōmiya Sansha," and was highly revered by the {{ill|Zakōji clan|ja|座光寺氏}} {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |pp=1118–1119 }}. After the {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} period, it merged with other shrines in {{ill|Yamabuki Village|ja|山吹村}}, forming its current structure and name {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1120 }}. It is a shrine closely tied to the Yamabuki Kamihira, Yamabuki Naka, and Shimohei districts of Takamori{{Cite web|title=Taizan Shrine|url=https://yamabukiku.main.jp/taizan.html|publisher=Yamabuki Ward of Takamori Town|accessdate=2021-12-07}}. === Chronology === * {{ill|Kan'ei|ja|寛永}} Period: Shrine established (as recorded in the shrine's detailed ledger) {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1118 }}. * {{ill|An'ei|ja|安永}} 1 (1772): Ōmiya Sansha was rebuilt and relocated {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1119 }}. * 1881 (Meiji 14): Kodama Shrine was merged and renamed "Yamabuki Shrine" {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1120 }}. * 1911 (Meiji 44): Suwa Shrine was merged, and the name was changed to the current Taizan Shrine {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1120 }}. * 2009 (Heisei 21), April 3: The shrine's main hall, kagura hall, office, and cultural facilities were destroyed by fire{{Cite news|title=Spring Festival at Taizan Shrine in Takamori Town|url=https://minamishinshu.jp/news/culture/高森町の泰山神社で春祭り.html|newspaper=Minamishinshu Shimbun|publisher=Minamishinshu Shimbunsha|date=2013-04-18|accessdate=2021-12-05}}. * 2012 (Heisei 24), June: The shrine was rebuilt. * 2015 (Heisei 27), October 5: Designated {{ill|corporate number|ja|法人番号}} 4100005010121. == Grounds == According to sources {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |pp=1121–1122 }}: * {{ill|Garden|ja|庭園}}: A garden with a pond is arranged at the front of the precincts. * {{ill|Path|ja|参道}}: The garden path leads alongside the garden. A pair of stone lanterns (dated Man'ei era) stand here. * {{ill|Torii|ja|鳥居}}: Built in 1901 (Meiji 34), the torii is a stone Myōjin-style gate. * Grand Shrine Inscription Monument: Erected on April 10, 1923 (Taishō 12). Located to the right of the above torii, with inscriptions by {{ill|Kameo Mitsutomi|ja|神尾光臣}}. * {{ill|Komainu|ja|狛犬}}: Positioned beyond the torii. Built in 1940 (Shōwa 15) through offerings from parishioners. * Second Torii: Relocated from the merged Kodama Shrine in 1881. * {{ill|Stone lanterns|ja|石灯籠}}: Present on either side of the second torii (date unknown). They are rare four-legged Miyadachi-style lanterns. * Stele of {{ill|Konpira Gongen|ja|金毘羅大権現}} and {{ill|Akiha Gongen|ja|秋葉権現}}: Positioned to the left of the main hall. == Auxiliary Shrines == According to sources. Parentheses indicate deities {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1120 }}: * Shinmei Shrine ({{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}}) * Suwa Shrine (Takeminakata) * Inari Shrine ({{ill|Toyoukehime|ja|トヨウケビメ}}) * Asama Shrine ({{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|コノハナノサクヤビメ}}) * Mountain God Shrine ({{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|オオヤマツミ}}) == Festivals == * Spring Festival: Features flower dances, Okame dances, and {{ill|Shishi-mai|ja|獅子舞}}. == Cultural Properties == * Sword marked "Made by Nagachika of Shinano Province": A sword by the Shinano swordsmith Nagachika (Tenbun–Keichō eras). Length: 2 shaku, 3 sun, 4 bu. Originally belonging to the Zakōji family, it was bought back through parishioners' donations and enshrined {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |pp=1120–1121 }}. == Access == {{Maplink2|frame=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-width=220|frame-height=240|text=Map. Shimodaira Station is in the lower right.}} ;[[Public Transport]] :The nearest station is {{ill|Shimodaira Station|ja|下平駅}} on the {{ill|Iida Line|ja|飯田線}} operated by {{ill|JR Central|ja|東海旅客鉄道}}. It is 701 meters away in a straight line. == Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Annotations === {{Reflist|group="Note"}} === References === {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|title=Upper Volume of the Takamori Town History|author=Takamori Town History Compilation Committee|publisher=Takamori Town History Publication Committee|year=1972|ref=harv}} == See Also == {{Commonscat}} * {{ill|Yamabuki Jin'ya|ja|山吹陣屋}} [[Category:Shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Takamori, Nagano]] [[Category:History of Takamori, Nagano]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Taisan Shrine-qq]] [[ja:泰山神社]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Taisan Shrine-qq]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Taisan Shrine-qq]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Taisan Shrine-qq]] [[Category:dd]] [[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:Taisan Shrine]][[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:new pages]] [[Category:Taisan Shrine]] [[Category:q--q]] [[sn:Taisan Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Taisan Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Taisan Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Taisan Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Taisan Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Taisan Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Taisan Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Taisan Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:泰山神社]]

It is a shrine closely tied to the Yamabuki Kamihira, Yamabuki Naka, and Shimohei districts of Takamori{{Cite web|title=Taizan Shrine|url=https://yamabukiku.main.jp/taizan.html|publisher=Yamabuki Ward of Takamori Town|accessdate=2021-12-07}}. === Chronology === * {{ill|Kan'ei|ja|寛永}} Period: Shrine established (as recorded in the shrine's detailed ledger) {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1118 }}. * {{ill|An'ei|ja|安永}} 1 (1772): Ōmiya Sansha was rebuilt and relocated {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1119 }}. * 1881 (Meiji 14): Kodama Shrine was merged and renamed "Yamabuki Shrine" {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1120 }}. * 1911 (Meiji 44): Suwa Shrine was merged, and the name was changed to the current Taizan Shrine {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1120 }}. * 2009 (Heisei 21), April 3: The shrine's main hall, kagura hall, office, and cultural facilities were destroyed by fire{{Cite news|title=Spring Festival at Taizan Shrine in Takamori Town|url=https://minamishinshu.jp/news/culture/高森町の泰山神社で春祭り.html|newspaper=Minamishinshu Shimbun|publisher=Minamishinshu Shimbunsha|date=2013-04-18|accessdate=2021-12-05}}. * 2012 (Heisei 24), June: The shrine was rebuilt. * 2015 (Heisei 27), October 5: Designated {{ill|corporate number|ja|法人番号}} 4100005010121. == Grounds == According to sources {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |pp=1121–1122 }}: * {{ill|Garden|ja|庭園}}: A garden with a pond is arranged at the front of the precincts. * {{ill|Path|ja|参道}}: The garden path leads alongside the garden. A pair of stone lanterns (dated Man'ei era) stand here. * {{ill|Torii|ja|鳥居}}: Built in 1901 (Meiji 34), the torii is a stone Myōjin-style gate. * Grand Shrine Inscription Monument: Erected on April 10, 1923 (Taishō 12). Located to the right of the above torii, with inscriptions by {{ill|Kameo Mitsutomi|ja|神尾光臣}}. * {{ill|Komainu|ja|狛犬}}: Positioned beyond the torii. Built in 1940 (Shōwa 15) through offerings from parishioners. * Second Torii: Relocated from the merged Kodama Shrine in 1881. * {{ill|Stone lanterns|ja|石灯籠}}: Present on either side of the second torii (date unknown). They are rare four-legged Miyadachi-style lanterns. * Stele of {{ill|Konpira Gongen|ja|金毘羅大権現}} and {{ill|Akiha Gongen|ja|秋葉権現}}: Positioned to the left of the main hall. == Auxiliary Shrines == According to sources. Parentheses indicate deities {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1120 }}: * Shinmei Shrine ({{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}}) * Suwa Shrine (Takeminakata) * Inari Shrine ({{ill|Toyoukehime|ja|トヨウケビメ}}) * Asama Shrine ({{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|コノハナノサクヤビメ}}) * Mountain God Shrine ({{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|オオヤマツミ}}) == Festivals == * Spring Festival: Features flower dances, Okame dances, and {{ill|Shishi-mai|ja|獅子舞}}. == Cultural Properties == * Sword marked "Made by Nagachika of Shinano Province": A sword by the Shinano swordsmith Nagachika (Tenbun–Keichō eras). Length: 2 shaku, 3 sun, 4 bu. Originally belonging to the Zakōji family, it was bought back through parishioners' donations and enshrined {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |pp=1120–1121 }}. == Access == {{Maplink2|frame=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-width=220|frame-height=240|text=Map. Shimodaira Station is in the lower right.}} ;[[Public Transport]] :The nearest station is {{ill|Shimodaira Station|ja|下平駅}} on the {{ill|Iida Line|ja|飯田線}} operated by {{ill|JR Central|ja|東海旅客鉄道}}. It is 701 meters away in a straight line. == Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Annotations === {{Reflist|group="Note"}} === References === {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|title=Upper Volume of the Takamori Town History|author=Takamori Town History Compilation Committee|publisher=Takamori Town History Publication Committee|year=1972|ref=harv}} == See Also == {{Commonscat}} * {{ill|Yamabuki Jin'ya|ja|山吹陣屋}} [[Category:Shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Takamori, Nagano]] [[Category:History of Takamori, Nagano]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qqqqqqqqqq]] [[Category:qq-Taisan Shrine-->[[Category:qq deprecated close]] [[ja:泰山神社]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qqqqqqqqqq]] [[Category:qq-Taisan Shrine-->[[Category:qq deprecated close]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qqqqqqqqqq]] [[Category:qq-Taisan Shrine-->[[Category:qq deprecated close]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qqqqqqqqqq]] [[Category:qq-Taisan Shrine-->[[Category:qq deprecated close]] [[Category:dd]] [[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:Taisan Shrine]][[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:new pages]] [[Category:Taisan Shrine]] [[Category:q--q]] [[sn:Taisan Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Taisan Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Taisan Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Taisan Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Taisan Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Taisan Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Taisan Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Taisan Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:泰山神社]]

{{Shrine |name = Taizan Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image =[[File:Taizanjinnja2.jpg|300px]]
Taizan Shrine's Torii Gate and Grand Shrine Inscription |location = {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}{{ill|Shimoina District, Nagano|ja|下伊那郡}}{{ill|Takamori, Nagano|ja|高森町 (長野県)}} Oaza Yamabuki Azamaruyama 1048{{Cite web|title=Shrine Introduction Iii Branch|url=https://www.nagano-jinjacho.jp/shibu/hani.htm|publisher=Nagano Shrine Association|accessdate=2021-12-04}} | Latitude = 35 | Lat_min = 34 | Lat_sec = 28.5 | Longitude = 137 | Lon_min = 53 | Lon_sec = 18.4 |ISO = JP-20 |Label_Position = right |Map = Japan Nagano |deity = {{ill|Homuda Wake no Mikoto|ja|応神天皇}}・{{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}}・{{ill|Susanoo|ja|スサノオ}}・Kodama Hikonomikoto・{{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方命}} {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1120 }} |Status = |consecration_year = {{ill|Kan'ei|ja|寛永}} Period {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1118 }} |Main_Hall = |Alias = |Festival = |Ritual = }} [[File:Taizanjinnja1.jpg|thumb|Main Hall]] '''Taizan Shrine''' (泰山神社) is a {{ill|shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}, {{ill|Shimoina District|ja|下伊那郡}}, {{ill|Takamori, Nagano|ja|高森町}}{{Cite web|title=Mapion Directory Taizan Shrine|url=https://www.mapion.co.jp/phonebook/M06005/20403/ILSP0061155717_ipclm/|publisher=ONE COMPATH|accessdate=2021-12-04}}. On registry records, it is listed as "𣳾{{Refnest|group="Note"|"券" written with the top half as "氺".}}"{{Cite web|title=Information about Taizan Shrine|url=https://www.houjin-bangou.nta.go.jp/henkorireki-johoto.html?selHouzinNo=4100005010121|website=Corporate Number Publication Site|publisher=National Tax Agency|date=2015-10-05|accessdate=2021-12-04}}. == Deities == According to sources {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1120 }}: * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|lt=Homuda Wake no Mikoto|ja|応神天皇}} * {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}} * {{ill|Susanoo|ja|スサノオ}} * Kodama Hikonomikoto * {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方命}} == History == The shrine was historically known as "Hachiman Shrine," "Ōmiya," or "Ōmiya Sansha," and was highly revered by the {{ill|Zakōji clan|ja|座光寺氏}} {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |pp=1118–1119 }}. After the {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} period, it merged with other shrines in {{ill|Yamabuki Village|ja|山吹村}}, forming its current structure and name {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1120 }}. It is a shrine closely tied to the Yamabuki Kamihira, Yamabuki Naka, and Shimohei districts of Takamori{{Cite web|title=Taizan Shrine|url=https://yamabukiku.main.jp/taizan.html|publisher=Yamabuki Ward of Takamori Town|accessdate=2021-12-07}}. === Chronology === * {{ill|Kan'ei|ja|寛永}} Period: Shrine established (as recorded in the shrine's detailed ledger) {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1118 }}. * {{ill|An'ei|ja|安永}} 1 (1772): Ōmiya Sansha was rebuilt and relocated {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1119 }}. * 1881 (Meiji 14): Kodama Shrine was merged and renamed "Yamabuki Shrine" {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1120 }}. * 1911 (Meiji 44): Suwa Shrine was merged, and the name was changed to the current Taizan Shrine {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1120 }}. * 2009 (Heisei 21), April 3: The shrine's main hall, kagura hall, office, and cultural facilities were destroyed by fire{{Cite news|title=Spring Festival at Taizan Shrine in Takamori Town|url=https://minamishinshu.jp/news/culture/高森町の泰山神社で春祭り.html|newspaper=Minamishinshu Shimbun|publisher=Minamishinshu Shimbunsha|date=2013-04-18|accessdate=2021-12-05}}. * 2012 (Heisei 24), June: The shrine was rebuilt. * 2015 (Heisei 27), October 5: Designated {{ill|corporate number|ja|法人番号}} 4100005010121. == Grounds == According to sources {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |pp=1121–1122 }}: * {{ill|Garden|ja|庭園}}: A garden with a pond is arranged at the front of the precincts. * {{ill|Path|ja|参道}}: The garden path leads alongside the garden. A pair of stone lanterns (dated Man'ei era) stand here. * {{ill|Torii|ja|鳥居}}: Built in 1901 (Meiji 34), the torii is a stone Myōjin-style gate. * Grand Shrine Inscription Monument: Erected on April 10, 1923 (Taishō 12). Located to the right of the above torii, with inscriptions by {{ill|Kameo Mitsutomi|ja|神尾光臣}}. * {{ill|Komainu|ja|狛犬}}: Positioned beyond the torii. Built in 1940 (Shōwa 15) through offerings from parishioners. * Second Torii: Relocated from the merged Kodama Shrine in 1881. * {{ill|Stone lanterns|ja|石灯籠}}: Present on either side of the second torii (date unknown). They are rare four-legged Miyadachi-style lanterns. * Stele of {{ill|Konpira Gongen|ja|金毘羅大権現}} and {{ill|Akiha Gongen|ja|秋葉権現}}: Positioned to the left of the main hall. == Auxiliary Shrines == According to sources. Parentheses indicate deities {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1120 }}: * Shinmei Shrine ({{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}}) * Suwa Shrine (Takeminakata) * Inari Shrine ({{ill|Toyoukehime|ja|トヨウケビメ}}) * Asama Shrine ({{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|コノハナノサクヤビメ}}) * Mountain God Shrine ({{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|オオヤマツミ}}) == Festivals == * Spring Festival: Features flower dances, Okame dances, and {{ill|Shishi-mai|ja|獅子舞}}. == Cultural Properties == * Sword marked "Made by Nagachika of Shinano Province": A sword by the Shinano swordsmith Nagachika (Tenbun–Keichō eras). Length: 2 shaku, 3 sun, 4 bu. Originally belonging to the Zakōji family, it was bought back through parishioners' donations and enshrined {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |pp=1120–1121 }}. == Access == {{Maplink2|frame=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-width=220|frame-height=240|text=Map. Shimodaira Station is in the lower right.}} ;[[Public Transport]] :The nearest station is {{ill|Shimodaira Station|ja|下平駅}} on the {{ill|Iida Line|ja|飯田線}} operated by {{ill|JR Central|ja|東海旅客鉄道}}. It is 701 meters away in a straight line. == Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Annotations === {{Reflist|group="Note"}} === References === {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|title=Upper Volume of the Takamori Town History|author=Takamori Town History Compilation Committee|publisher=Takamori Town History Publication Committee|year=1972|ref=harv}} == See Also == {{Commonscat}} * {{ill|Yamabuki Jin'ya|ja|山吹陣屋}} [[Category:Shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Takamori, Nagano]] [[Category:History of Takamori, Nagano]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qqqqqqqqqq]] [[Category:qq deprecated start]][[Category:qq deprecated close]] [[ja:泰山神社]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qqqqqqqqqq]] [[Category:qq deprecated start]][[Category:qq deprecated close]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qqqqqqqqqq]] [[Category:qq deprecated start]][[Category:qq deprecated close]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qqqqqqqqqq]] [[Category:qq deprecated start]][[Category:qq deprecated close]] [[Category:dd]] [[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:Taisan Shrine]][[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:new pages]] [[Category:Taisan Shrine]] [[Category:q--q]] [[sn:Taisan Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Taisan Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Taisan Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Taisan Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Taisan Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Taisan Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Taisan Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Taisan Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:泰山神社]]

{{Shrine |name = Taizan Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image =[[File:Taizanjinnja2.jpg|300px]]
Taizan Shrine's Torii Gate and Grand Shrine Inscription |location = {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}{{ill|Shimoina District, Nagano|ja|下伊那郡}}{{ill|Takamori, Nagano|ja|高森町 (長野県)}} Oaza Yamabuki Azamaruyama 1048{{Cite web|title=Shrine Introduction Iii Branch|url=https://www.nagano-jinjacho.jp/shibu/hani.htm|publisher=Nagano Shrine Association|accessdate=2021-12-04}} | Latitude = 35 | Lat_min = 34 | Lat_sec = 28.5 | Longitude = 137 | Lon_min = 53 | Lon_sec = 18.4 |ISO = JP-20 |Label_Position = right |Map = Japan Nagano |deity = {{ill|Homuda Wake no Mikoto|ja|応神天皇}}・{{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}}・{{ill|Susanoo|ja|スサノオ}}・Kodama Hikonomikoto・{{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方命}} {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1120 }} |Status = |consecration_year = {{ill|Kan'ei|ja|寛永}} Period {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1118 }} |Main_Hall = |Alias = |Festival = |Ritual = }} [[File:Taizanjinnja1.jpg|thumb|Main Hall]] '''Taizan Shrine''' (泰山神社) is a {{ill|shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}, {{ill|Shimoina District|ja|下伊那郡}}, {{ill|Takamori, Nagano|ja|高森町}}{{Cite web|title=Mapion Directory Taizan Shrine|url=https://www.mapion.co.jp/phonebook/M06005/20403/ILSP0061155717_ipclm/|publisher=ONE COMPATH|accessdate=2021-12-04}}. On registry records, it is listed as "𣳾{{Refnest|group="Note"|"券" written with the top half as "氺".}}"{{Cite web|title=Information about Taizan Shrine|url=https://www.houjin-bangou.nta.go.jp/henkorireki-johoto.html?selHouzinNo=4100005010121|website=Corporate Number Publication Site|publisher=National Tax Agency|date=2015-10-05|accessdate=2021-12-04}}. == Deities == According to sources {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1120 }}: * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|lt=Homuda Wake no Mikoto|ja|応神天皇}} * {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}} * {{ill|Susanoo|ja|スサノオ}} * Kodama Hikonomikoto * {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方命}} == History == The shrine was historically known as "Hachiman Shrine," "Ōmiya," or "Ōmiya Sansha," and was highly revered by the {{ill|Zakōji clan|ja|座光寺氏}} {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |pp=1118–1119 }}. After the {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} period, it merged with other shrines in {{ill|Yamabuki Village|ja|山吹村}}, forming its current structure and name {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1120 }}. It is a shrine closely tied to the Yamabuki Kamihira, Yamabuki Naka, and Shimohei districts of Takamori{{Cite web|title=Taizan Shrine|url=https://yamabukiku.main.jp/taizan.html|publisher=Yamabuki Ward of Takamori Town|accessdate=2021-12-07}}. === Chronology === * {{ill|Kan'ei|ja|寛永}} Period: Shrine established (as recorded in the shrine's detailed ledger) {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1118 }}. * {{ill|An'ei|ja|安永}} 1 (1772): Ōmiya Sansha was rebuilt and relocated {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1119 }}. * 1881 (Meiji 14): Kodama Shrine was merged and renamed "Yamabuki Shrine" {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1120 }}. * 1911 (Meiji 44): Suwa Shrine was merged, and the name was changed to the current Taizan Shrine {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1120 }}. * 2009 (Heisei 21), April 3: The shrine's main hall, kagura hall, office, and cultural facilities were destroyed by fire{{Cite news|title=Spring Festival at Taizan Shrine in Takamori Town|url=https://minamishinshu.jp/news/culture/高森町の泰山神社で春祭り.html|newspaper=Minamishinshu Shimbun|publisher=Minamishinshu Shimbunsha|date=2013-04-18|accessdate=2021-12-05}}. * 2012 (Heisei 24), June: The shrine was rebuilt. * 2015 (Heisei 27), October 5: Designated {{ill|corporate number|ja|法人番号}} 4100005010121. == Grounds == According to sources {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |pp=1121–1122 }}: * {{ill|Garden|ja|庭園}}: A garden with a pond is arranged at the front of the precincts. * {{ill|Path|ja|参道}}: The garden path leads alongside the garden. A pair of stone lanterns (dated Man'ei era) stand here. * {{ill|Torii|ja|鳥居}}: Built in 1901 (Meiji 34), the torii is a stone Myōjin-style gate. * Grand Shrine Inscription Monument: Erected on April 10, 1923 (Taishō 12). Located to the right of the above torii, with inscriptions by {{ill|Kameo Mitsutomi|ja|神尾光臣}}. * {{ill|Komainu|ja|狛犬}}: Positioned beyond the torii. Built in 1940 (Shōwa 15) through offerings from parishioners. * Second Torii: Relocated from the merged Kodama Shrine in 1881. * {{ill|Stone lanterns|ja|石灯籠}}: Present on either side of the second torii (date unknown). They are rare four-legged Miyadachi-style lanterns. * Stele of {{ill|Konpira Gongen|ja|金毘羅大権現}} and {{ill|Akiha Gongen|ja|秋葉権現}}: Positioned to the left of the main hall. == Auxiliary Shrines == According to sources. Parentheses indicate deities {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1120 }}: * Shinmei Shrine ({{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}}) * Suwa Shrine (Takeminakata) * Inari Shrine ({{ill|Toyoukehime|ja|トヨウケビメ}}) * Asama Shrine ({{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|コノハナノサクヤビメ}}) * Mountain God Shrine ({{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|オオヤマツミ}}) == Festivals == * Spring Festival: Features flower dances, Okame dances, and {{ill|Shishi-mai|ja|獅子舞}}. == Cultural Properties == * Sword marked "Made by Nagachika of Shinano Province": A sword by the Shinano swordsmith Nagachika (Tenbun–Keichō eras). Length: 2 shaku, 3 sun, 4 bu. Originally belonging to the Zakōji family, it was bought back through parishioners' donations and enshrined {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |pp=1120–1121 }}. == Access == {{Maplink2|frame=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-width=220|frame-height=240|text=Map. Shimodaira Station is in the lower right.}} ;[[Public Transport]] :The nearest station is {{ill|Shimodaira Station|ja|下平駅}} on the {{ill|Iida Line|ja|飯田線}} operated by {{ill|JR Central|ja|東海旅客鉄道}}. It is 701 meters away in a straight line. == Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Annotations === {{Reflist|group="Note"}} === References === {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|title=Upper Volume of the Takamori Town History|author=Takamori Town History Compilation Committee|publisher=Takamori Town History Publication Committee|year=1972|ref=harv}} == See Also == {{Commonscat}} * {{ill|Yamabuki Jin'ya|ja|山吹陣屋}} [[Category:Shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Takamori, Nagano]] [[Category:History of Takamori, Nagano]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Taisan Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqqqqqqqqq]] [[Category:qq deprecated start]][[Category:qq deprecated close]] [[ja:泰山神社]] [[Category:qqqqqqqqqq]] [[Category:qq deprecated start]][[Category:qq deprecated close]] [[Category:qqqqqqqqqq]] [[Category:qq deprecated start]][[Category:qq deprecated close]] [[Category:qqqqqqqqqq]] [[Category:qq deprecated start]][[Category:qq deprecated close]] [[Category:dd]] [[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:Taisan Shrine]][[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:new pages]] [[Category:Taisan Shrine]] [[Category:q--q]] [[sn:Taisan Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Taisan Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Taisan Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Taisan Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Taisan Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Taisan Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Taisan Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Taisan Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:泰山神社]]

revision.comment: Removing from [[Category:Qq deprecated start]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]

Shimodaira Station is in the lower right.}} ;[[Public Transport]] :The nearest station is {{ill|Shimodaira Station|ja|下平駅}} on the {{ill|Iida Line|ja|飯田線}} operated by {{ill|JR Central|ja|東海旅客鉄道}}. It is 701 meters away in a straight line. == Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Annotations === {{Reflist|group="Note"}} === References === {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|title=Upper Volume of the Takamori Town History|author=Takamori Town History Compilation Committee|publisher=Takamori Town History Publication Committee|year=1972|ref=harv}} == See Also == {{Commonscat}} * {{ill|Yamabuki Jin'ya|ja|山吹陣屋}} [[Category:Shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Takamori, Nagano]] [[Category:History of Takamori, Nagano]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Taisan Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq deprecated close]] [[ja:泰山神社]] [[Category:qq deprecated close]] [[Category:qq deprecated close]] [[Category:qq deprecated close]] [[Category:dd]] [[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:Taisan Shrine]][[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:new pages]] [[Category:Taisan Shrine]] [[Category:q--q]] [[sn:Taisan Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Taisan Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Taisan Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Taisan Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Taisan Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Taisan Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Taisan Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Taisan Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:泰山神社]]

revision.comment: Removing from [[Category:Qq deprecated close]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{Shrine |name = Taizan Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image =[[File:Taizanjinnja2.jpg|300px]]
Taizan Shrine's Torii Gate and Grand Shrine Inscription |location = {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}{{ill|Shimoina District, Nagano|ja|下伊那郡}}{{ill|Takamori, Nagano|ja|高森町 (長野県)}} Oaza Yamabuki Azamaruyama 1048{{Cite web|title=Shrine Introduction Iii Branch|url=https://www.nagano-jinjacho.jp/shibu/hani.htm|publisher=Nagano Shrine Association|accessdate=2021-12-04}} | Latitude = 35 | Lat_min = 34 | Lat_sec = 28.5 | Longitude = 137 | Lon_min = 53 | Lon_sec = 18.4 |ISO = JP-20 |Label_Position = right |Map = Japan Nagano |deity = {{ill|Homuda Wake no Mikoto|ja|応神天皇}}・{{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}}・{{ill|Susanoo|ja|スサノオ}}・Kodama Hikonomikoto・{{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方命}} {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1120 }} |Status = |consecration_year = {{ill|Kan'ei|ja|寛永}} Period {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1118 }} |Main_Hall = |Alias = |Festival = |Ritual = }} [[File:Taizanjinnja1.jpg|thumb|Main Hall]] '''Taizan Shrine''' (泰山神社) is a {{ill|shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}, {{ill|Shimoina District|ja|下伊那郡}}, {{ill|Takamori, Nagano|ja|高森町}}{{Cite web|title=Mapion Directory Taizan Shrine|url=https://www.mapion.co.jp/phonebook/M06005/20403/ILSP0061155717_ipclm/|publisher=ONE COMPATH|accessdate=2021-12-04}}. On registry records, it is listed as "𣳾{{Refnest|group="Note"|"券" written with the top half as "氺".}}"{{Cite web|title=Information about Taizan Shrine|url=https://www.houjin-bangou.nta.go.jp/henkorireki-johoto.html?selHouzinNo=4100005010121|website=Corporate Number Publication Site|publisher=National Tax Agency|date=2015-10-05|accessdate=2021-12-04}}. == Deities == According to sources {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1120 }}: * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|lt=Homuda Wake no Mikoto|ja|応神天皇}} * {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}} * {{ill|Susanoo|ja|スサノオ}} * Kodama Hikonomikoto * {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方命}} == History == The shrine was historically known as "Hachiman Shrine," "Ōmiya," or "Ōmiya Sansha," and was highly revered by the {{ill|Zakōji clan|ja|座光寺氏}} {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |pp=1118–1119 }}. After the {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} period, it merged with other shrines in {{ill|Yamabuki Village|ja|山吹村}}, forming its current structure and name {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1120 }}. It is a shrine closely tied to the Yamabuki Kamihira, Yamabuki Naka, and Shimohei districts of Takamori{{Cite web|title=Taizan Shrine|url=https://yamabukiku.main.jp/taizan.html|publisher=Yamabuki Ward of Takamori Town|accessdate=2021-12-07}}. === Chronology === * {{ill|Kan'ei|ja|寛永}} Period: Shrine established (as recorded in the shrine's detailed ledger) {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1118 }}. * {{ill|An'ei|ja|安永}} 1 (1772): Ōmiya Sansha was rebuilt and relocated {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1119 }}. * 1881 (Meiji 14): Kodama Shrine was merged and renamed "Yamabuki Shrine" {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1120 }}. * 1911 (Meiji 44): Suwa Shrine was merged, and the name was changed to the current Taizan Shrine {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1120 }}. * 2009 (Heisei 21), April 3: The shrine's main hall, kagura hall, office, and cultural facilities were destroyed by fire{{Cite news|title=Spring Festival at Taizan Shrine in Takamori Town|url=https://minamishinshu.jp/news/culture/高森町の泰山神社で春祭り.html|newspaper=Minamishinshu Shimbun|publisher=Minamishinshu Shimbunsha|date=2013-04-18|accessdate=2021-12-05}}. * 2012 (Heisei 24), June: The shrine was rebuilt. * 2015 (Heisei 27), October 5: Designated {{ill|corporate number|ja|法人番号}} 4100005010121. == Grounds == According to sources {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |pp=1121–1122 }}: * {{ill|Garden|ja|庭園}}: A garden with a pond is arranged at the front of the precincts. * {{ill|Path|ja|参道}}: The garden path leads alongside the garden. A pair of stone lanterns (dated Man'ei era) stand here. * {{ill|Torii|ja|鳥居}}: Built in 1901 (Meiji 34), the torii is a stone Myōjin-style gate. * Grand Shrine Inscription Monument: Erected on April 10, 1923 (Taishō 12). Located to the right of the above torii, with inscriptions by {{ill|Kameo Mitsutomi|ja|神尾光臣}}. * {{ill|Komainu|ja|狛犬}}: Positioned beyond the torii. Built in 1940 (Shōwa 15) through offerings from parishioners. * Second Torii: Relocated from the merged Kodama Shrine in 1881. * {{ill|Stone lanterns|ja|石灯籠}}: Present on either side of the second torii (date unknown). They are rare four-legged Miyadachi-style lanterns. * Stele of {{ill|Konpira Gongen|ja|金毘羅大権現}} and {{ill|Akiha Gongen|ja|秋葉権現}}: Positioned to the left of the main hall. == Auxiliary Shrines == According to sources. Parentheses indicate deities {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1120 }}: * Shinmei Shrine ({{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}}) * Suwa Shrine (Takeminakata) * Inari Shrine ({{ill|Toyoukehime|ja|トヨウケビメ}}) * Asama Shrine ({{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|コノハナノサクヤビメ}}) * Mountain God Shrine ({{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|オオヤマツミ}}) == Festivals == * Spring Festival: Features flower dances, Okame dances, and {{ill|Shishi-mai|ja|獅子舞}}. == Cultural Properties == * Sword marked "Made by Nagachika of Shinano Province": A sword by the Shinano swordsmith Nagachika (Tenbun–Keichō eras). Length: 2 shaku, 3 sun, 4 bu. Originally belonging to the Zakōji family, it was bought back through parishioners' donations and enshrined {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |pp=1120–1121 }}. == Access == {{Maplink2|frame=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-width=220|frame-height=240|text=Map. Shimodaira Station is in the lower right.}} ;[[Public Transport]] :The nearest station is {{ill|Shimodaira Station|ja|下平駅}} on the {{ill|Iida Line|ja|飯田線}} operated by {{ill|JR Central|ja|東海旅客鉄道}}. It is 701 meters away in a straight line. == Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Annotations === {{Reflist|group="Note"}} === References === {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|title=Upper Volume of the Takamori Town History|author=Takamori Town History Compilation Committee|publisher=Takamori Town History Publication Committee|year=1972|ref=harv}} == See Also == {{Commonscat}} * {{ill|Yamabuki Jin'ya|ja|山吹陣屋}} [[Category:Shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Takamori, Nagano]] [[Category:History of Takamori, Nagano]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Taisan Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:泰山神社]] [[Category:dd]] [[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:Taisan Shrine]][[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:new pages]] [[Category:Taisan Shrine]] [[Category:q--q]] [[sn:Taisan Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Taisan Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Taisan Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Taisan Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Taisan Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Taisan Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Taisan Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Taisan Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:泰山神社]]

It is 701 meters away in a straight line. == Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Annotations === {{Reflist|group="Note"}} === References === {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|title=Upper Volume of the Takamori Town History|author=Takamori Town History Compilation Committee|publisher=Takamori Town History Publication Committee|year=1972|ref=harv}} == See Also == {{Commonscat}} * {{ill|Yamabuki Jin'ya|ja|山吹陣屋}} [[Category:Shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Takamori, Nagano]] [[Category:History of Takamori, Nagano]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Taisan Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:泰山神社]] [[Category:dd]] [[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:Taisan Shrine]][[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:new pages]] [[Category:Taisan Shrine]] [[Category:qq]] [[sn:Taisan Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Taisan Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Taisan Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Taisan Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Taisan Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Taisan Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Taisan Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Taisan Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Taisan Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:泰山神社]]

{{Shrine |name = Taizan Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image =[[File:Taizanjinnja2.jpg|300px]]
Taizan Shrine's Torii Gate and Grand Shrine Inscription |location = {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}{{ill|Shimoina District, Nagano|ja|下伊那郡}}{{ill|Takamori, Nagano|ja|高森町 (長野県)}} Oaza Yamabuki Azamaruyama 1048{{Cite web|title=Shrine Introduction Iii Branch|url=https://www.nagano-jinjacho.jp/shibu/hani.htm|publisher=Nagano Shrine Association|accessdate=2021-12-04}} | Latitude = 35 | Lat_min = 34 | Lat_sec = 28.5 | Longitude = 137 | Lon_min = 53 | Lon_sec = 18.4 |ISO = JP-20 |Label_Position = right |Map = Japan Nagano |deity = {{ill|Homuda Wake no Mikoto|ja|応神天皇}}・{{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}}・{{ill|Susanoo|ja|スサノオ}}・Kodama Hikonomikoto・{{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方命}} {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1120 }} |Status = |consecration_year = {{ill|Kan'ei|ja|寛永}} Period {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1118 }} |Main_Hall = |Alias = |Festival = |Ritual = }} [[File:Taizanjinnja1.jpg|thumb|Main Hall]] '''Taizan Shrine''' (泰山神社) is a {{ill|shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}, {{ill|Shimoina District|ja|下伊那郡}}, {{ill|Takamori, Nagano|ja|高森町}}{{Cite web|title=Mapion Directory Taizan Shrine|url=https://www.mapion.co.jp/phonebook/M06005/20403/ILSP0061155717_ipclm/|publisher=ONE COMPATH|accessdate=2021-12-04}}. On registry records, it is listed as "𣳾{{Refnest|group="Note"|"券" written with the top half as "氺".}}"{{Cite web|title=Information about Taizan Shrine|url=https://www.houjin-bangou.nta.go.jp/henkorireki-johoto.html?selHouzinNo=4100005010121|website=Corporate Number Publication Site|publisher=National Tax Agency|date=2015-10-05|accessdate=2021-12-04}}. == Deities == According to sources {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1120 }}: * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|lt=Homuda Wake no Mikoto|ja|応神天皇}} * {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}} * {{ill|Susanoo|ja|スサノオ}} * Kodama Hikonomikoto * {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方命}} == History == The shrine was historically known as "Hachiman Shrine," "Ōmiya," or "Ōmiya Sansha," and was highly revered by the {{ill|Zakōji clan|ja|座光寺氏}} {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |pp=1118–1119 }}. After the {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} period, it merged with other shrines in {{ill|Yamabuki Village|ja|山吹村}}, forming its current structure and name {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1120 }}. It is a shrine closely tied to the Yamabuki Kamihira, Yamabuki Naka, and Shimohei districts of Takamori{{Cite web|title=Taizan Shrine|url=https://yamabukiku.main.jp/taizan.html|publisher=Yamabuki Ward of Takamori Town|accessdate=2021-12-07}}. === Chronology === * {{ill|Kan'ei|ja|寛永}} Period: Shrine established (as recorded in the shrine's detailed ledger) {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1118 }}. * {{ill|An'ei|ja|安永}} 1 (1772): Ōmiya Sansha was rebuilt and relocated {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1119 }}. * 1881 (Meiji 14): Kodama Shrine was merged and renamed "Yamabuki Shrine" {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1120 }}. * 1911 (Meiji 44): Suwa Shrine was merged, and the name was changed to the current Taizan Shrine {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1120 }}. * 2009 (Heisei 21), April 3: The shrine's main hall, kagura hall, office, and cultural facilities were destroyed by fire{{Cite news|title=Spring Festival at Taizan Shrine in Takamori Town|url=https://minamishinshu.jp/news/culture/高森町の泰山神社で春祭り.html|newspaper=Minamishinshu Shimbun|publisher=Minamishinshu Shimbunsha|date=2013-04-18|accessdate=2021-12-05}}. * 2012 (Heisei 24), June: The shrine was rebuilt. * 2015 (Heisei 27), October 5: Designated {{ill|corporate number|ja|法人番号}} 4100005010121. == Grounds == According to sources {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |pp=1121–1122 }}: * {{ill|Garden|ja|庭園}}: A garden with a pond is arranged at the front of the precincts. * {{ill|Path|ja|参道}}: The garden path leads alongside the garden. A pair of stone lanterns (dated Man'ei era) stand here. * {{ill|Torii|ja|鳥居}}: Built in 1901 (Meiji 34), the torii is a stone Myōjin-style gate. * Grand Shrine Inscription Monument: Erected on April 10, 1923 (Taishō 12). Located to the right of the above torii, with inscriptions by {{ill|Kameo Mitsutomi|ja|神尾光臣}}. * {{ill|Komainu|ja|狛犬}}: Positioned beyond the torii. Built in 1940 (Shōwa 15) through offerings from parishioners. * Second Torii: Relocated from the merged Kodama Shrine in 1881. * {{ill|Stone lanterns|ja|石灯籠}}: Present on either side of the second torii (date unknown). They are rare four-legged Miyadachi-style lanterns. * Stele of {{ill|Konpira Gongen|ja|金毘羅大権現}} and {{ill|Akiha Gongen|ja|秋葉権現}}: Positioned to the left of the main hall. == Auxiliary Shrines == According to sources. Parentheses indicate deities {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1120 }}: * Shinmei Shrine ({{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}}) * Suwa Shrine (Takeminakata) * Inari Shrine ({{ill|Toyoukehime|ja|トヨウケビメ}}) * Asama Shrine ({{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|コノハナノサクヤビメ}}) * Mountain God Shrine ({{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|オオヤマツミ}}) == Festivals == * Spring Festival: Features flower dances, Okame dances, and {{ill|Shishi-mai|ja|獅子舞}}. == Cultural Properties == * Sword marked "Made by Nagachika of Shinano Province": A sword by the Shinano swordsmith Nagachika (Tenbun–Keichō eras). Length: 2 shaku, 3 sun, 4 bu. Originally belonging to the Zakōji family, it was bought back through parishioners' donations and enshrined {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |pp=1120–1121 }}. == Access == {{Maplink2|frame=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-width=220|frame-height=240|text=Map. Shimodaira Station is in the lower right.}} ;[[Public Transport]] :The nearest station is {{ill|Shimodaira Station|ja|下平駅}} on the {{ill|Iida Line|ja|飯田線}} operated by {{ill|JR Central|ja|東海旅客鉄道}}. It is 701 meters away in a straight line. == Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Annotations === {{Reflist|group="Note"}} === References === {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|title=Upper Volume of the Takamori Town History|author=Takamori Town History Compilation Committee|publisher=Takamori Town History Publication Committee|year=1972|ref=harv}} == See Also == {{Commonscat}} * {{ill|Yamabuki Jin'ya|ja|山吹陣屋}} [[Category:Shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Takamori, Nagano]] [[Category:History of Takamori, Nagano]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Taisan Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:泰山神社]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:Taisan Shrine]][[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:new pages]] [[Category:Taisan Shrine]] [[Category:qq]] [[sn:Taisan Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Taisan Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Taisan Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Taisan Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Taisan Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Taisan Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Taisan Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Taisan Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Taisan Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:泰山神社]] [[Category:New]]

{{Shrine |name = Taizan Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image =[[File:Taizanjinnja2.jpg|300px]]
Taizan Shrine's Torii Gate and Grand Shrine Inscription |location = {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}{{ill|Shimoina District, Nagano|ja|下伊那郡}}{{ill|Takamori, Nagano|ja|高森町 (長野県)}} Oaza Yamabuki Azamaruyama 1048{{Cite web|title=Shrine Introduction Iii Branch|url=https://www.nagano-jinjacho.jp/shibu/hani.htm|publisher=Nagano Shrine Association|accessdate=2021-12-04}} | Latitude = 35 | Lat_min = 34 | Lat_sec = 28.5 | Longitude = 137 | Lon_min = 53 | Lon_sec = 18.4 |ISO = JP-20 |Label_Position = right |Map = Japan Nagano |deity = {{ill|Homuda Wake no Mikoto|ja|応神天皇}}・{{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}}・{{ill|Susanoo|ja|スサノオ}}・Kodama Hikonomikoto・{{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方命}} {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1120 }} |Status = |consecration_year = {{ill|Kan'ei|ja|寛永}} Period {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1118 }} |Main_Hall = |Alias = |Festival = |Ritual = }} [[File:Taizanjinnja1.jpg|thumb|Main Hall]] '''Taizan Shrine''' (泰山神社) is a {{ill|shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}, {{ill|Shimoina District|ja|下伊那郡}}, {{ill|Takamori, Nagano|ja|高森町}}{{Cite web|title=Mapion Directory Taizan Shrine|url=https://www.mapion.co.jp/phonebook/M06005/20403/ILSP0061155717_ipclm/|publisher=ONE COMPATH|accessdate=2021-12-04}}. On registry records, it is listed as "𣳾{{Refnest|group="Note"|"券" written with the top half as "氺".}}"{{Cite web|title=Information about Taizan Shrine|url=https://www.houjin-bangou.nta.go.jp/henkorireki-johoto.html?selHouzinNo=4100005010121|website=Corporate Number Publication Site|publisher=National Tax Agency|date=2015-10-05|accessdate=2021-12-04}}. == Deities == According to sources {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1120 }}: * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|lt=Homuda Wake no Mikoto|ja|応神天皇}} * {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}} * {{ill|Susanoo|ja|スサノオ}} * Kodama Hikonomikoto * {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方命}} == History == The shrine was historically known as "Hachiman Shrine," "Ōmiya," or "Ōmiya Sansha," and was highly revered by the {{ill|Zakōji clan|ja|座光寺氏}} {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |pp=1118–1119 }}. After the {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} period, it merged with other shrines in {{ill|Yamabuki Village|ja|山吹村}}, forming its current structure and name {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1120 }}. It is a shrine closely tied to the Yamabuki Kamihira, Yamabuki Naka, and Shimohei districts of Takamori{{Cite web|title=Taizan Shrine|url=https://yamabukiku.main.jp/taizan.html|publisher=Yamabuki Ward of Takamori Town|accessdate=2021-12-07}}. === Chronology === * {{ill|Kan'ei|ja|寛永}} Period: Shrine established (as recorded in the shrine's detailed ledger) {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1118 }}. * {{ill|An'ei|ja|安永}} 1 (1772): Ōmiya Sansha was rebuilt and relocated {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1119 }}. * 1881 (Meiji 14): Kodama Shrine was merged and renamed "Yamabuki Shrine" {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1120 }}. * 1911 (Meiji 44): Suwa Shrine was merged, and the name was changed to the current Taizan Shrine {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1120 }}. * 2009 (Heisei 21), April 3: The shrine's main hall, kagura hall, office, and cultural facilities were destroyed by fire{{Cite news|title=Spring Festival at Taizan Shrine in Takamori Town|url=https://minamishinshu.jp/news/culture/高森町の泰山神社で春祭り.html|newspaper=Minamishinshu Shimbun|publisher=Minamishinshu Shimbunsha|date=2013-04-18|accessdate=2021-12-05}}. * 2012 (Heisei 24), June: The shrine was rebuilt. * 2015 (Heisei 27), October 5: Designated {{ill|corporate number|ja|法人番号}} 4100005010121. == Grounds == According to sources {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |pp=1121–1122 }}: * {{ill|Garden|ja|庭園}}: A garden with a pond is arranged at the front of the precincts. * {{ill|Path|ja|参道}}: The garden path leads alongside the garden. A pair of stone lanterns (dated Man'ei era) stand here. * {{ill|Torii|ja|鳥居}}: Built in 1901 (Meiji 34), the torii is a stone Myōjin-style gate. * Grand Shrine Inscription Monument: Erected on April 10, 1923 (Taishō 12). Located to the right of the above torii, with inscriptions by {{ill|Kameo Mitsutomi|ja|神尾光臣}}. * {{ill|Komainu|ja|狛犬}}: Positioned beyond the torii. Built in 1940 (Shōwa 15) through offerings from parishioners. * Second Torii: Relocated from the merged Kodama Shrine in 1881. * {{ill|Stone lanterns|ja|石灯籠}}: Present on either side of the second torii (date unknown). They are rare four-legged Miyadachi-style lanterns. * Stele of {{ill|Konpira Gongen|ja|金毘羅大権現}} and {{ill|Akiha Gongen|ja|秋葉権現}}: Positioned to the left of the main hall. == Auxiliary Shrines == According to sources. Parentheses indicate deities {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1120 }}: * Shinmei Shrine ({{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}}) * Suwa Shrine (Takeminakata) * Inari Shrine ({{ill|Toyoukehime|ja|トヨウケビメ}}) * Asama Shrine ({{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|コノハナノサクヤビメ}}) * Mountain God Shrine ({{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|オオヤマツミ}}) == Festivals == * Spring Festival: Features flower dances, Okame dances, and {{ill|Shishi-mai|ja|獅子舞}}. == Cultural Properties == * Sword marked "Made by Nagachika of Shinano Province": A sword by the Shinano swordsmith Nagachika (Tenbun–Keichō eras). Length: 2 shaku, 3 sun, 4 bu. Originally belonging to the Zakōji family, it was bought back through parishioners' donations and enshrined {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |pp=1120–1121 }}. == Access == {{Maplink2|frame=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-width=220|frame-height=240|text=Map. Shimodaira Station is in the lower right.}} ;[[Public Transport]] :The nearest station is {{ill|Shimodaira Station|ja|下平駅}} on the {{ill|Iida Line|ja|飯田線}} operated by {{ill|JR Central|ja|東海旅客鉄道}}. It is 701 meters away in a straight line. == Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Annotations === {{Reflist|group="Note"}} === References === {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|title=Upper Volume of the Takamori Town History|author=Takamori Town History Compilation Committee|publisher=Takamori Town History Publication Committee|year=1972|ref=harv}} == See Also == {{Commonscat}} * {{ill|Yamabuki Jin'ya|ja|山吹陣屋}} [[Category:Shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Takamori, Nagano]] [[Category:History of Takamori, Nagano]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Taisan Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:泰山神社]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:Taisan Shrine]][[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:Taisan Shrine]] [[Category:qq]] [[sn:Taisan Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Taisan Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Taisan Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Taisan Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Taisan Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Taisan Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Taisan Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Taisan Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Taisan Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:泰山神社]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Taisan Shrine]][[Category:qq]]

{{Shrine |name = Taizan Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image =[[File:Taizanjinnja2.jpg|300px]]
Taizan Shrine's Torii Gate and Grand Shrine Inscription |location = {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}{{ill|Shimoina District, Nagano|ja|下伊那郡}}{{ill|Takamori, Nagano|ja|高森町 (長野県)}} Oaza Yamabuki Azamaruyama 1048{{Cite web|title=Shrine Introduction Iii Branch|url=https://www.nagano-jinjacho.jp/shibu/hani.htm|publisher=Nagano Shrine Association|accessdate=2021-12-04}} | Latitude = 35 | Lat_min = 34 | Lat_sec = 28.5 | Longitude = 137 | Lon_min = 53 | Lon_sec = 18.4 |ISO = JP-20 |Label_Position = right |Map = Japan Nagano |deity = {{ill|Homuda Wake no Mikoto|ja|応神天皇}}・{{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}}・{{ill|Susanoo|ja|スサノオ}}・Kodama Hikonomikoto・{{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方命}} {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1120 }} |Status = |consecration_year = {{ill|Kan'ei|ja|寛永}} Period {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1118 }} |Main_Hall = |Alias = |Festival = |Ritual = }} [[File:Taizanjinnja1.jpg|thumb|Main Hall]] '''Taizan Shrine''' (泰山神社) is a {{ill|shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}, {{ill|Shimoina District|ja|下伊那郡}}, {{ill|Takamori, Nagano|ja|高森町}}{{Cite web|title=Mapion Directory Taizan Shrine|url=https://www.mapion.co.jp/phonebook/M06005/20403/ILSP0061155717_ipclm/|publisher=ONE COMPATH|accessdate=2021-12-04}}. On registry records, it is listed as "𣳾{{Refnest|group="Note"|"券" written with the top half as "氺".}}"{{Cite web|title=Information about Taizan Shrine|url=https://www.houjin-bangou.nta.go.jp/henkorireki-johoto.html?selHouzinNo=4100005010121|website=Corporate Number Publication Site|publisher=National Tax Agency|date=2015-10-05|accessdate=2021-12-04}}. == Deities == According to sources {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1120 }}: * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|lt=Homuda Wake no Mikoto|ja|応神天皇}} * {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}} * {{ill|Susanoo|ja|スサノオ}} * Kodama Hikonomikoto * {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方命}} == History == The shrine was historically known as "Hachiman Shrine," "Ōmiya," or "Ōmiya Sansha," and was highly revered by the {{ill|Zakōji clan|ja|座光寺氏}} {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |pp=1118–1119 }}. After the {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} period, it merged with other shrines in {{ill|Yamabuki Village|ja|山吹村}}, forming its current structure and name {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1120 }}. It is a shrine closely tied to the Yamabuki Kamihira, Yamabuki Naka, and Shimohei districts of Takamori{{Cite web|title=Taizan Shrine|url=https://yamabukiku.main.jp/taizan.html|publisher=Yamabuki Ward of Takamori Town|accessdate=2021-12-07}}. === Chronology === * {{ill|Kan'ei|ja|寛永}} Period: Shrine established (as recorded in the shrine's detailed ledger) {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1118 }}. * {{ill|An'ei|ja|安永}} 1 (1772): Ōmiya Sansha was rebuilt and relocated {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1119 }}. * 1881 (Meiji 14): Kodama Shrine was merged and renamed "Yamabuki Shrine" {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1120 }}. * 1911 (Meiji 44): Suwa Shrine was merged, and the name was changed to the current Taizan Shrine {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1120 }}. * 2009 (Heisei 21), April 3: The shrine's main hall, kagura hall, office, and cultural facilities were destroyed by fire{{Cite news|title=Spring Festival at Taizan Shrine in Takamori Town|url=https://minamishinshu.jp/news/culture/高森町の泰山神社で春祭り.html|newspaper=Minamishinshu Shimbun|publisher=Minamishinshu Shimbunsha|date=2013-04-18|accessdate=2021-12-05}}. * 2012 (Heisei 24), June: The shrine was rebuilt. * 2015 (Heisei 27), October 5: Designated {{ill|corporate number|ja|法人番号}} 4100005010121. == Grounds == According to sources {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |pp=1121–1122 }}: * {{ill|Garden|ja|庭園}}: A garden with a pond is arranged at the front of the precincts. * {{ill|Path|ja|参道}}: The garden path leads alongside the garden. A pair of stone lanterns (dated Man'ei era) stand here. * {{ill|Torii|ja|鳥居}}: Built in 1901 (Meiji 34), the torii is a stone Myōjin-style gate. * Grand Shrine Inscription Monument: Erected on April 10, 1923 (Taishō 12). Located to the right of the above torii, with inscriptions by {{ill|Kameo Mitsutomi|ja|神尾光臣}}. * {{ill|Komainu|ja|狛犬}}: Positioned beyond the torii. Built in 1940 (Shōwa 15) through offerings from parishioners. * Second Torii: Relocated from the merged Kodama Shrine in 1881. * {{ill|Stone lanterns|ja|石灯籠}}: Present on either side of the second torii (date unknown). They are rare four-legged Miyadachi-style lanterns. * Stele of {{ill|Konpira Gongen|ja|金毘羅大権現}} and {{ill|Akiha Gongen|ja|秋葉権現}}: Positioned to the left of the main hall. == Auxiliary Shrines == According to sources. Parentheses indicate deities {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1120 }}: * Shinmei Shrine ({{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}}) * Suwa Shrine (Takeminakata) * Inari Shrine ({{ill|Toyoukehime|ja|トヨウケビメ}}) * Asama Shrine ({{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|コノハナノサクヤビメ}}) * Mountain God Shrine ({{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|オオヤマツミ}}) == Festivals == * Spring Festival: Features flower dances, Okame dances, and {{ill|Shishi-mai|ja|獅子舞}}. == Cultural Properties == * Sword marked "Made by Nagachika of Shinano Province": A sword by the Shinano swordsmith Nagachika (Tenbun–Keichō eras). Length: 2 shaku, 3 sun, 4 bu. Originally belonging to the Zakōji family, it was bought back through parishioners' donations and enshrined {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |pp=1120–1121 }}. == Access == {{Maplink2|frame=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-width=220|frame-height=240|text=Map. Shimodaira Station is in the lower right.}} ;[[Public Transport]] :The nearest station is {{ill|Shimodaira Station|ja|下平駅}} on the {{ill|Iida Line|ja|飯田線}} operated by {{ill|JR Central|ja|東海旅客鉄道}}. It is 701 meters away in a straight line. == Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Annotations === {{Reflist|group="Note"}} === References === {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|title=Upper Volume of the Takamori Town History|author=Takamori Town History Compilation Committee|publisher=Takamori Town History Publication Committee|year=1972|ref=harv}} == See Also == {{Commonscat}} * {{ill|Yamabuki Jin'ya|ja|山吹陣屋}} [[Category:Shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Takamori, Nagano]] [[Category:History of Takamori, Nagano]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Taisan Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[ja:泰山神社]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Taisan Shrine]][[Category:Taisan Shrine]] [[Category:11]] [[sn:Taisan Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Taisan Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Taisan Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Taisan Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Taisan Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Taisan Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Taisan Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Taisan Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Taisan Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[ja:泰山神社]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Taisan Shrine]][[Category:11]]

Shimodaira Station is in the lower right.}} ;[[Public Transport]] :The nearest station is {{ill|Shimodaira Station|ja|下平駅}} on the {{ill|Iida Line|ja|飯田線}} operated by {{ill|JR Central|ja|東海旅客鉄道}}. It is 701 meters away in a straight line. == Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Annotations === {{Reflist|group="Note"}} === References === {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|title=Upper Volume of the Takamori Town History|author=Takamori Town History Compilation Committee|publisher=Takamori Town History Publication Committee|year=1972|ref=harv}} == See Also == {{Commonscat}} * {{ill|Yamabuki Jin'ya|ja|山吹陣屋}} [[Category:Shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Takamori, Nagano]] [[Category:History of Takamori, Nagano]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]][[Category: tier 5]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Taisan Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[ja:泰山神社]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Taisan Shrine]][[Category:Taisan Shrine]] [[Category:11]] [[sn:Taisan Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Taisan Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Taisan Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Taisan Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Taisan Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Taisan Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Taisan Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Taisan Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Taisan Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[ja:泰山神社]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Taisan Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Taisan Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]]

{{Shrine |name = Taizan Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image =[[File:Taizanjinnja2.jpg|300px]]
Taizan Shrine's Torii Gate and Grand Shrine Inscription |location = {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}{{ill|Shimoina District, Nagano|ja|下伊那郡}}{{ill|Takamori, Nagano|ja|高森町 (長野県)}} Oaza Yamabuki Azamaruyama 1048{{Cite web|title=Shrine Introduction Iii Branch|url=https://www.nagano-jinjacho.jp/shibu/hani.htm|publisher=Nagano Shrine Association|accessdate=2021-12-04}} | Latitude = 35 | Lat_min = 34 | Lat_sec = 28.5 | Longitude = 137 | Lon_min = 53 | Lon_sec = 18.4 |ISO = JP-20 |Label_Position = right |Map = Japan Nagano |deity = {{ill|Homuda Wake no Mikoto|ja|応神天皇}}・{{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}}・{{ill|Susanoo|ja|スサノオ}}・Kodama Hikonomikoto・{{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方命}} {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1120 }} |Status = |consecration_year = {{ill|Kan'ei|ja|寛永}} Period {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1118 }} |Main_Hall = |Alias = |Festival = |Ritual = }} [[File:Taizanjinnja1.jpg|thumb|Main Hall]] '''Taizan Shrine''' (泰山神社) is a {{ill|shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}, {{ill|Shimoina District|ja|下伊那郡}}, {{ill|Takamori, Nagano|ja|高森町}}{{Cite web|title=Mapion Directory Taizan Shrine|url=https://www.mapion.co.jp/phonebook/M06005/20403/ILSP0061155717_ipclm/|publisher=ONE COMPATH|accessdate=2021-12-04}}. On registry records, it is listed as "𣳾{{Refnest|group="Note"|"券" written with the top half as "氺".}}"{{Cite web|title=Information about Taizan Shrine|url=https://www.houjin-bangou.nta.go.jp/henkorireki-johoto.html?selHouzinNo=4100005010121|website=Corporate Number Publication Site|publisher=National Tax Agency|date=2015-10-05|accessdate=2021-12-04}}. == Deities == According to sources {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1120 }}: * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|lt=Homuda Wake no Mikoto|ja|応神天皇}} * {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}} * {{ill|Susanoo|ja|スサノオ}} * Kodama Hikonomikoto * {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方命}} == History == The shrine was historically known as "Hachiman Shrine," "Ōmiya," or "Ōmiya Sansha," and was highly revered by the {{ill|Zakōji clan|ja|座光寺氏}} {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |pp=1118–1119 }}. After the {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} period, it merged with other shrines in {{ill|Yamabuki Village|ja|山吹村}}, forming its current structure and name {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1120 }}. It is a shrine closely tied to the Yamabuki Kamihira, Yamabuki Naka, and Shimohei districts of Takamori{{Cite web|title=Taizan Shrine|url=https://yamabukiku.main.jp/taizan.html|publisher=Yamabuki Ward of Takamori Town|accessdate=2021-12-07}}. === Chronology === * {{ill|Kan'ei|ja|寛永}} Period: Shrine established (as recorded in the shrine's detailed ledger) {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1118 }}. * {{ill|An'ei|ja|安永}} 1 (1772): Ōmiya Sansha was rebuilt and relocated {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1119 }}. * 1881 (Meiji 14): Kodama Shrine was merged and renamed "Yamabuki Shrine" {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1120 }}. * 1911 (Meiji 44): Suwa Shrine was merged, and the name was changed to the current Taizan Shrine {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1120 }}. * 2009 (Heisei 21), April 3: The shrine's main hall, kagura hall, office, and cultural facilities were destroyed by fire{{Cite news|title=Spring Festival at Taizan Shrine in Takamori Town|url=https://minamishinshu.jp/news/culture/高森町の泰山神社で春祭り.html|newspaper=Minamishinshu Shimbun|publisher=Minamishinshu Shimbunsha|date=2013-04-18|accessdate=2021-12-05}}. * 2012 (Heisei 24), June: The shrine was rebuilt. * 2015 (Heisei 27), October 5: Designated {{ill|corporate number|ja|法人番号}} 4100005010121. == Grounds == According to sources {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |pp=1121–1122 }}: * {{ill|Garden|ja|庭園}}: A garden with a pond is arranged at the front of the precincts. * {{ill|Path|ja|参道}}: The garden path leads alongside the garden. A pair of stone lanterns (dated Man'ei era) stand here. * {{ill|Torii|ja|鳥居}}: Built in 1901 (Meiji 34), the torii is a stone Myōjin-style gate. * Grand Shrine Inscription Monument: Erected on April 10, 1923 (Taishō 12). Located to the right of the above torii, with inscriptions by {{ill|Kameo Mitsutomi|ja|神尾光臣}}. * {{ill|Komainu|ja|狛犬}}: Positioned beyond the torii. Built in 1940 (Shōwa 15) through offerings from parishioners. * Second Torii: Relocated from the merged Kodama Shrine in 1881. * {{ill|Stone lanterns|ja|石灯籠}}: Present on either side of the second torii (date unknown). They are rare four-legged Miyadachi-style lanterns. * Stele of {{ill|Konpira Gongen|ja|金毘羅大権現}} and {{ill|Akiha Gongen|ja|秋葉権現}}: Positioned to the left of the main hall. == Auxiliary Shrines == According to sources. Parentheses indicate deities {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1120 }}: * Shinmei Shrine ({{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}}) * Suwa Shrine (Takeminakata) * Inari Shrine ({{ill|Toyoukehime|ja|トヨウケビメ}}) * Asama Shrine ({{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|コノハナノサクヤビメ}}) * Mountain God Shrine ({{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|オオヤマツミ}}) == Festivals == * Spring Festival: Features flower dances, Okame dances, and {{ill|Shishi-mai|ja|獅子舞}}. == Cultural Properties == * Sword marked "Made by Nagachika of Shinano Province": A sword by the Shinano swordsmith Nagachika (Tenbun–Keichō eras). Length: 2 shaku, 3 sun, 4 bu. Originally belonging to the Zakōji family, it was bought back through parishioners' donations and enshrined {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |pp=1120–1121 }}. == Access == {{Maplink2|frame=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-width=220|frame-height=240|text=Map. Shimodaira Station is in the lower right.}} ;[[Public Transport]] :The nearest station is {{ill|Shimodaira Station|ja|下平駅}} on the {{ill|Iida Line|ja|飯田線}} operated by {{ill|JR Central|ja|東海旅客鉄道}}. It is 701 meters away in a straight line. == Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Annotations === {{Reflist|group="Note"}} === References === {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|title=Upper Volume of the Takamori Town History|author=Takamori Town History Compilation Committee|publisher=Takamori Town History Publication Committee|year=1972|ref=harv}} == See Also == {{Commonscat}} * {{ill|Yamabuki Jin'ya|ja|山吹陣屋}} [[Category:Shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Takamori, Nagano]] [[Category:History of Takamori, Nagano]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]][[Category:11]][[sn:Taisan Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[ja:泰山神社]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Taisan Shrine]][[Category:Taisan Shrine]] [[Category:11]] [[sn:Taisan Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Taisan Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Taisan Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Taisan Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Taisan Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Taisan Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Taisan Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Taisan Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Taisan Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[ja:泰山神社]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Taisan Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Taisan Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]]

revision.comment: Text replacement - "|日本武尊}}" to "|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル}}"
