Built in 1940 (Shōwa 15) through offerings from parishioners. * Second Torii: Relocated from the merged Kodama Shrine in 1881. * {{ill|Stone lanterns|ja|石灯籠}}: Present on either side of the second torii (date unknown). They are rare four-legged Miyadachi-style lanterns. * Stele of {{ill|Konpira Gongen|ja|金毘羅大権現}} and {{ill|Akiha Gongen|ja|秋葉権現}}: Positioned to the left of the main hall. == Auxiliary Shrines == According to sources. Parentheses indicate deities {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1120 }}: * Shinmei Shrine ({{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}}) * Suwa Shrine (Takeminakata) * Inari Shrine ({{ill|Toyoukehime|ja|トヨウケビメ}}) * Asama Shrine ({{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|コノハナノサクヤビメ}}) * Mountain God Shrine ({{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|オオヤマツミ}}) == Festivals == * Spring Festival: Features flower dances, Okame dances, and {{ill|Shishi-mai|ja|獅子舞}}. == Cultural Properties == * Sword marked "Made by Nagachika of Shinano Province": A sword by the Shinano swordsmith Nagachika (Tenbun–Keichō eras). Length: 2 shaku, 3 sun, 4 bu. Originally belonging to the Zakōji family, it was bought back through parishioners' donations and enshrined {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |pp=1120–1121 }}. == Access == {{Maplink2|frame=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-width=220|frame-height=240|text=Map. Shimodaira Station is in the lower right.}} ;[[Public Transport]] :The nearest station is {{ill|Shimodaira Station|ja|下平駅}} on the {{ill|Iida Line|ja|飯田線}} operated by {{ill|JR Central|ja|東海旅客鉄道}}. It is 701 meters away in a straight line. == Notes == === Annotations === {{Reflist|group="Note"}} === References === {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|title=Upper Volume of the Takamori Town History|author=Takamori Town History Compilation Committee|publisher=Takamori Town History Publication Committee|year=1972|ref=harv}} == See Also == {{Commonscat}} * {{ill|Yamabuki Jin'ya|ja|山吹陣屋}} [[Category:Shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Takamori, Nagano]] [[Category:History of Takamori, Nagano]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]][[Category:11]][[sn:Taisan Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[ja:泰山神社]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Taisan Shrine]][[Category:Taisan Shrine]] [[Category:11]] [[sn:Taisan Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Taisan Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Taisan Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Taisan Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Taisan Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Taisan Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Taisan Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Taisan Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Taisan Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[ja:泰山神社]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Taisan Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Taisan Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]]

{{Shrine |name = Taizan Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image =[[File:Taizanjinnja2.jpg|300px]]
Taizan Shrine's Torii Gate and Grand Shrine Inscription |location = {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}{{ill|Shimoina District, Nagano|ja|下伊那郡}}{{ill|Takamori, Nagano|ja|高森町 (長野県)}} Oaza Yamabuki Azamaruyama 1048{{Cite web|title=Shrine Introduction Iii Branch|url=https://www.nagano-jinjacho.jp/shibu/hani.htm|publisher=Nagano Shrine Association|accessdate=2021-12-04}} | Latitude = 35 | Lat_min = 34 | Lat_sec = 28.5 | Longitude = 137 | Lon_min = 53 | Lon_sec = 18.4 |ISO = JP-20 |Label_Position = right |Map = Japan Nagano |deity = {{ill|Homuda Wake no Mikoto|ja|応神天皇}}・{{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル}}・{{ill|Susanoo|ja|スサノオ}}・Kodama Hikonomikoto・{{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方命}} {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1120 }} |Status = |consecration_year = {{ill|Kan'ei|ja|寛永}} Period {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1118 }} |Main_Hall = |Alias = |Festival = |Ritual = }} [[File:Taizanjinnja1.jpg|thumb|Main Hall]] '''Taizan Shrine''' (泰山神社) is a {{ill|shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}, {{ill|Shimoina District|ja|下伊那郡}}, {{ill|Takamori, Nagano|ja|高森町}}{{Cite web|title=Mapion Directory Taizan Shrine|url=https://www.mapion.co.jp/phonebook/M06005/20403/ILSP0061155717_ipclm/|publisher=ONE COMPATH|accessdate=2021-12-04}}. On registry records, it is listed as "𣳾{{Refnest|group="Note"|"券" written with the top half as "氺".}}"{{Cite web|title=Information about Taizan Shrine|url=https://www.houjin-bangou.nta.go.jp/henkorireki-johoto.html?selHouzinNo=4100005010121|website=Corporate Number Publication Site|publisher=National Tax Agency|date=2015-10-05|accessdate=2021-12-04}}. == Deities == According to sources {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1120 }}: * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|lt=Homuda Wake no Mikoto|ja|応神天皇}} * {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル}} * {{ill|Susanoo|ja|スサノオ}} * Kodama Hikonomikoto * {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方命}} == History == The shrine was historically known as "Hachiman Shrine," "Ōmiya," or "Ōmiya Sansha," and was highly revered by the {{ill|Zakōji clan|ja|座光寺氏}} {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |pp=1118–1119 }}. After the {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} period, it merged with other shrines in {{ill|Yamabuki Village|ja|山吹村}}, forming its current structure and name {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1120 }}. It is a shrine closely tied to the Yamabuki Kamihira, Yamabuki Naka, and Shimohei districts of Takamori{{Cite web|title=Taizan Shrine|url=https://yamabukiku.main.jp/taizan.html|publisher=Yamabuki Ward of Takamori Town|accessdate=2021-12-07}}. === Chronology === * {{ill|Kan'ei|ja|寛永}} Period: Shrine established (as recorded in the shrine's detailed ledger) {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1118 }}. * {{ill|An'ei|ja|安永}} 1 (1772): Ōmiya Sansha was rebuilt and relocated {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1119 }}. * 1881 (Meiji 14): Kodama Shrine was merged and renamed "Yamabuki Shrine" {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1120 }}. * 1911 (Meiji 44): Suwa Shrine was merged, and the name was changed to the current Taizan Shrine {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1120 }}. * 2009 (Heisei 21), April 3: The shrine's main hall, kagura hall, office, and cultural facilities were destroyed by fire{{Cite news|title=Spring Festival at Taizan Shrine in Takamori Town|url=https://minamishinshu.jp/news/culture/高森町の泰山神社で春祭り.html|newspaper=Minamishinshu Shimbun|publisher=Minamishinshu Shimbunsha|date=2013-04-18|accessdate=2021-12-05}}. * 2012 (Heisei 24), June: The shrine was rebuilt. * 2015 (Heisei 27), October 5: Designated {{ill|corporate number|ja|法人番号}} 4100005010121. == Grounds == According to sources {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |pp=1121–1122 }}: * {{ill|Garden|ja|庭園}}: A garden with a pond is arranged at the front of the precincts. * {{ill|Path|ja|参道}}: The garden path leads alongside the garden. A pair of stone lanterns (dated Man'ei era) stand here. * {{ill|Torii|ja|鳥居}}: Built in 1901 (Meiji 34), the torii is a stone Myōjin-style gate. * Grand Shrine Inscription Monument: Erected on April 10, 1923 (Taishō 12). Located to the right of the above torii, with inscriptions by {{ill|Kameo Mitsutomi|ja|神尾光臣}}. * {{ill|Komainu|ja|狛犬}}: Positioned beyond the torii. Built in 1940 (Shōwa 15) through offerings from parishioners. * Second Torii: Relocated from the merged Kodama Shrine in 1881. * {{ill|Stone lanterns|ja|石灯籠}}: Present on either side of the second torii (date unknown). They are rare four-legged Miyadachi-style lanterns. * Stele of {{ill|Konpira Gongen|ja|金毘羅大権現}} and {{ill|Akiha Gongen|ja|秋葉権現}}: Positioned to the left of the main hall. == Auxiliary Shrines == According to sources. Parentheses indicate deities {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1120 }}: * Shinmei Shrine ({{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}}) * Suwa Shrine (Takeminakata) * Inari Shrine ({{ill|Toyoukehime|ja|トヨウケビメ}}) * Asama Shrine ({{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|コノハナノサクヤビメ}}) * Mountain God Shrine ({{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|オオヤマツミ}}) == Festivals == * Spring Festival: Features flower dances, Okame dances, and {{ill|Shishi-mai|ja|獅子舞}}. == Cultural Properties == * Sword marked "Made by Nagachika of Shinano Province": A sword by the Shinano swordsmith Nagachika (Tenbun–Keichō eras). Length: 2 shaku, 3 sun, 4 bu. Originally belonging to the Zakōji family, it was bought back through parishioners' donations and enshrined {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |pp=1120–1121 }}. == Access == {{Maplink2|frame=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-width=220|frame-height=240|text=Map. Shimodaira Station is in the lower right.}} ;[[Public Transport]] :The nearest station is {{ill|Shimodaira Station|ja|下平駅}} on the {{ill|Iida Line|ja|飯田線}} operated by {{ill|JR Central|ja|東海旅客鉄道}}. It is 701 meters away in a straight line. == Notes == === Annotations === {{Reflist|group="Note"}} === References === {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|title=Upper Volume of the Takamori Town History|author=Takamori Town History Compilation Committee|publisher=Takamori Town History Publication Committee|year=1972|ref=harv}} == See Also == {{Commonscat}} * {{ill|Yamabuki Jin'ya|ja|山吹陣屋}} [[Category:Shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Takamori, Nagano]] [[Category:History of Takamori, Nagano]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]][[Category: tier 5]][[Category:11]][[sn:Taisan Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[ja:泰山神社]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Taisan Shrine]][[Category:Taisan Shrine]] [[Category:11]] [[sn:Taisan Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Taisan Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Taisan Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Taisan Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Taisan Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Taisan Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Taisan Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Taisan Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Taisan Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[ja:泰山神社]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Taisan Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Taisan Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]]

After the {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} period, it merged with other shrines in {{ill|Yamabuki Village|ja|山吹村}}, forming its current structure and name {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1120 }}. It is a shrine closely tied to the Yamabuki Kamihira, Yamabuki Naka, and Shimohei districts of Takamori{{Cite web|title=Taizan Shrine|url=https://yamabukiku.main.jp/taizan.html|publisher=Yamabuki Ward of Takamori Town|accessdate=2021-12-07}}. === Chronology === * {{ill|Kan'ei|ja|寛永}} Period: Shrine established (as recorded in the shrine's detailed ledger) {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1118 }}. * {{ill|An'ei|ja|安永}} 1 (1772): Ōmiya Sansha was rebuilt and relocated {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1119 }}. * 1881 (Meiji 14): Kodama Shrine was merged and renamed "Yamabuki Shrine" {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1120 }}. * 1911 (Meiji 44): Suwa Shrine was merged, and the name was changed to the current Taizan Shrine {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1120 }}. * 2009 (Heisei 21), April 3: The shrine's main hall, kagura hall, office, and cultural facilities were destroyed by fire{{Cite news|title=Spring Festival at Taizan Shrine in Takamori Town|url=https://minamishinshu.jp/news/culture/高森町の泰山神社で春祭り.html|newspaper=Minamishinshu Shimbun|publisher=Minamishinshu Shimbunsha|date=2013-04-18|accessdate=2021-12-05}}. * 2012 (Heisei 24), June: The shrine was rebuilt. * 2015 (Heisei 27), October 5: Designated {{ill|corporate number|ja|法人番号}} 4100005010121. == Grounds == According to sources {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |pp=1121–1122 }}: * {{ill|Garden|ja|庭園}}: A garden with a pond is arranged at the front of the precincts. * {{ill|Path|ja|参道}}: The garden path leads alongside the garden. A pair of stone lanterns (dated Man'ei era) stand here. * {{ill|Torii|ja|鳥居}}: Built in 1901 (Meiji 34), the torii is a stone Myōjin-style gate. * Grand Shrine Inscription Monument: Erected on April 10, 1923 (Taishō 12). Located to the right of the above torii, with inscriptions by {{ill|Kameo Mitsutomi|ja|神尾光臣}}. * {{ill|Komainu|ja|狛犬}}: Positioned beyond the torii. Built in 1940 (Shōwa 15) through offerings from parishioners. * Second Torii: Relocated from the merged Kodama Shrine in 1881. * {{ill|Stone lanterns|ja|石灯籠}}: Present on either side of the second torii (date unknown). They are rare four-legged Miyadachi-style lanterns. * Stele of {{ill|Konpira Gongen|ja|金毘羅大権現}} and {{ill|Akiha Gongen|ja|秋葉権現}}: Positioned to the left of the main hall. == Auxiliary Shrines == According to sources. Parentheses indicate deities {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1120 }}: * Shinmei Shrine ({{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}}) * Suwa Shrine (Takeminakata) * Inari Shrine ({{ill|Toyoukehime|ja|トヨウケビメ}}) * Asama Shrine ({{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|コノハナノサクヤビメ}}) * Mountain God Shrine ({{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|オオヤマツミ}}) == Festivals == * Spring Festival: Features flower dances, Okame dances, and {{ill|Shishi-mai|ja|獅子舞}}. == Cultural Properties == * Sword marked "Made by Nagachika of Shinano Province": A sword by the Shinano swordsmith Nagachika (Tenbun–Keichō eras). Length: 2 shaku, 3 sun, 4 bu. Originally belonging to the Zakōji family, it was bought back through parishioners' donations and enshrined {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |pp=1120–1121 }}. == Access == {{Maplink2|frame=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-width=220|frame-height=240|text=Map. Shimodaira Station is in the lower right.}} ;[[Public Transport]] :The nearest station is {{ill|Shimodaira Station|ja|下平駅}} on the {{ill|Iida Line|ja|飯田線}} operated by {{ill|JR Central|ja|東海旅客鉄道}}. It is 701 meters away in a straight line. == Notes == === Annotations === {{Reflist|group="Note"}} === References === {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|title=Upper Volume of the Takamori Town History|author=Takamori Town History Compilation Committee|publisher=Takamori Town History Publication Committee|year=1972|ref=harv}}== See Also == {{Commonscat}} * {{ill|Yamabuki Jin'ya|ja|山吹陣屋}} [[Category:Shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Takamori, Nagano]] [[Category:History of Takamori, Nagano]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]][[Category: tier 5]][[Category:11]][[sn:Taisan Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[ja:泰山神社]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Taisan Shrine]][[Category:Taisan Shrine]] [[Category:11]] [[sn:Taisan Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Taisan Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Taisan Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Taisan Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Taisan Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Taisan Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Taisan Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Taisan Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Taisan Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[ja:泰山神社]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Taisan Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Taisan Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:return the see also]]

{{Shrine |name = Taizan Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image =[[File:Taizanjinnja2.jpg|300px]]
Taizan Shrine's Torii Gate and Grand Shrine Inscription |location = {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}{{ill|Shimoina District, Nagano|ja|下伊那郡}}{{ill|Takamori, Nagano|ja|高森町 (長野県)}} Oaza Yamabuki Azamaruyama 1048{{Cite web|title=Shrine Introduction Iii Branch|url=https://www.nagano-jinjacho.jp/shibu/hani.htm|publisher=Nagano Shrine Association|accessdate=2021-12-04}} | Latitude = 35 | Lat_min = 34 | Lat_sec = 28.5 | Longitude = 137 | Lon_min = 53 | Lon_sec = 18.4 |ISO = JP-20 |Label_Position = right |Map = Japan Nagano |deity = {{ill|Homuda Wake no Mikoto|ja|応神天皇}}・{{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル}}・{{ill|Susanoo|ja|スサノオ}}・Kodama Hikonomikoto・{{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方命}} {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1120 }} |Status = |consecration_year = {{ill|Kan'ei|ja|寛永}} Period {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1118 }} |Main_Hall = |Alias = |Festival = |Ritual = }} [[File:Taizanjinnja1.jpg|thumb|Main Hall]] '''Taizan Shrine''' (泰山神社) is a {{ill|shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}, {{ill|Shimoina District|ja|下伊那郡}}, {{ill|Takamori, Nagano|ja|高森町}}{{Cite web|title=Mapion Directory Taizan Shrine|url=https://www.mapion.co.jp/phonebook/M06005/20403/ILSP0061155717_ipclm/|publisher=ONE COMPATH|accessdate=2021-12-04}}. On registry records, it is listed as "𣳾{{Refnest|group="Note"|"券" written with the top half as "氺".}}"{{Cite web|title=Information about Taizan Shrine|url=https://www.houjin-bangou.nta.go.jp/henkorireki-johoto.html?selHouzinNo=4100005010121|website=Corporate Number Publication Site|publisher=National Tax Agency|date=2015-10-05|accessdate=2021-12-04}}. == Deities == According to sources {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1120 }}: * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|lt=Homuda Wake no Mikoto|ja|応神天皇}} * {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル}} * {{ill|Susanoo|ja|スサノオ}} * Kodama Hikonomikoto * {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方命}} == History == The shrine was historically known as "Hachiman Shrine," "Ōmiya," or "Ōmiya Sansha," and was highly revered by the {{ill|Zakōji clan|ja|座光寺氏}} {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |pp=1118–1119 }}. After the {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} period, it merged with other shrines in {{ill|Yamabuki Village|ja|山吹村}}, forming its current structure and name {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1120 }}. It is a shrine closely tied to the Yamabuki Kamihira, Yamabuki Naka, and Shimohei districts of Takamori{{Cite web|title=Taizan Shrine|url=https://yamabukiku.main.jp/taizan.html|publisher=Yamabuki Ward of Takamori Town|accessdate=2021-12-07}}. === Chronology === * {{ill|Kan'ei|ja|寛永}} Period: Shrine established (as recorded in the shrine's detailed ledger) {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1118 }}. * {{ill|An'ei|ja|安永}} 1 (1772): Ōmiya Sansha was rebuilt and relocated {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1119 }}. * 1881 (Meiji 14): Kodama Shrine was merged and renamed "Yamabuki Shrine" {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1120 }}. * 1911 (Meiji 44): Suwa Shrine was merged, and the name was changed to the current Taizan Shrine {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1120 }}. * 2009 (Heisei 21), April 3: The shrine's main hall, kagura hall, office, and cultural facilities were destroyed by fire{{Cite news|title=Spring Festival at Taizan Shrine in Takamori Town|url=https://minamishinshu.jp/news/culture/高森町の泰山神社で春祭り.html|newspaper=Minamishinshu Shimbun|publisher=Minamishinshu Shimbunsha|date=2013-04-18|accessdate=2021-12-05}}. * 2012 (Heisei 24), June: The shrine was rebuilt. * 2015 (Heisei 27), October 5: Designated {{ill|corporate number|ja|法人番号}} 4100005010121. == Grounds == According to sources {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |pp=1121–1122 }}: * {{ill|Garden|ja|庭園}}: A garden with a pond is arranged at the front of the precincts. * {{ill|Path|ja|参道}}: The garden path leads alongside the garden. A pair of stone lanterns (dated Man'ei era) stand here. * {{ill|Torii|ja|鳥居}}: Built in 1901 (Meiji 34), the torii is a stone Myōjin-style gate. * Grand Shrine Inscription Monument: Erected on April 10, 1923 (Taishō 12). Located to the right of the above torii, with inscriptions by {{ill|Kameo Mitsutomi|ja|神尾光臣}}. * {{ill|Komainu|ja|狛犬}}: Positioned beyond the torii. Built in 1940 (Shōwa 15) through offerings from parishioners. * Second Torii: Relocated from the merged Kodama Shrine in 1881. * {{ill|Stone lanterns|ja|石灯籠}}: Present on either side of the second torii (date unknown). They are rare four-legged Miyadachi-style lanterns. * Stele of {{ill|Konpira Gongen|ja|金毘羅大権現}} and {{ill|Akiha Gongen|ja|秋葉権現}}: Positioned to the left of the main hall. == Auxiliary Shrines == According to sources. Parentheses indicate deities {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1120 }}: * Shinmei Shrine ({{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}}) * Suwa Shrine (Takeminakata) * Inari Shrine ({{ill|Toyoukehime|ja|トヨウケビメ}}) * Asama Shrine ({{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|コノハナノサクヤビメ}}) * Mountain God Shrine ({{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|オオヤマツミ|1=Ōyamatsumi}}) == Festivals == * Spring Festival: Features flower dances, Okame dances, and {{ill|Shishi-mai|ja|獅子舞}}. == Cultural Properties == * Sword marked "Made by Nagachika of Shinano Province": A sword by the Shinano swordsmith Nagachika (Tenbun–Keichō eras). Length: 2 shaku, 3 sun, 4 bu. Originally belonging to the Zakōji family, it was bought back through parishioners' donations and enshrined {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |pp=1120–1121 }}. == Access == {{Maplink2|frame=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-width=220|frame-height=240|text=Map. Shimodaira Station is in the lower right.}} ;[[Public Transport]] :The nearest station is {{ill|Shimodaira Station|ja|下平駅}} on the {{ill|Iida Line|ja|飯田線}} operated by {{ill|JR Central|ja|東海旅客鉄道}}. It is 701 meters away in a straight line. == Notes == === Annotations === {{Reflist|group="Note"}} === References === {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|title=Upper Volume of the Takamori Town History|author=Takamori Town History Compilation Committee|publisher=Takamori Town History Publication Committee|year=1972|ref=harv}} == See Also == {{Commonscat}} * {{ill|Yamabuki Jin'ya|ja|山吹陣屋}} [[Category:Shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Takamori, Nagano]] [[Category:History of Takamori, Nagano]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]][[Category: tier 5]][[Category:11]][[sn:Taisan Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[ja:泰山神社]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Taisan Shrine]][[Category:Taisan Shrine]] [[Category:11]] [[sn:Taisan Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Taisan Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Taisan Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Taisan Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Taisan Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Taisan Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Taisan Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Taisan Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Taisan Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[ja:泰山神社]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Taisan Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Taisan Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:return the see also]]

It is 701 meters away in a straight line. == Notes == === Annotations === {{Reflist|group="Note"}} === References === {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|title=Upper Volume of the Takamori Town History|author=Takamori Town History Compilation Committee|publisher=Takamori Town History Publication Committee|year=1972|ref=harv}} == See Also == {{Commonscat}} * {{ill|Yamabuki Jin'ya|ja|山吹陣屋}} [[Category:Shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Takamori, Nagano]] [[Category:History of Takamori, Nagano]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]][[Category:11]][[sn:Taisan Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[ja:泰山神社]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Taisan Shrine]][[Category:Taisan Shrine]] [[Category:11]] [[sn:Taisan Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Taisan Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Taisan Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Taisan Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Taisan Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Taisan Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Taisan Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Taisan Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Taisan Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[ja:泰山神社]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Taisan Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Taisan Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:return the see also]] [[Category:New]]

revision.comment: Bot: Reformatted page - moved categories, interwikis, DEFAULTSORT, and removed duplicates.
revision.text:
{{Shrine |name = Taizan Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image =[[File:Taizanjinnja2.jpg|300px]]
Taizan Shrine's Torii Gate and Grand Shrine Inscription |location = {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}{{ill|Shimoina District, Nagano|ja|下伊那郡}}{{ill|Takamori, Nagano|ja|高森町 (長野県)}} Oaza Yamabuki Azamaruyama 1048{{Cite web|title=Shrine Introduction Iii Branch|url=https://www.nagano-jinjacho.jp/shibu/hani.htm|publisher=Nagano Shrine Association|accessdate=2021-12-04}} | Latitude = 35 | Lat_min = 34 | Lat_sec = 28.5 | Longitude = 137 | Lon_min = 53 | Lon_sec = 18.4 |ISO = JP-20 |Label_Position = right |Map = Japan Nagano |deity = {{ill|Homuda Wake no Mikoto|ja|応神天皇}}・{{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル}}・{{ill|Susanoo|ja|スサノオ}}・Kodama Hikonomikoto・{{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方命}} {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1120 }} |Status = |consecration_year = {{ill|Kan'ei|ja|寛永}} Period {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1118 }} |Main_Hall = |Alias = |Festival = |Ritual = }} [[File:Taizanjinnja1.jpg|thumb|Main Hall]] '''Taizan Shrine''' (泰山神社) is a {{ill|shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}, {{ill|Shimoina District|ja|下伊那郡}}, {{ill|Takamori, Nagano|ja|高森町}}{{Cite web|title=Mapion Directory Taizan Shrine|url=https://www.mapion.co.jp/phonebook/M06005/20403/ILSP0061155717_ipclm/|publisher=ONE COMPATH|accessdate=2021-12-04}}. On registry records, it is listed as "𣳾{{Refnest|group="Note"|"券" written with the top half as "氺".}}"{{Cite web|title=Information about Taizan Shrine|url=https://www.houjin-bangou.nta.go.jp/henkorireki-johoto.html?selHouzinNo=4100005010121|website=Corporate Number Publication Site|publisher=National Tax Agency|date=2015-10-05|accessdate=2021-12-04}}. == Deities == According to sources {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1120 }}: * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|lt=Homuda Wake no Mikoto|ja|応神天皇}} * {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル}} * {{ill|Susanoo|ja|スサノオ}} * Kodama Hikonomikoto * {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方命}} == History == The shrine was historically known as "Hachiman Shrine," "Ōmiya," or "Ōmiya Sansha," and was highly revered by the {{ill|Zakōji clan|ja|座光寺氏}} {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |pp=1118–1119 }}. After the {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} period, it merged with other shrines in {{ill|Yamabuki Village|ja|山吹村}}, forming its current structure and name {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1120 }}. It is a shrine closely tied to the Yamabuki Kamihira, Yamabuki Naka, and Shimohei districts of Takamori{{Cite web|title=Taizan Shrine|url=https://yamabukiku.main.jp/taizan.html|publisher=Yamabuki Ward of Takamori Town|accessdate=2021-12-07}}. === Chronology === * {{ill|Kan'ei|ja|寛永}} Period: Shrine established (as recorded in the shrine's detailed ledger) {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1118 }}. * {{ill|An'ei|ja|安永}} 1 (1772): Ōmiya Sansha was rebuilt and relocated {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1119 }}. * 1881 (Meiji 14): Kodama Shrine was merged and renamed "Yamabuki Shrine" {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1120 }}. * 1911 (Meiji 44): Suwa Shrine was merged, and the name was changed to the current Taizan Shrine {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1120 }}. * 2009 (Heisei 21), April 3: The shrine's main hall, kagura hall, office, and cultural facilities were destroyed by fire{{Cite news|title=Spring Festival at Taizan Shrine in Takamori Town|url=https://minamishinshu.jp/news/culture/高森町の泰山神社で春祭り.html|newspaper=Minamishinshu Shimbun|publisher=Minamishinshu Shimbunsha|date=2013-04-18|accessdate=2021-12-05}}. * 2012 (Heisei 24), June: The shrine was rebuilt. * 2015 (Heisei 27), October 5: Designated {{ill|corporate number|ja|法人番号}} 4100005010121. == Grounds == According to sources {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |pp=1121–1122 }}: * {{ill|Garden|ja|庭園}}: A garden with a pond is arranged at the front of the precincts. * {{ill|Path|ja|参道}}: The garden path leads alongside the garden. A pair of stone lanterns (dated Man'ei era) stand here. * {{ill|Torii|ja|鳥居}}: Built in 1901 (Meiji 34), the torii is a stone Myōjin-style gate. * Grand Shrine Inscription Monument: Erected on April 10, 1923 (Taishō 12). Located to the right of the above torii, with inscriptions by {{ill|Kameo Mitsutomi|ja|神尾光臣}}. * {{ill|Komainu|ja|狛犬}}: Positioned beyond the torii. Built in 1940 (Shōwa 15) through offerings from parishioners. * Second Torii: Relocated from the merged Kodama Shrine in 1881. * {{ill|Stone lanterns|ja|石灯籠}}: Present on either side of the second torii (date unknown). They are rare four-legged Miyadachi-style lanterns. * Stele of {{ill|Konpira Gongen|ja|金毘羅大権現}} and {{ill|Akiha Gongen|ja|秋葉権現}}: Positioned to the left of the main hall. == Auxiliary Shrines == According to sources. Parentheses indicate deities {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1120 }}: * Shinmei Shrine ({{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}}) * Suwa Shrine (Takeminakata) * Inari Shrine ({{ill|Toyoukehime|ja|トヨウケビメ}}) * Asama Shrine ({{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|コノハナノサクヤビメ}}) * Mountain God Shrine ({{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|オオヤマツミ|1=Ōyamatsumi}}) == Festivals == * Spring Festival: Features flower dances, Okame dances, and {{ill|Shishi-mai|ja|獅子舞}}. == Cultural Properties == * Sword marked "Made by Nagachika of Shinano Province": A sword by the Shinano swordsmith Nagachika (Tenbun–Keichō eras). Length: 2 shaku, 3 sun, 4 bu. Originally belonging to the Zakōji family, it was bought back through parishioners' donations and enshrined {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |pp=1120–1121 }}. == Access == {{Maplink2|frame=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-width=220|frame-height=240|text=Map. Shimodaira Station is in the lower right.}} ;[[Public Transport]] :The nearest station is {{ill|Shimodaira Station|ja|下平駅}} on the {{ill|Iida Line|ja|飯田線}} operated by {{ill|JR Central|ja|東海旅客鉄道}}. It is 701 meters away in a straight line. == Notes == === Annotations === {{Reflist|group="Note"}} === References === {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|title=Upper Volume of the Takamori Town History|author=Takamori Town History Compilation Committee|publisher=Takamori Town History Publication Committee|year=1972|ref=harv}} == See Also == {{Commonscat}} * {{ill|Yamabuki Jin'ya|ja|山吹陣屋}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Takamori, Nagano]] [[Category:History of Takamori, Nagano]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Taisan Shrine]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:return the see also]] }} [[sn:Taisan Shrine]] [[ja:泰山神社]]

Length: 2 shaku, 3 sun, 4 bu. Originally belonging to the Zakōji family, it was bought back through parishioners' donations and enshrined {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |pp=1120–1121 }}. == Access == {{Maplink2|frame=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-width=220|frame-height=240|text=Map. Shimodaira Station is in the lower right.}} ;[[Public Transport]] :The nearest station is {{ill|Shimodaira Station|ja|下平駅|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Iida Line|ja|飯田線|12=draft}} operated by {{ill|JR Central|ja|東海旅客鉄道|12=draft}}. It is 701 meters away in a straight line. == Notes == === Annotations === {{Reflist|group="Note"}} === References === {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|title=Upper Volume of the Takamori Town History|author=Takamori Town History Compilation Committee|publisher=Takamori Town History Publication Committee|year=1972|ref=harv}} == See Also == {{Commonscat}} * {{ill|Yamabuki Jin'ya|ja|山吹陣屋|12=draft}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Takamori, Nagano]] [[Category:History of Takamori, Nagano]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Taisan Shrine]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:return the see also]] }} [[sn:Taisan Shrine]] [[ja:泰山神社]]

revision.text:
{{Shrine |name = Taizan Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image =[[File:Taizanjinnja2.jpg|300px]]
Taizan Shrine's Torii Gate and Grand Shrine Inscription |location = {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県|12=draft}}{{ill|Shimoina District, Nagano|ja|下伊那郡|12=draft}}{{ill|Takamori, Nagano|ja|高森町 (長野県)|12=draft}} Oaza Yamabuki Azamaruyama 1048{{Cite web|title=Shrine Introduction Iii Branch|url=https://www.nagano-jinjacho.jp/shibu/hani.htm|publisher=Nagano Shrine Association|accessdate=2021-12-04}} | Latitude = 35 | Lat_min = 34 | Lat_sec = 28.5 | Longitude = 137 | Lon_min = 53 | Lon_sec = 18.4 |ISO = JP-20 |Label_Position = right |Map = Japan Nagano |deity = {{ill|Homuda Wake no Mikoto|ja|応神天皇|12=draft}}・{{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=draft}}・{{ill|Susanoo|ja|スサノオ|12=draft}}・Kodama Hikonomikoto・{{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方命|12=draft}} {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1120 }} |Status = |consecration_year = {{ill|Kan'ei|ja|寛永|12=draft}} Period {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1118 }} |Main_Hall = |Alias = |Festival = |Ritual = }} [[File:Taizanjinnja1.jpg|thumb|Main Hall]] '''Taizan Shrine''' (泰山神社) is a {{ill|shrine|ja|神社|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県|12=draft}}, {{ill|Shimoina District|ja|下伊那郡|12=draft}}, {{ill|Takamori, Nagano|ja|高森町|12=draft}}{{Cite web|title=Mapion Directory Taizan Shrine|url=https://www.mapion.co.jp/phonebook/M06005/20403/ILSP0061155717_ipclm/|publisher=ONE COMPATH|accessdate=2021-12-04}}. On registry records, it is listed as "𣳾{{Refnest|group="Note"|"券" written with the top half as "氺".}}"{{Cite web|title=Information about Taizan Shrine|url=https://www.houjin-bangou.nta.go.jp/henkorireki-johoto.html?selHouzinNo=4100005010121|website=Corporate Number Publication Site|publisher=National Tax Agency|date=2015-10-05|accessdate=2021-12-04}}. == Deities == According to sources {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1120 }}: * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|lt=Homuda Wake no Mikoto|ja|応神天皇|12=draft}} * {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=draft}} * {{ill|Susanoo|ja|スサノオ|12=draft}} * Kodama Hikonomikoto * {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方命|12=draft}} == History == The shrine was historically known as "Hachiman Shrine," "Ōmiya," or "Ōmiya Sansha," and was highly revered by the {{ill|Zakōji clan|ja|座光寺氏|12=draft}} {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |pp=1118–1119 }}. After the {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|12=draft}} period, it merged with other shrines in {{ill|Yamabuki Village|ja|山吹村|12=draft}}, forming its current structure and name {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1120 }}. It is a shrine closely tied to the Yamabuki Kamihira, Yamabuki Naka, and Shimohei districts of Takamori{{Cite web|title=Taizan Shrine|url=https://yamabukiku.main.jp/taizan.html|publisher=Yamabuki Ward of Takamori Town|accessdate=2021-12-07}}. === Chronology === * {{ill|Kan'ei|ja|寛永|12=draft}} Period: Shrine established (as recorded in the shrine's detailed ledger) {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1118 }}. * {{ill|An'ei|ja|安永|12=draft}} 1 (1772): Ōmiya Sansha was rebuilt and relocated {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1119 }}. * 1881 (Meiji 14): Kodama Shrine was merged and renamed "Yamabuki Shrine" {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1120 }}. * 1911 (Meiji 44): Suwa Shrine was merged, and the name was changed to the current Taizan Shrine {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1120 }}. * 2009 (Heisei 21), April 3: The shrine's main hall, kagura hall, office, and cultural facilities were destroyed by fire{{Cite news|title=Spring Festival at Taizan Shrine in Takamori Town|url=https://minamishinshu.jp/news/culture/高森町の泰山神社で春祭り.html|newspaper=Minamishinshu Shimbun|publisher=Minamishinshu Shimbunsha|date=2013-04-18|accessdate=2021-12-05}}. * 2012 (Heisei 24), June: The shrine was rebuilt. * 2015 (Heisei 27), October 5: Designated {{ill|corporate number|ja|法人番号|12=draft}} 4100005010121. == Grounds == According to sources {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |pp=1121–1122 }}: * {{ill|Garden|ja|庭園|12=draft}}: A garden with a pond is arranged at the front of the precincts. * {{ill|Path|ja|参道|12=draft}}: The garden path leads alongside the garden. A pair of stone lanterns (dated Man'ei era) stand here. * {{ill|Torii|ja|鳥居|12=draft}}: Built in 1901 (Meiji 34), the torii is a stone Myōjin-style gate. * Grand Shrine Inscription Monument: Erected on April 10, 1923 (Taishō 12). Located to the right of the above torii, with inscriptions by {{ill|Kameo Mitsutomi|ja|神尾光臣|12=draft}}. * {{ill|Komainu|ja|狛犬|12=draft}}: Positioned beyond the torii. Built in 1940 (Shōwa 15) through offerings from parishioners. * Second Torii: Relocated from the merged Kodama Shrine in 1881. * {{ill|Stone lanterns|ja|石灯籠|12=draft}}: Present on either side of the second torii (date unknown). They are rare four-legged Miyadachi-style lanterns. * Stele of {{ill|Konpira Gongen|ja|金毘羅大権現|12=draft}} and {{ill|Akiha Gongen|ja|秋葉権現|12=draft}}: Positioned to the left of the main hall. == Auxiliary Shrines == According to sources. Parentheses indicate deities {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1120 }}: * Shinmei Shrine ({{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神|12=draft}}) * Suwa Shrine (Takeminakata) * Inari Shrine ({{ill|Toyoukehime|ja|トヨウケビメ|12=draft}}) * Asama Shrine ({{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|コノハナノサクヤビメ|12=draft}}) * Mountain God Shrine ({{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|オオヤマツミ|1=Ōyamatsumi|12=draft}}) == Festivals == * Spring Festival: Features flower dances, Okame dances, and {{ill|Shishi-mai|ja|獅子舞|12=draft}}. == Cultural Properties == * Sword marked "Made by Nagachika of Shinano Province": A sword by the Shinano swordsmith Nagachika (Tenbun–Keichō eras). Length: 2 shaku, 3 sun, 4 bu. Originally belonging to the Zakōji family, it was bought back through parishioners' donations and enshrined {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |pp=1120–1121 }}. == Access == {{Maplink2|frame=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-width=220|frame-height=240|text=Map. Shimodaira Station is in the lower right.}} ;[[Public Transport]] :The nearest station is {{ill|Shimodaira Station|ja|下平駅|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Iida Line|ja|飯田線|12=draft}} operated by {{ill|JR Central|ja|東海旅客鉄道|12=draft}}. It is 701 meters away in a straight line. == Notes == === Annotations === {{Reflist|group="Note"}} === References === {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|title=Upper Volume of the Takamori Town History|author=Takamori Town History Compilation Committee|publisher=Takamori Town History Publication Committee|year=1972|ref=harv}} == See Also == {{Commonscat}} * {{ill|Yamabuki Jin'ya|ja|山吹陣屋|12=draft}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Takamori, Nagano]] [[Category:History of Takamori, Nagano]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Taisan Shrine]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Taisan Shrine]] [[ja:泰山神社]]

Located to the right of the above torii, with inscriptions by {{ill|Kameo Mitsutomi|ja|神尾光臣|12=draft}}. * {{ill|Komainu|ja|狛犬|12=draft}}: Positioned beyond the torii. Built in 1940 (Shōwa 15) through offerings from parishioners. * Second Torii: Relocated from the merged Kodama Shrine in 1881. * {{ill|Stone lanterns|ja|石灯籠|12=draft}}: Present on either side of the second torii (date unknown). They are rare four-legged Miyadachi-style lanterns. * Stele of {{ill|Konpira Gongen|ja|金毘羅大権現|12=draft}} and {{ill|Akiha Gongen|ja|秋葉権現|12=draft}}: Positioned to the left of the main hall. == Auxiliary Shrines == According to sources. Parentheses indicate deities {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1120 }}: * Shinmei Shrine ({{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|12=draft}}) * Suwa Shrine (Takeminakata) * Inari Shrine ({{ill|Toyoukehime|ja|トヨウケビメ|12=draft}}) * Asama Shrine ({{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|コノハナノサクヤビメ|12=draft}}) * Mountain God Shrine ({{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|オオヤマツミ|1=Ōyamatsumi|12=draft}}) == Festivals == * Spring Festival: Features flower dances, Okame dances, and {{ill|Shishi-mai|ja|獅子舞|12=draft}}. == Cultural Properties == * Sword marked "Made by Nagachika of Shinano Province": A sword by the Shinano swordsmith Nagachika (Tenbun–Keichō eras). Length: 2 shaku, 3 sun, 4 bu. Originally belonging to the Zakōji family, it was bought back through parishioners' donations and enshrined {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |pp=1120–1121 }}. == Access == {{Maplink2|frame=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-width=220|frame-height=240|text=Map. Shimodaira Station is in the lower right.}} ;[[Public Transport]] :The nearest station is {{ill|Shimodaira Station|ja|下平駅|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Iida Line|ja|飯田線|12=draft}} operated by {{ill|JR Central|ja|東海旅客鉄道|12=draft}}. It is 701 meters away in a straight line. == Notes == === Annotations === {{Reflist|group="Note"}} === References === {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|title=Upper Volume of the Takamori Town History|author=Takamori Town History Compilation Committee|publisher=Takamori Town History Publication Committee|year=1972|ref=harv}} == See Also == {{Commonscat}} * {{ill|Yamabuki Jin'ya|ja|山吹陣屋|12=draft}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Takamori, Nagano]] [[Category:History of Takamori, Nagano]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Taisan Shrine]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Taisan Shrine]] [[ja:泰山神社]]

{{Shrine |name = Taizan Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image =[[File:Taizanjinnja2.jpg|300px]]
Taizan Shrine's Torii Gate and Grand Shrine Inscription |location = {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県|12=draft|12=draft}}{{ill|Shimoina District, Nagano|ja|下伊那郡|12=draft|12=draft}}{{ill|Takamori, Nagano|ja|高森町 (長野県)|12=draft|12=draft}} Oaza Yamabuki Azamaruyama 1048{{Cite web|title=Shrine Introduction Iii Branch|url=https://www.nagano-jinjacho.jp/shibu/hani.htm|publisher=Nagano Shrine Association|accessdate=2021-12-04}} | Latitude = 35 | Lat_min = 34 | Lat_sec = 28.5 | Longitude = 137 | Lon_min = 53 | Lon_sec = 18.4 |ISO = JP-20 |Label_Position = right |Map = Japan Nagano |deity = {{ill|Homuda Wake no Mikoto|ja|応神天皇|12=draft|12=draft}}・{{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=draft|12=draft}}・{{ill|Susanoo|ja|スサノオ|12=draft|12=draft}}・Kodama Hikonomikoto・{{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方命|12=draft|12=draft}} {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1120 }} |Status = |consecration_year = {{ill|Kan'ei|ja|寛永|12=draft|12=draft}} Period {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1118 }} |Main_Hall = |Alias = |Festival = |Ritual = }} [[File:Taizanjinnja1.jpg|thumb|Main Hall]] '''Taizan Shrine''' (泰山神社) is a {{ill|shrine|ja|神社|12=draft|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Shimoina District|ja|下伊那郡|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Takamori, Nagano|ja|高森町|12=draft|12=draft}}{{Cite web|title=Mapion Directory Taizan Shrine|url=https://www.mapion.co.jp/phonebook/M06005/20403/ILSP0061155717_ipclm/|publisher=ONE COMPATH|accessdate=2021-12-04}}. On registry records, it is listed as "𣳾{{Refnest|group="Note"|"券" written with the top half as "氺".}}"{{Cite web|title=Information about Taizan Shrine|url=https://www.houjin-bangou.nta.go.jp/henkorireki-johoto.html?selHouzinNo=4100005010121|website=Corporate Number Publication Site|publisher=National Tax Agency|date=2015-10-05|accessdate=2021-12-04}}. == Deities == According to sources {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1120 }}: * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|lt=Homuda Wake no Mikoto|ja|応神天皇|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Susanoo|ja|スサノオ|12=draft|12=draft}} * Kodama Hikonomikoto * {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方命|12=draft|12=draft}} == History == The shrine was historically known as "Hachiman Shrine," "Ōmiya," or "Ōmiya Sansha," and was highly revered by the {{ill|Zakōji clan|ja|座光寺氏|12=draft|12=draft}} {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |pp=1118–1119 }}. After the {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|12=draft|12=draft}} period, it merged with other shrines in {{ill|Yamabuki Village|ja|山吹村|12=draft|12=draft}}, forming its current structure and name {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1120 }}. It is a shrine closely tied to the Yamabuki Kamihira, Yamabuki Naka, and Shimohei districts of Takamori{{Cite web|title=Taizan Shrine|url=https://yamabukiku.main.jp/taizan.html|publisher=Yamabuki Ward of Takamori Town|accessdate=2021-12-07}}. === Chronology === * {{ill|Kan'ei|ja|寛永|12=draft|12=draft}} Period: Shrine established (as recorded in the shrine's detailed ledger) {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1118 }}. * {{ill|An'ei|ja|安永|12=draft|12=draft}} 1 (1772): Ōmiya Sansha was rebuilt and relocated {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1119 }}. * 1881 (Meiji 14): Kodama Shrine was merged and renamed "Yamabuki Shrine" {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1120 }}. * 1911 (Meiji 44): Suwa Shrine was merged, and the name was changed to the current Taizan Shrine {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1120 }}. * 2009 (Heisei 21), April 3: The shrine's main hall, kagura hall, office, and cultural facilities were destroyed by fire{{Cite news|title=Spring Festival at Taizan Shrine in Takamori Town|url=https://minamishinshu.jp/news/culture/高森町の泰山神社で春祭り.html|newspaper=Minamishinshu Shimbun|publisher=Minamishinshu Shimbunsha|date=2013-04-18|accessdate=2021-12-05}}. * 2012 (Heisei 24), June: The shrine was rebuilt. * 2015 (Heisei 27), October 5: Designated {{ill|corporate number|ja|法人番号|12=draft|12=draft}} 4100005010121. == Grounds == According to sources {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |pp=1121–1122 }}: * {{ill|Garden|ja|庭園|12=draft|12=draft}}: A garden with a pond is arranged at the front of the precincts. * {{ill|Path|ja|参道|12=draft|12=draft}}: The garden path leads alongside the garden. A pair of stone lanterns (dated Man'ei era) stand here. * {{ill|Torii|ja|鳥居|12=draft|12=draft}}: Built in 1901 (Meiji 34), the torii is a stone Myōjin-style gate. * Grand Shrine Inscription Monument: Erected on April 10, 1923 (Taishō 12). Located to the right of the above torii, with inscriptions by {{ill|Kameo Mitsutomi|ja|神尾光臣|12=draft|12=draft}}. * {{ill|Komainu|ja|狛犬|12=draft|12=draft}}: Positioned beyond the torii. Built in 1940 (Shōwa 15) through offerings from parishioners. * Second Torii: Relocated from the merged Kodama Shrine in 1881. * {{ill|Stone lanterns|ja|石灯籠|12=draft|12=draft}}: Present on either side of the second torii (date unknown). They are rare four-legged Miyadachi-style lanterns. * Stele of {{ill|Konpira Gongen|ja|金毘羅大権現|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Akiha Gongen|ja|秋葉権現|12=draft|12=draft}}: Positioned to the left of the main hall. == Auxiliary Shrines == According to sources. Parentheses indicate deities {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1120 }}: * Shinmei Shrine ({{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|12=draft|12=draft}}) * Suwa Shrine (Takeminakata) * Inari Shrine ({{ill|Toyoukehime|ja|トヨウケビメ|12=draft|12=draft}}) * Asama Shrine ({{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|コノハナノサクヤビメ|12=draft|12=draft}}) * Mountain God Shrine ({{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|オオヤマツミ|1=Ōyamatsumi|12=draft|12=draft}}) == Festivals == * Spring Festival: Features flower dances, Okame dances, and {{ill|Shishi-mai|ja|獅子舞|12=draft|12=draft}}. == Cultural Properties == * Sword marked "Made by Nagachika of Shinano Province": A sword by the Shinano swordsmith Nagachika (Tenbun–Keichō eras). Length: 2 shaku, 3 sun, 4 bu. Originally belonging to the Zakōji family, it was bought back through parishioners' donations and enshrined {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |pp=1120–1121 }}. == Access == {{Maplink2|frame=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-width=220|frame-height=240|text=Map. Shimodaira Station is in the lower right.}} ;[[Public Transport]] :The nearest station is {{ill|Shimodaira Station|ja|下平駅|12=draft|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Iida Line|ja|飯田線|12=draft|12=draft}} operated by {{ill|JR Central|ja|東海旅客鉄道|12=draft|12=draft}}. It is 701 meters away in a straight line. == Notes == === Annotations === {{Reflist|group="Note"}} === References === {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|title=Upper Volume of the Takamori Town History|author=Takamori Town History Compilation Committee|publisher=Takamori Town History Publication Committee|year=1972|ref=harv}} == See Also == {{Commonscat}} * {{ill|Yamabuki Jin'ya|ja|山吹陣屋|12=draft|12=draft}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Takamori, Nagano]] [[Category:History of Takamori, Nagano]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Taisan Shrine]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Taisan Shrine]] [[ja:泰山神社]]

On registry records, it is listed as "𣳾{{Refnest|group="Note"|"券" written with the top half as "氺".}}"{{Cite web|title=Information about Taizan Shrine|url=https://www.houjin-bangou.nta.go.jp/henkorireki-johoto.html?selHouzinNo=4100005010121|website=Corporate Number Publication Site|publisher=National Tax Agency|date=2015-10-05|accessdate=2021-12-04}}. == Deities == According to sources {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1120 }}: * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|lt=Homuda Wake no Mikoto|ja|応神天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Susanoo|ja|スサノオ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * Kodama Hikonomikoto * {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == History == The shrine was historically known as "Hachiman Shrine," "Ōmiya," or "Ōmiya Sansha," and was highly revered by the {{ill|Zakōji clan|ja|座光寺氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |pp=1118–1119 }}. After the {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=draft|qq=draft}} period, it merged with other shrines in {{ill|Yamabuki Village|ja|山吹村|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, forming its current structure and name {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1120 }}. It is a shrine closely tied to the Yamabuki Kamihira, Yamabuki Naka, and Shimohei districts of Takamori{{Cite web|title=Taizan Shrine|url=https://yamabukiku.main.jp/taizan.html|publisher=Yamabuki Ward of Takamori Town|accessdate=2021-12-07}}. === Chronology === * {{ill|Kan'ei|ja|寛永|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Period: Shrine established (as recorded in the shrine's detailed ledger) {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1118 }}. * {{ill|An'ei|ja|安永|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 1 (1772): Ōmiya Sansha was rebuilt and relocated {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1119 }}. * 1881 (Meiji 14): Kodama Shrine was merged and renamed "Yamabuki Shrine" {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1120 }}. * 1911 (Meiji 44): Suwa Shrine was merged, and the name was changed to the current Taizan Shrine {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1120 }}. * 2009 (Heisei 21), April 3: The shrine's main hall, kagura hall, office, and cultural facilities were destroyed by fire{{Cite news|title=Spring Festival at Taizan Shrine in Takamori Town|url=https://minamishinshu.jp/news/culture/高森町の泰山神社で春祭り.html|newspaper=Minamishinshu Shimbun|publisher=Minamishinshu Shimbunsha|date=2013-04-18|accessdate=2021-12-05}}. * 2012 (Heisei 24), June: The shrine was rebuilt. * 2015 (Heisei 27), October 5: Designated {{ill|corporate number|ja|法人番号|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 4100005010121. == Grounds == According to sources {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |pp=1121–1122 }}: * {{ill|Garden|ja|庭園|qq=draft|qq=draft}}: A garden with a pond is arranged at the front of the precincts. * {{ill|Path|ja|参道|qq=draft|qq=draft}}: The garden path leads alongside the garden. A pair of stone lanterns (dated Man'ei era) stand here. * {{ill|Torii|ja|鳥居|qq=draft|qq=draft}}: Built in 1901 (Meiji 34), the torii is a stone Myōjin-style gate. * Grand Shrine Inscription Monument: Erected on April 10, 1923 (Taishō 12). Located to the right of the above torii, with inscriptions by {{ill|Kameo Mitsutomi|ja|神尾光臣|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * {{ill|Komainu|ja|狛犬|qq=draft|qq=draft}}: Positioned beyond the torii. Built in 1940 (Shōwa 15) through offerings from parishioners. * Second Torii: Relocated from the merged Kodama Shrine in 1881. * {{ill|Stone lanterns|ja|石灯籠|qq=draft|qq=draft}}: Present on either side of the second torii (date unknown). They are rare four-legged Miyadachi-style lanterns. * Stele of {{ill|Konpira Gongen|ja|金毘羅大権現|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Akiha Gongen|ja|秋葉権現|qq=draft|qq=draft}}: Positioned to the left of the main hall. == Auxiliary Shrines == According to sources. Parentheses indicate deities {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1120 }}: * Shinmei Shrine ({{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) * Suwa Shrine (Takeminakata) * Inari Shrine ({{ill|Toyoukehime|ja|トヨウケビメ|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) * Asama Shrine ({{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|コノハナノサクヤビメ|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) * Mountain God Shrine ({{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|オオヤマツミ|1=Ōyamatsumi|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) == Festivals == * Spring Festival: Features flower dances, Okame dances, and {{ill|Shishi-mai|ja|獅子舞|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == Cultural Properties == * Sword marked "Made by Nagachika of Shinano Province": A sword by the Shinano swordsmith Nagachika (Tenbun–Keichō eras). Length: 2 shaku, 3 sun, 4 bu. Originally belonging to the Zakōji family, it was bought back through parishioners' donations and enshrined {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |pp=1120–1121 }}. == Access == {{Maplink2|frame=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-width=220|frame-height=240|text=Map. Shimodaira Station is in the lower right.}} ;[[Public Transport]] :The nearest station is {{ill|Shimodaira Station|ja|下平駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}} on the {{ill|Iida Line|ja|飯田線|qq=draft|qq=draft}} operated by {{ill|JR Central|ja|東海旅客鉄道|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. It is 701 meters away in a straight line. == Notes == === Annotations === {{Reflist|group="Note"}} === References === {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|title=Upper Volume of the Takamori Town History|author=Takamori Town History Compilation Committee|publisher=Takamori Town History Publication Committee|year=1972|ref=harv}} == See Also == {{Commonscat}} * {{ill|Yamabuki Jin'ya|ja|山吹陣屋|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Takamori, Nagano]] [[Category:History of Takamori, Nagano]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Taisan Shrine]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Taisan Shrine]] [[ja:泰山神社]]

revision.comment: 16 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:泰山神社 up to rev 98169647
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 泰山神社 |画像 =[[File:Taizanjinnja2.jpg|300px]]
泰山神社の鳥居と大社号碑 |所在地 = [[長野県]][[下伊那郡]][[高森町 (長野県)|高森町]]大字山吹字円山1048{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.nagano-jinjacho.jp/shibu/hani.htm |title=神社紹介 飯伊支部 |publisher=長野県神社庁 |accessdate=2021-12-04 }} | 緯度度 = 35 | 緯度分 = 34 | 緯度秒 = 28.5 | 経度度 = 137 | 経度分 = 53 | 経度秒 = 18.4 |ISO = JP-20 |ラベル位置 = right |地図 = Japan Nagano |祭神 = [[応神天皇|誉田別尊]]・[[ヤマトタケル|日本武尊]]・[[スサノオ|素戔鳴尊]]・[[児玉彦命]]・[[タケミナカタ|建御名方命]]{{Sfn |高森町史編纂委員会 |1972 |p=1120 }} |社格 = |創建 = [[寛永]]年間{{Sfn |高森町史編纂委員会 |1972 |p=1118 }} |本殿 = |別名 = |例祭 = |神事 = }} [[File:Taizanjinnja1.jpg|thumb|社殿]] '''泰山神社'''（たいざんじんじゃ）は、[[長野県]][[下伊那郡]][[高森町 (長野県)|高森町]]にある[[神社]]{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.mapion.co.jp/phonebook/M06005/20403/ILSP0061155717_ipclm/ |title=Mapion電話帳 泰山神社 |publisher=ONE COMPATH |accessdate=2021-12-04 }}。登記上の表記は「𣳾{{Refnest |group="注" |「券」の字の上半分に「氺」。}}山神社」{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.houjin-bangou.nta.go.jp/henkorireki-johoto.html?selHouzinNo=4100005010121 |title=泰山神社の情報 |website=法人番号公表サイト |publisher=国税庁 |date=2015-10-05 |accessdate=2021-12-04 }}。 == 祭神 == 出典による{{Sfn |高森町史編纂委員会 |1972 |p=1120 }}。 * [[応神天皇|誉田別尊]] * [[ヤマトタケル|日本武尊]] * [[スサノオ|素戔鳴尊]] * [[児玉彦命]] * [[タケミナカタ|建御名方命]] == 歴史 == 古くは「八幡社」、「大宮」、「大宮三社」と称し、[[座光寺氏]]の信仰篤い神社であった{{Sfn |高森町史編纂委員会 |1972 |pp=1118 - 1119 }}。[[明治]]以降、[[山吹村]]内にあった神社を合併し、現在の姿・名称となった{{Sfn |高森町史編纂委員会 |1972 |p=1120 }}。高森町山吹上地区・山吹中地区・下平地区ゆかりの神社である{{Cite web|和書|url=https://yamabukiku.main.jp/taizan.html |title=泰山神社 |publisher=高森町山吹区 |accessdate=2021-12-07 }}。 === 沿革 === * [[寛永]]年間 - 創立（当社所有の神社明細帳による）{{Sfn |高森町史編纂委員会 |1972 |p=1118 }}。 * [[安永 (元号)|安永]]元年（[[1772年]]）12月 - 大宮三社造営、遷宮{{Sfn |高森町史編纂委員会 |1972 |p=1119 }}。 * [[1881年]]（明治14年） - 児玉社（山吹村山部位置鎮座）を合併し「山吹神社」に改名{{Sfn |高森町史編纂委員会 |1972 |p=1120 }}。 * [[1911年]]（明治44年） - 諏訪神社（田沢部落鎮座）を合併し現社名に改名{{Sfn |高森町史編纂委員会 |1972 |p=1120 }}。 * [[2009年]]（平成21年）4月3日 - [[火災]]により本殿・神楽殿・社務所・伝統芸能保存施設など全焼{{Cite news |url=https://minamishinshu.jp/news/culture/高森町の泰山神社で春祭り.html |title=高森町の泰山神社で春祭り |newspaper=[[南信州新聞]] |publisher=南信州新聞社 |date=2013-04-18 |accessdate=2021-12-05 }}。 * [[2012年]]（平成24年）6月 - 神社再建。 * [[2015年]]（[[平成]]27年）10月5日 - {{法人番号|4100005010121}}指定。 == 境内 == 出典による{{Sfn |高森町史編纂委員会 |1972 |pp=1121 - 1122 }}。 * [[庭園]] - 境内前に[[池]]のある庭園が整備されている。 * [[参道]] - 上記庭園脇を通る。1対の石灯籠（万永元年）が建つ。 * [[鳥居]] - 1901年（明治34年）6月建立。石造の明神鳥居。 * 大社号碑 - 1923年（大正12年）4月10日建立。上記鳥居の向かって右側に建つ。書は[[神尾光臣]]による。 * [[狛犬]] - 上記鳥居の奥側に位置する。1940年（昭和15年）9月、氏子らの奉献により建立。 * 二の鳥居 - 1881年に合併した児玉社より移建。 * [[石灯籠]] - 二の鳥居左右にある（年代不明）。宮立形灯籠としては異形にも4脚の地輪を持つ。 * [[金毘羅権現|金毘羅大権現]]・[[秋葉権現|秋葉山大権現]]の石碑 - 本殿向かって左側に建つ。 == 境内社 == 出典による。かっこ内は祭神{{Sfn |高森町史編纂委員会 |1972 |p=1120 }}。 * 神明社（[[天照大神]]） * 諏訪社（建御名方命） * 稲荷社（[[トヨウケビメ|登由宇気神]]） * 浅間社（[[コノハナノサクヤビメ|木花咲姫命]]） * 山の神社（[[オオヤマツミ|大山祇命]]） == 祭事・年中行事 == * 春祭り - はな踊り・おかめ踊り・[[獅子舞]]で賑わう。 == 文化財 == * [[刀]] 銘「信州住長治造之」 - 当社宝蔵の刀。[[信濃国]]の[[刀工]]・長治（[[天正]] - [[慶長]]年間）の作。長さ2[[尺]]3[[寸]]4[[分]]。座光寺家から骨董商に渡ったものを氏子惣代斡旋のもと帰還兵から寄付を募り、買い戻して当社に奉納した{{Sfn |高森町史編纂委員会 |1972 |pp=1120 - 1121 }}。 == 交通アクセス == {{Maplink2 |frame=yes |type=point |zoom=14 |frame-width=220 |frame-height=240 |text=地図。右下は下平駅。}} ;[[公共交通機関]] :[[東海旅客鉄道|JR]][[飯田線]]・[[下平駅]]が最寄り。直線距離で701メートル。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} === 注釈 === {{Reflist|group="注"}} === 出典 === {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * {{Cite book |和書 |author=高森町史編纂委員会 |title=高森町史 上巻後篇 |publisher=高森町史刊行会 |year=1972 |ref=harv }} == 関連項目 == {{Commonscat}} * [[山吹陣屋]] {{デフォルトソート:たいさんしんしや}} [[Category:長野県の神社]] [[Category:長野県高森町の建築物]] [[Category:長野県高森町の歴史]]

当社宝蔵の刀。[[信濃国]]の[[刀工]]・長治（[[天正]] - [[慶長]]年間）の作。長さ2[[尺]]3[[寸]]4[[分]]。座光寺家から骨董商に渡ったものを氏子惣代斡旋のもと帰還兵から寄付を募り、買い戻して当社に奉納した{{Sfn |高森町史編纂委員会 |1972 |pp=1120 - 1121 }}。 == 交通アクセス == {{Maplink2 |frame=yes |type=point |zoom=14 |frame-width=220 |frame-height=240 |text=地図。右下は下平駅。}} ;[[公共交通機関]] :[[東海旅客鉄道|JR]][[飯田線]]・[[下平駅]]が最寄り。直線距離で701メートル。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} === 注釈 === {{Reflist|group="注"}} === 出典 === {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * {{Cite book |和書 |author=高森町史編纂委員会 |title=高森町史 上巻後篇 |publisher=高森町史刊行会 |year=1972 |ref=harv }} == 関連項目 == {{Commonscat}} * [[山吹陣屋]] {{デフォルトソート:たいさんしんしや}} [[Category:長野県の神社]] [[Category:長野県高森町の建築物]] [[Category:長野県高森町の歴史]]

{{Shrine |name = Taizan Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image =[[File:Taizanjinnja2.jpg|300px]]
Taizan Shrine's Torii Gate and Grand Shrine Inscription |location = {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}{{ill|Shimoina District, Nagano|ja|下伊那郡|qq=draft|qq=draft}}{{ill|Takamori, Nagano|ja|高森町 (長野県)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Oaza Yamabuki Azamaruyama 1048{{Cite web|title=Shrine Introduction Iii Branch|url=https://www.nagano-jinjacho.jp/shibu/hani.htm|publisher=Nagano Shrine Association|accessdate=2021-12-04}} | Latitude = 35 | Lat_min = 34 | Lat_sec = 28.5 | Longitude = 137 | Lon_min = 53 | Lon_sec = 18.4 |ISO = JP-20 |Label_Position = right |Map = Japan Nagano |deity = {{ill|Homuda Wake no Mikoto|ja|応神天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}}・{{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|qq=draft}}・{{ill|Susanoo|ja|スサノオ|qq=draft|qq=draft}}・Kodama Hikonomikoto・{{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1120 }} |Status = |consecration_year = {{ill|Kan'ei|ja|寛永|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Period {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1118 }} |Main_Hall = |Alias = |Festival = |Ritual = }} [[File:Taizanjinnja1.jpg|thumb|Main Hall]] '''Taizan Shrine''' (泰山神社) is a {{ill|shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Shimoina District|ja|下伊那郡|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Takamori, Nagano|ja|高森町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}{{Cite web|title=Mapion Directory Taizan Shrine|url=https://www.mapion.co.jp/phonebook/M06005/20403/ILSP0061155717_ipclm/|publisher=ONE COMPATH|accessdate=2021-12-04}}. On registry records, it is listed as "𣳾{{Refnest|group="Note"|"券" written with the top half as "氺".}}"{{Cite web|title=Information about Taizan Shrine|url=https://www.houjin-bangou.nta.go.jp/henkorireki-johoto.html?selHouzinNo=4100005010121|website=Corporate Number Publication Site|publisher=National Tax Agency|date=2015-10-05|accessdate=2021-12-04}}. == Deities == According to sources {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1120 }}: * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|lt=Homuda Wake no Mikoto|ja|応神天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Susanoo|ja|スサノオ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * Kodama Hikonomikoto * {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == History == The shrine was historically known as "Hachiman Shrine," "Ōmiya," or "Ōmiya Sansha," and was highly revered by the {{ill|Zakōji clan|ja|座光寺氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |pp=1118–1119 }}. After the {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=draft|qq=draft}} period, it merged with other shrines in {{ill|Yamabuki Village|ja|山吹村|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, forming its current structure and name {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1120 }}. It is a shrine closely tied to the Yamabuki Kamihira, Yamabuki Naka, and Shimohei districts of Takamori{{Cite web|title=Taizan Shrine|url=https://yamabukiku.main.jp/taizan.html|publisher=Yamabuki Ward of Takamori Town|accessdate=2021-12-07}}. === Chronology === * {{ill|Kan'ei|ja|寛永|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Period: Shrine established (as recorded in the shrine's detailed ledger) {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1118 }}. * {{ill|An'ei|ja|安永|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 1 (1772): Ōmiya Sansha was rebuilt and relocated {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1119 }}. * 1881 (Meiji 14): Kodama Shrine was merged and renamed "Yamabuki Shrine" {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1120 }}. * 1911 (Meiji 44): Suwa Shrine was merged, and the name was changed to the current Taizan Shrine {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1120 }}. * 2009 (Heisei 21), April 3: The shrine's main hall, kagura hall, office, and cultural facilities were destroyed by fire{{Cite news|title=Spring Festival at Taizan Shrine in Takamori Town|url=https://minamishinshu.jp/news/culture/高森町の泰山神社で春祭り.html|newspaper=Minamishinshu Shimbun|publisher=Minamishinshu Shimbunsha|date=2013-04-18|accessdate=2021-12-05}}. * 2012 (Heisei 24), June: The shrine was rebuilt. * 2015 (Heisei 27), October 5: Designated {{ill|corporate number|ja|法人番号|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 4100005010121. == Grounds == According to sources {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |pp=1121–1122 }}: * {{ill|Garden|ja|庭園|qq=draft|qq=draft}}: A garden with a pond is arranged at the front of the precincts. * {{ill|Path|ja|参道|qq=draft|qq=draft}}: The garden path leads alongside the garden. A pair of stone lanterns (dated Man'ei era) stand here. * {{ill|Torii|ja|鳥居|qq=draft|qq=draft}}: Built in 1901 (Meiji 34), the torii is a stone Myōjin-style gate. * Grand Shrine Inscription Monument: Erected on April 10, 1923 (Taishō 12). Located to the right of the above torii, with inscriptions by {{ill|Kameo Mitsutomi|ja|神尾光臣|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * {{ill|Komainu|ja|狛犬|qq=draft|qq=draft}}: Positioned beyond the torii. Built in 1940 (Shōwa 15) through offerings from parishioners. * Second Torii: Relocated from the merged Kodama Shrine in 1881. * {{ill|Stone lanterns|ja|石灯籠|qq=draft|qq=draft}}: Present on either side of the second torii (date unknown). They are rare four-legged Miyadachi-style lanterns. * Stele of {{ill|Konpira Gongen|ja|金毘羅大権現|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Akiha Gongen|ja|秋葉権現|qq=draft|qq=draft}}: Positioned to the left of the main hall. == Auxiliary Shrines == According to sources. Parentheses indicate deities {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1120 }}: * Shinmei Shrine ({{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) * Suwa Shrine (Takeminakata) * Inari Shrine ({{ill|Toyoukehime|ja|トヨウケビメ|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) * Asama Shrine ({{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|コノハナノサクヤビメ|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) * Mountain God Shrine ({{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|オオヤマツミ|1=Ōyamatsumi|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) == Festivals == * Spring Festival: Features flower dances, Okame dances, and {{ill|Shishi-mai|ja|獅子舞|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == Cultural Properties == * Sword marked "Made by Nagachika of Shinano Province": A sword by the Shinano swordsmith Nagachika (Tenbun–Keichō eras). Length: 2 shaku, 3 sun, 4 bu. Originally belonging to the Zakōji family, it was bought back through parishioners' donations and enshrined {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |pp=1120–1121 }}. == Access == {{Maplink2|frame=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-width=220|frame-height=240|text=Map. Shimodaira Station is in the lower right.}} ;[[Public Transport]] :The nearest station is {{ill|Shimodaira Station|ja|下平駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}} on the {{ill|Iida Line|ja|飯田線|qq=draft|qq=draft}} operated by {{ill|JR Central|ja|東海旅客鉄道|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. It is 701 meters away in a straight line. == Notes == === Annotations === {{Reflist|group="Note"}} === References === {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|title=Upper Volume of the Takamori Town History|author=Takamori Town History Compilation Committee|publisher=Takamori Town History Publication Committee|year=1972|ref=harv}} == See Also == {{Commonscat}} * {{ill|Yamabuki Jin'ya|ja|山吹陣屋|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Takamori, Nagano]] [[Category:History of Takamori, Nagano]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Taisan Shrine]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Taisan Shrine]] [[ja:泰山神社]] {{translated page|ja|泰山神社|version=98169647|comment=Imported full ja history}}

It is a shrine closely tied to the Yamabuki Kamihira, Yamabuki Naka, and Shimohei districts of Takamori{{Cite web|title=Taizan Shrine|url=https://yamabukiku.main.jp/taizan.html|publisher=Yamabuki Ward of Takamori Town|accessdate=2021-12-07}}. === Chronology === * {{ill|Kan'ei|ja|寛永|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Period: Shrine established (as recorded in the shrine's detailed ledger) {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1118 }}. * {{ill|An'ei|ja|安永|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 1 (1772): Ōmiya Sansha was rebuilt and relocated {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1119 }}. * 1881 (Meiji 14): Kodama Shrine was merged and renamed "Yamabuki Shrine" {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1120 }}. * 1911 (Meiji 44): Suwa Shrine was merged, and the name was changed to the current Taizan Shrine {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1120 }}. * 2009 (Heisei 21), April 3: The shrine's main hall, kagura hall, office, and cultural facilities were destroyed by fire{{Cite news|title=Spring Festival at Taizan Shrine in Takamori Town|url=https://minamishinshu.jp/news/culture/高森町の泰山神社で春祭り.html|newspaper=Minamishinshu Shimbun|publisher=Minamishinshu Shimbunsha|date=2013-04-18|accessdate=2021-12-05}}. * 2012 (Heisei 24), June: The shrine was rebuilt. * 2015 (Heisei 27), October 5: Designated {{ill|corporate number|ja|法人番号|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 4100005010121. == Grounds == According to sources {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |pp=1121–1122 }}: * {{ill|Garden|ja|庭園|qq=draft|qq=draft}}: A garden with a pond is arranged at the front of the precincts. * {{ill|Path|ja|参道|qq=draft|qq=draft}}: The garden path leads alongside the garden. A pair of stone lanterns (dated Man'ei era) stand here. * {{ill|Torii|ja|鳥居|qq=draft|qq=draft}}: Built in 1901 (Meiji 34), the torii is a stone Myōjin-style gate. * Grand Shrine Inscription Monument: Erected on April 10, 1923 (Taishō 12). Located to the right of the above torii, with inscriptions by {{ill|Kameo Mitsutomi|ja|神尾光臣|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * {{ill|Komainu|ja|狛犬|qq=draft|qq=draft}}: Positioned beyond the torii. Built in 1940 (Shōwa 15) through offerings from parishioners. * Second Torii: Relocated from the merged Kodama Shrine in 1881. * {{ill|Stone lanterns|ja|石灯籠|qq=draft|qq=draft}}: Present on either side of the second torii (date unknown). They are rare four-legged Miyadachi-style lanterns. * Stele of {{ill|Konpira Gongen|ja|金毘羅大権現|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Akiha Gongen|ja|秋葉権現|qq=draft|qq=draft}}: Positioned to the left of the main hall. == Auxiliary Shrines == According to sources. Parentheses indicate deities {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1120 }}: * Shinmei Shrine ({{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) * Suwa Shrine (Takeminakata) * Inari Shrine ({{ill|Toyoukehime|ja|トヨウケビメ|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) * Asama Shrine ({{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|コノハナノサクヤビメ|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) * Mountain God Shrine ({{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|オオヤマツミ|1=Ōyamatsumi|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) == Festivals == * Spring Festival: Features flower dances, Okame dances, and {{ill|Shishi-mai|ja|獅子舞|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == Cultural Properties == * Sword marked "Made by Nagachika of Shinano Province": A sword by the Shinano swordsmith Nagachika (Tenbun–Keichō eras). Length: 2 shaku, 3 sun, 4 bu. Originally belonging to the Zakōji family, it was bought back through parishioners' donations and enshrined {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |pp=1120–1121 }}. == Access == {{Maplink2|frame=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-width=220|frame-height=240|text=Map. Shimodaira Station is in the lower right.}} ;[[Public Transport]] :The nearest station is {{ill|Shimodaira Station|ja|下平駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}} on the {{ill|Iida Line|ja|飯田線|qq=draft|qq=draft}} operated by {{ill|JR Central|ja|東海旅客鉄道|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. It is 701 meters away in a straight line. == Notes == === Annotations === {{Reflist|group="Note"}} === References === {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|title=Upper Volume of the Takamori Town History|author=Takamori Town History Compilation Committee|publisher=Takamori Town History Publication Committee|year=1972|ref=harv}} == See Also == {{Commonscat}} * {{ill|Yamabuki Jin'ya|ja|山吹陣屋|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{translated page|ja|泰山神社|version=98169647|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Takamori, Nagano]] [[Category:History of Takamori, Nagano]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Taisan Shrine]] }} [[sn:Taisan Shrine]] [[ja:泰山神社]]

Taizan Shrine's Torii Gate and Grand Shrine Inscription |location = {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}{{ill|Shimoina District, Nagano|ja|下伊那郡|qq=draft|qq=draft}}{{ill|Takamori, Nagano|ja|高森町 (長野県)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Oaza Yamabuki Azamaruyama 1048{{Cite web|title=Shrine Introduction Iii Branch|url=https://www.nagano-jinjacho.jp/shibu/hani.htm|publisher=Nagano Shrine Association|accessdate=2021-12-04}} | Latitude = 35 | Lat_min = 34 | Lat_sec = 28.5 | Longitude = 137 | Lon_min = 53 | Lon_sec = 18.4 |ISO = JP-20 |Label_Position = right |Map = Japan Nagano |deity = {{ill|Homuda Wake no Mikoto|ja|応神天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}}・{{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|qq=draft}}・{{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|スサノオ|qq=draft|qq=draft}}・Kodama Hikonomikoto・{{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1120 }} |Status = |consecration_year = {{ill|Kan'ei|ja|寛永|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Period {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1118 }} |Main_Hall = |Alias = |Festival = |Ritual = }} [[File:Taizanjinnja1.jpg|thumb|Main Hall]] '''Taizan Shrine''' (泰山神社) is a {{ill|shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Shimoina District|ja|下伊那郡|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Takamori, Nagano|ja|高森町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}{{Cite web|title=Mapion Directory Taizan Shrine|url=https://www.mapion.co.jp/phonebook/M06005/20403/ILSP0061155717_ipclm/|publisher=ONE COMPATH|accessdate=2021-12-04}}. On registry records, it is listed as "𣳾{{Refnest|group="Note"|"券" written with the top half as "氺".}}"{{Cite web|title=Information about Taizan Shrine|url=https://www.houjin-bangou.nta.go.jp/henkorireki-johoto.html?selHouzinNo=4100005010121|website=Corporate Number Publication Site|publisher=National Tax Agency|date=2015-10-05|accessdate=2021-12-04}}. == Deities == According to sources {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1120 }}: * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|lt=Homuda Wake no Mikoto|ja|応神天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|スサノオ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * Kodama Hikonomikoto * {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == History == The shrine was historically known as "Hachiman Shrine," "Ōmiya," or "Ōmiya Sansha," and was highly revered by the {{ill|Zakōji clan|ja|座光寺氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |pp=1118–1119 }}. After the {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=draft|qq=draft}} period, it merged with other shrines in {{ill|Yamabuki Village|ja|山吹村|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, forming its current structure and name {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1120 }}. It is a shrine closely tied to the Yamabuki Kamihira, Yamabuki Naka, and Shimohei districts of Takamori{{Cite web|title=Taizan Shrine|url=https://yamabukiku.main.jp/taizan.html|publisher=Yamabuki Ward of Takamori Town|accessdate=2021-12-07}}. === Chronology === * {{ill|Kan'ei|ja|寛永|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Period: Shrine established (as recorded in the shrine's detailed ledger) {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1118 }}. * {{ill|An'ei|ja|安永|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 1 (1772): Ōmiya Sansha was rebuilt and relocated {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1119 }}. * 1881 (Meiji 14): Kodama Shrine was merged and renamed "Yamabuki Shrine" {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1120 }}. * 1911 (Meiji 44): Suwa Shrine was merged, and the name was changed to the current Taizan Shrine {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1120 }}. * 2009 (Heisei 21), April 3: The shrine's main hall, kagura hall, office, and cultural facilities were destroyed by fire{{Cite news|title=Spring Festival at Taizan Shrine in Takamori Town|url=https://minamishinshu.jp/news/culture/高森町の泰山神社で春祭り.html|newspaper=Minamishinshu Shimbun|publisher=Minamishinshu Shimbunsha|date=2013-04-18|accessdate=2021-12-05}}. * 2012 (Heisei 24), June: The shrine was rebuilt. * 2015 (Heisei 27), October 5: Designated {{ill|corporate number|ja|法人番号|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 4100005010121. == Grounds == According to sources {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |pp=1121–1122 }}: * {{ill|Garden|ja|庭園|qq=draft|qq=draft}}: A garden with a pond is arranged at the front of the precincts. * {{ill|Path|ja|参道|qq=draft|qq=draft}}: The garden path leads alongside the garden.
