Takeru|qq=}}・{{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|スサノオ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}}・Kodama Hikonomikoto・{{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeminakata|qq=}} {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1120 }} |Status = |consecration_year = {{ill|Kan'ei|ja|寛永|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kan'ei|qq=}} Period {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1118 }} |Main_Hall = |Alias = |Festival = |Ritual = }} [[File:Taizanjinnja1.jpg|thumb|Main Hall]] '''Taizan Shrine''' (泰山神社) is a {{ill|shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shrine|qq=}} located in {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagano Prefecture|qq=}}, {{ill|Shimoina District|ja|下伊那郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimoina District|qq=}}, {{ill|Takamori, Nagano|ja|高森町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamori, Nagano|qq=}}{{Cite web|title=Mapion Directory Taizan Shrine|url=https://www.mapion.co.jp/phonebook/M06005/20403/ILSP0061155717_ipclm/|publisher=ONE COMPATH|accessdate=2021-12-04}}. On registry records, it is listed as "𣳾{{Refnest|group="Note"|"券" written with the top half as "氺".}}"{{Cite web|title=Information about Taizan Shrine|url=https://www.houjin-bangou.nta.go.jp/henkorireki-johoto.html?selHouzinNo=4100005010121|website=Corporate Number Publication Site|publisher=National Tax Agency|date=2015-10-05|accessdate=2021-12-04}}. == Deities == According to sources {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1120 }}: * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|lt=Homuda Wake no Mikoto|ja|応神天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Ōjin|qq=}} * {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=}} * {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|スサノオ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}} * Kodama Hikonomikoto * {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeminakata|qq=}} == History == The shrine was historically known as "Hachiman Shrine," "Ōmiya," or "Ōmiya Sansha," and was highly revered by the {{ill|Zakōji clan|ja|座光寺氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zakōji clan|qq=}} {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |pp=1118–1119 }}. After the {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji era|qq=}} period, it merged with other shrines in {{ill|Yamabuki Village|ja|山吹村|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamabuki Village|qq=}}, forming its current structure and name {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1120 }}. It is a shrine closely tied to the Yamabuki Kamihira, Yamabuki Naka, and Shimohei districts of Takamori{{Cite web|title=Taizan Shrine|url=https://yamabukiku.main.jp/taizan.html|publisher=Yamabuki Ward of Takamori Town|accessdate=2021-12-07}}. === Chronology === * {{ill|Kan'ei|ja|寛永|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kan'ei|qq=}} Period: Shrine established (as recorded in the shrine's detailed ledger) {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1118 }}. * {{ill|An'ei|ja|安永|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/An'ei|qq=}} 1 (1772): Ōmiya Sansha was rebuilt and relocated {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1119 }}. * 1881 (Meiji 14): Kodama Shrine was merged and renamed "Yamabuki Shrine" {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1120 }}. * 1911 (Meiji 44): Suwa Shrine was merged, and the name was changed to the current Taizan Shrine {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1120 }}. * 2009 (Heisei 21), April 3: The shrine's main hall, kagura hall, office, and cultural facilities were destroyed by fire{{Cite news|title=Spring Festival at Taizan Shrine in Takamori Town|url=https://minamishinshu.jp/news/culture/高森町の泰山神社で春祭り.html|newspaper=Minamishinshu Shimbun|publisher=Minamishinshu Shimbunsha|date=2013-04-18|accessdate=2021-12-05}}. * 2012 (Heisei 24), June: The shrine was rebuilt. * 2015 (Heisei 27), October 5: Designated {{ill|corporate number|ja|法人番号|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/corporate number|qq=}} 4100005010121. == Grounds == According to sources {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |pp=1121–1122 }}: * {{ill|Garden|ja|庭園|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Garden|qq=}}: A garden with a pond is arranged at the front of the precincts. * {{ill|Path|ja|参道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Path|qq=}}: The garden path leads alongside the garden. A pair of stone lanterns (dated Man'ei era) stand here. * {{ill|Torii|ja|鳥居|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Torii|qq=}}: Built in 1901 (Meiji 34), the torii is a stone Myōjin-style gate. * Grand Shrine Inscription Monument: Erected on April 10, 1923 (Taishō 12). Located to the right of the above torii, with inscriptions by {{ill|Kameo Mitsutomi|ja|神尾光臣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kameo Mitsutomi|qq=}}. * {{ill|Komainu|ja|狛犬|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komainu|qq=}}: Positioned beyond the torii. Built in 1940 (Shōwa 15) through offerings from parishioners. * Second Torii: Relocated from the merged Kodama Shrine in 1881. * {{ill|Stone lanterns|ja|石灯籠|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Stone lanterns|qq=}}: Present on either side of the second torii (date unknown). They are rare four-legged Miyadachi-style lanterns. * Stele of {{ill|Konpira Gongen|ja|金毘羅大権現|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Gongen|qq=}} and {{ill|Akiha Gongen|ja|秋葉権現|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akiha Gongen|qq=}}: Positioned to the left of the main hall. == Auxiliary Shrines == According to sources. Parentheses indicate deities {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1120 }}: * Shinmei Shrine ({{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|qq=}}) * Suwa Shrine (Takeminakata) * Inari Shrine ({{ill|Toyoukehime|ja|トヨウケビメ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyoukehime|qq=}}) * Asama Shrine ({{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|コノハナノサクヤビメ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|qq=}}) * Mountain God Shrine ({{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|オオヤマツミ|1=Ōyamatsumi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|qq=}}) == Festivals == * Spring Festival: Features flower dances, Okame dances, and {{ill|Shishi-mai|ja|獅子舞|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shishi-mai|qq=}}. == Cultural Properties == * Sword marked "Made by Nagachika of Shinano Province": A sword by the Shinano swordsmith Nagachika (Tenbun–Keichō eras). Length: 2 shaku, 3 sun, 4 bu. Originally belonging to the Zakōji family, it was bought back through parishioners' donations and enshrined {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |pp=1120–1121 }}. == Access == {{Maplink2|frame=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-width=220|frame-height=240|text=Map. Shimodaira Station is in the lower right.}} ;[[Public Transport]] :The nearest station is {{ill|Shimodaira Station|ja|下平駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimodaira Station|qq=}} on the {{ill|Iida Line|ja|飯田線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iida Line|qq=}} operated by {{ill|JR Central|ja|東海旅客鉄道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JR Central|qq=}}. It is 701 meters away in a straight line. == Notes == === Annotations === {{Reflist|group="Note"}} === References === {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|title=Upper Volume of the Takamori Town History|author=Takamori Town History Compilation Committee|publisher=Takamori Town History Publication Committee|year=1972|ref=harv}} == See Also == {{Commonscat}} * {{ill|Yamabuki Jin'ya|ja|山吹陣屋|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamabuki Jin'ya|qq=}} {{translated page|ja|泰山神社|version=98169647|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Takamori, Nagano]] [[Category:History of Takamori, Nagano]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Taisan Shrine]] }} [[sn:Taisan Shrine]] [[ja:泰山神社]]

{{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagano Prefecture|qq=}}, {{ill|Shimoina District|ja|下伊那郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimoina District|qq=}}, {{ill|Takamori, Nagano|ja|高森町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamori, Nagano|qq=}}{{Cite web|title=Mapion Directory Taizan Shrine|url=https://www.mapion.co.jp/phonebook/M06005/20403/ILSP0061155717_ipclm/|publisher=ONE COMPATH|accessdate=2021-12-04}}. On registry records, it is listed as "𣳾{{Refnest|group="Note"|"券" written with the top half as "氺".}}"{{Cite web|title=Information about Taizan Shrine|url=https://www.houjin-bangou.nta.go.jp/henkorireki-johoto.html?selHouzinNo=4100005010121|website=Corporate Number Publication Site|publisher=National Tax Agency|date=2015-10-05|accessdate=2021-12-04}}. == Deities == According to sources {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1120 }}: * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|lt=Homuda Wake no Mikoto|ja|応神天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Ōjin|qq=}} * {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=}} * {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|スサノオ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}} * Kodama Hikonomikoto * {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeminakata|qq=}} == History == The shrine was historically known as "Hachiman Shrine," "Ōmiya," or "Ōmiya Sansha," and was highly revered by the {{ill|Zakōji clan|ja|座光寺氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zakōji clan|qq=}} {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |pp=1118–1119 }}. After the {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji era|qq=}} period, it merged with other shrines in {{ill|Yamabuki Village|ja|山吹村|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamabuki Village|qq=}}, forming its current structure and name {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1120 }}. It is a shrine closely tied to the Yamabuki Kamihira, Yamabuki Naka, and Shimohei districts of Takamori{{Cite web|title=Taizan Shrine|url=https://yamabukiku.main.jp/taizan.html|publisher=Yamabuki Ward of Takamori Town|accessdate=2021-12-07}}. === Chronology === * {{ill|Kan'ei|ja|寛永|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kan'ei|qq=}} Period: Shrine established (as recorded in the shrine's detailed ledger) {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1118 }}. * {{ill|An'ei|ja|安永|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/An'ei|qq=}} 1 (1772): Ōmiya Sansha was rebuilt and relocated {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1119 }}. * 1881 (Meiji 14): Kodama Shrine was merged and renamed "Yamabuki Shrine" {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1120 }}. * 1911 (Meiji 44): Suwa Shrine was merged, and the name was changed to the current Taizan Shrine {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1120 }}. * 2009 (Heisei 21), April 3: The shrine's main hall, kagura hall, office, and cultural facilities were destroyed by fire{{Cite news|title=Spring Festival at Taizan Shrine in Takamori Town|url=https://minamishinshu.jp/news/culture/高森町の泰山神社で春祭り.html|newspaper=Minamishinshu Shimbun|publisher=Minamishinshu Shimbunsha|date=2013-04-18|accessdate=2021-12-05}}. * 2012 (Heisei 24), June: The shrine was rebuilt. * 2015 (Heisei 27), October 5: Designated {{ill|corporate number|ja|法人番号|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/corporate number|qq=}} 4100005010121. == Grounds == According to sources {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |pp=1121–1122 }}: * {{ill|Garden|ja|庭園|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Garden|qq=}}: A garden with a pond is arranged at the front of the precincts. * {{ill|Path|ja|参道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Path|qq=}}: The garden path leads alongside the garden. A pair of stone lanterns (dated Man'ei era) stand here. * {{ill|Torii|ja|鳥居|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Torii|qq=}}: Built in 1901 (Meiji 34), the torii is a stone Myōjin-style gate. * Grand Shrine Inscription Monument: Erected on April 10, 1923 (Taishō 12). Located to the right of the above torii, with inscriptions by {{ill|Kameo Mitsutomi|ja|神尾光臣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kameo Mitsutomi|qq=}}. * {{ill|Komainu|ja|狛犬|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komainu|qq=}}: Positioned beyond the torii. Built in 1940 (Shōwa 15) through offerings from parishioners. * Second Torii: Relocated from the merged Kodama Shrine in 1881. * {{ill|Stone lanterns|ja|石灯籠|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Stone lanterns|qq=}}: Present on either side of the second torii (date unknown). They are rare four-legged Miyadachi-style lanterns. * Stele of {{ill|Konpira Gongen|ja|金毘羅大権現|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Gongen|qq=}} and {{ill|Akiba Gongen|ja|秋葉権現|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akiha Gongen|qq=}}: Positioned to the left of the main hall. == Auxiliary Shrines == According to sources. Parentheses indicate deities {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1120 }}: * Shinmei Shrine ({{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|qq=}}) * Suwa Shrine (Takeminakata) * Inari Shrine ({{ill|Toyoukehime|ja|トヨウケビメ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyoukehime|qq=}}) * Asama Shrine ({{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|コノハナノサクヤビメ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|qq=}}) * Mountain God Shrine ({{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|オオヤマツミ|1=Ōyamatsumi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|qq=}}) == Festivals == * Spring Festival: Features flower dances, Okame dances, and {{ill|Shishi-mai|ja|獅子舞|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shishi-mai|qq=}}. == Cultural Properties == * Sword marked "Made by Nagachika of Shinano Province": A sword by the Shinano swordsmith Nagachika (Tenbun–Keichō eras). Length: 2 shaku, 3 sun, 4 bu. Originally belonging to the Zakōji family, it was bought back through parishioners' donations and enshrined {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |pp=1120–1121 }}. == Access == {{Maplink2|frame=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-width=220|frame-height=240|text=Map. Shimodaira Station is in the lower right.}} ;[[Public Transport]] :The nearest station is {{ill|Shimodaira Station|ja|下平駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimodaira Station|qq=}} on the {{ill|Iida Line|ja|飯田線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iida Line|qq=}} operated by {{ill|JR Central|ja|東海旅客鉄道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JR Central|qq=}}. It is 701 meters away in a straight line. == Notes == === Annotations === {{Reflist|group="Note"}} === References === {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|title=Upper Volume of the Takamori Town History|author=Takamori Town History Compilation Committee|publisher=Takamori Town History Publication Committee|year=1972|ref=harv}} == See Also == {{Commonscat}} * {{ill|Yamabuki Jin'ya|ja|山吹陣屋|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamabuki Jin'ya|qq=}} {{translated page|ja|泰山神社|version=98169647|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Takamori, Nagano]] [[Category:History of Takamori, Nagano]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] }} [[sn:Taisan Shrine]] [[ja:泰山神社]]

Built in 1940 (Shōwa 15) through offerings from parishioners. * Second Torii: Relocated from the merged Kodama Shrine in 1881. * {{ill|Stone lanterns|ja|石灯籠|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Stone lanterns|qq=}}: Present on either side of the second torii (date unknown). Parentheses indicate deities {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1120 }}: * Shinmei Shrine ([[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]]) * Suwa Shrine (Takeminakata) * Inari Shrine ([[:en:Toyouke-hime|Toyoukehime]]) * Asama Shrine ([[:en:Konohanasakuya-hime|Konohanasakuya-hime]]) * Mountain God Shrine ([[:en:Ōyamatsumi|Ōyamatsumi]]) == Festivals == * Spring Festival: Features flower dances, Okame dances, and [[:en:Lion dance|Shishi-mai]]. == Cultural Properties == * Sword marked "Made by Nagachika of Shinano Province": A sword by the Shinano swordsmith Nagachika (Tenbun–Keichō eras). Length: 2 shaku, 3 sun, 4 bu. Originally belonging to the Zakōji family, it was bought back through parishioners' donations and enshrined {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |pp=1120–1121 }}. == Access == {{Maplink2|frame=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-width=220|frame-height=240|text=Map. Shimodaira Station is in the lower right.}} ;[[Public Transport]] :The nearest station is [[:en:Shimodaira Station|Shimodaira Station]] on the [[:en:Iida Line|Iida Line]] operated by [[:en:Central Japan Railway Company|JR Central]]. It is 701 meters away in a straight line. == Notes == === Annotations === {{Reflist|group="Note"}} === References === {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|title=Upper Volume of the Takamori Town History|author=Takamori Town History Compilation Committee|publisher=Takamori Town History Publication Committee|year=1972|ref=harv}} == See Also == {{Commonscat}} * {{ill|Yamabuki Jin'ya|ja|山吹陣屋|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamabuki Jin'ya|qq=}} {{translated page|ja|泰山神社|version=98169647|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Takamori, Nagano]] [[Category:History of Takamori, Nagano]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] }} [[sn:Taisan Shrine]] [[ja:泰山神社]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano Prefecture]]

{{Shrine |name = Taizan Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image =[[File:Taizanjinnja2.jpg|300px]]
Taizan Shrine's Torii Gate and Grand Shrine Inscription |location = [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]][[:en:Shimoina District, Nagano|Shimoina District, Nagano]][[:en:Takamori, Nagano|Takamori, Nagano]] Oaza Yamabuki Azamaruyama 1048{{Cite web|title=Shrine Introduction Iii Branch|url=https://www.nagano-jinjacho.jp/shibu/hani.htm|publisher=Nagano Shrine Association|accessdate=2021-12-04}} | Latitude = 35 | Lat_min = 34 | Lat_sec = 28.5 | Longitude = 137 | Lon_min = 53 | Lon_sec = 18.4 |ISO = JP-20 |Label_Position = right |Map = Japan Nagano |deity = [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Homuda Wake no Mikoto]]・{{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=}}・[[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]]・Kodama Hikonomikoto・{{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeminakata|qq=}} {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1120 }} |Status = |consecration_year = [[:en:Kan'ei|Kan'ei]] Period {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1118 }} |Main_Hall = |Alias = |Festival = |Ritual = }} [[File:Taizanjinnja1.jpg|thumb|Main Hall]] '''Taizan Shrine''' (泰山神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|shrine]] located in [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]], [[:en:Shimoina District, Nagano|Shimoina District]], {{ill|Takamori, Nagano|ja|高森町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamori, Nagano|qq=}}{{Cite web|title=Mapion Directory Taizan Shrine|url=https://www.mapion.co.jp/phonebook/M06005/20403/ILSP0061155717_ipclm/|publisher=ONE COMPATH|accessdate=2021-12-04}}. On registry records, it is listed as "𣳾{{Refnest|group="Note"|"券" written with the top half as "氺".}}"{{Cite web|title=Information about Taizan Shrine|url=https://www.houjin-bangou.nta.go.jp/henkorireki-johoto.html?selHouzinNo=4100005010121|website=Corporate Number Publication Site|publisher=National Tax Agency|date=2015-10-05|accessdate=2021-12-04}}. == Deities == According to sources {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1120 }}: * [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Homuda Wake no Mikoto]] * {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=}} * [[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] * Kodama Hikonomikoto * {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeminakata|qq=}} == History == The shrine was historically known as "Hachiman Shrine," "Ōmiya," or "Ōmiya Sansha," and was highly revered by the {{ill|Zakōji clan|ja|座光寺氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zakōji clan|qq=}} {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |pp=1118–1119 }}. After the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] period, it merged with other shrines in {{ill|Yamabuki Village|ja|山吹村|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamabuki Village|qq=}}, forming its current structure and name {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1120 }}. It is a shrine closely tied to the Yamabuki Kamihira, Yamabuki Naka, and Shimohei districts of Takamori{{Cite web|title=Taizan Shrine|url=https://yamabukiku.main.jp/taizan.html|publisher=Yamabuki Ward of Takamori Town|accessdate=2021-12-07}}. === Chronology === * [[:en:Kan'ei|Kan'ei]] Period: Shrine established (as recorded in the shrine's detailed ledger) {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1118 }}. * {{ill|An'ei|ja|安永|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/An'ei|qq=}} 1 (1772): Ōmiya Sansha was rebuilt and relocated {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1119 }}. * 1881 (Meiji 14): Kodama Shrine was merged and renamed "Yamabuki Shrine" {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1120 }}. * 1911 (Meiji 44): Suwa Shrine was merged, and the name was changed to the current Taizan Shrine {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1120 }}. * 2009 (Heisei 21), April 3: The shrine's main hall, kagura hall, office, and cultural facilities were destroyed by fire{{Cite news|title=Spring Festival at Taizan Shrine in Takamori Town|url=https://minamishinshu.jp/news/culture/高森町の泰山神社で春祭り.html|newspaper=Minamishinshu Shimbun|publisher=Minamishinshu Shimbunsha|date=2013-04-18|accessdate=2021-12-05}}. * 2012 (Heisei 24), June: The shrine was rebuilt. * 2015 (Heisei 27), October 5: Designated [[:en:Corporate Number|corporate number]] 4100005010121. == Grounds == According to sources {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |pp=1121–1122 }}: * [[:en:Garden|Garden]]: A garden with a pond is arranged at the front of the precincts. * [[:en:Sandō|Path]]: The garden path leads alongside the garden. A pair of stone lanterns (dated Man'ei era) stand here. * [[:en:Torii|Torii]]: Built in 1901 (Meiji 34), the torii is a stone Myōjin-style gate. * Grand Shrine Inscription Monument: Erected on April 10, 1923 (Taishō 12). Located to the right of the above torii, with inscriptions by [[:en:Kamio Mitsuomi|Kameo Mitsutomi]]. * [[:en:Komainu|Komainu]]: Positioned beyond the torii. Built in 1940 (Shōwa 15) through offerings from parishioners. * Second Torii: Relocated from the merged Kodama Shrine in 1881. * {{ill|Stone lanterns|ja|石灯籠|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Stone lanterns|qq=}}: Present on either side of the second torii (date unknown). They are rare four-legged Miyadachi-style lanterns. * Stele of {{ill|Konpira Gongen|ja|金毘羅大権現|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Gongen|qq=}} and {{ill|Akiba Gongen|ja|秋葉権現|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akiha Gongen|qq=}}: Positioned to the left of the main hall. == Auxiliary Shrines == According to sources. Parentheses indicate deities {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1120 }}: * Shinmei Shrine ([[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]]) * Suwa Shrine (Takeminakata) * Inari Shrine ([[:en:Toyouke-hime|Toyoukehime]]) * Asama Shrine ([[:en:Konohanasakuya-hime|Konohanasakuya-hime]]) * Mountain God Shrine ([[:en:Ōyamatsumi|Ōyamatsumi]]) == Festivals == * Spring Festival: Features flower dances, Okame dances, and [[:en:Lion dance|Shishi-mai]]. == Cultural Properties == * Sword marked "Made by Nagachika of Shinano Province": A sword by the Shinano swordsmith Nagachika (Tenbun–Keichō eras). Length: 2 shaku, 3 sun, 4 bu. Originally belonging to the Zakōji family, it was bought back through parishioners' donations and enshrined {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |pp=1120–1121 }}. == Access == {{Maplink2|frame=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-width=220|frame-height=240|text=Map. Shimodaira Station is in the lower right.}} ;[[Public Transport]] :The nearest station is [[:en:Shimodaira Station|Shimodaira Station]] on the [[:en:Iida Line|Iida Line]] operated by [[:en:Central Japan Railway Company|JR Central]]. It is 701 meters away in a straight line. == Notes == === Annotations === {{Reflist|group="Note"}} === References === {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|title=Upper Volume of the Takamori Town History|author=Takamori Town History Compilation Committee|publisher=Takamori Town History Publication Committee|year=1972|ref=harv}} == See Also == {{Commonscat}} * {{ill|Yamabuki Jin'ya|ja|山吹陣屋|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamabuki Jin'ya|qq=}} {{translated page|ja|泰山神社|version=98169647|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Takamori, Nagano]] [[Category:History of Takamori, Nagano]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] }} [[sn:Taisan Shrine]] [[ja:泰山神社]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:長野県高森町の建築物]]

On registry records, it is listed as "𣳾{{Refnest|group="Note"|"券" written with the top half as "氺".}}"{{Cite web|title=Information about Taizan Shrine|url=https://www.houjin-bangou.nta.go.jp/henkorireki-johoto.html?selHouzinNo=4100005010121|website=Corporate Number Publication Site|publisher=National Tax Agency|date=2015-10-05|accessdate=2021-12-04}}. == Deities == According to sources {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1120 }}: * [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Homuda Wake no Mikoto]] * {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=}} * [[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] * Kodama Hikonomikoto * {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeminakata|qq=}} == History == The shrine was historically known as "Hachiman Shrine," "Ōmiya," or "Ōmiya Sansha," and was highly revered by the {{ill|Zakōji clan|ja|座光寺氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zakōji clan|qq=}} {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |pp=1118–1119 }}. After the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] period, it merged with other shrines in {{ill|Yamabuki Village|ja|山吹村|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamabuki Village|qq=}}, forming its current structure and name {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1120 }}. It is a shrine closely tied to the Yamabuki Kamihira, Yamabuki Naka, and Shimohei districts of Takamori{{Cite web|title=Taizan Shrine|url=https://yamabukiku.main.jp/taizan.html|publisher=Yamabuki Ward of Takamori Town|accessdate=2021-12-07}}. === Chronology === * [[:en:Kan'ei|Kan'ei]] Period: Shrine established (as recorded in the shrine's detailed ledger) {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1118 }}. * {{ill|An'ei|ja|安永|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/An'ei|qq=}} 1 (1772): Ōmiya Sansha was rebuilt and relocated {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1119 }}. * 1881 (Meiji 14): Kodama Shrine was merged and renamed "Yamabuki Shrine" {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1120 }}. * 1911 (Meiji 44): Suwa Shrine was merged, and the name was changed to the current Taizan Shrine {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1120 }}. * 2009 (Heisei 21), April 3: The shrine's main hall, kagura hall, office, and cultural facilities were destroyed by fire{{Cite news|title=Spring Festival at Taizan Shrine in Takamori Town|url=https://minamishinshu.jp/news/culture/高森町の泰山神社で春祭り.html|newspaper=Minamishinshu Shimbun|publisher=Minamishinshu Shimbunsha|date=2013-04-18|accessdate=2021-12-05}}. * 2012 (Heisei 24), June: The shrine was rebuilt. * 2015 (Heisei 27), October 5: Designated [[:en:Corporate Number|corporate number]] 4100005010121. == Grounds == According to sources {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |pp=1121–1122 }}: * [[:en:Garden|Garden]]: A garden with a pond is arranged at the front of the precincts. * [[:en:Sandō|Path]]: The garden path leads alongside the garden. A pair of stone lanterns (dated Man'ei era) stand here. * [[:en:Torii|Torii]]: Built in 1901 (Meiji 34), the torii is a stone Myōjin-style gate. * Grand Shrine Inscription Monument: Erected on April 10, 1923 (Taishō 12). Located to the right of the above torii, with inscriptions by [[:en:Kamio Mitsuomi|Kameo Mitsutomi]]. * [[:en:Komainu|Komainu]]: Positioned beyond the torii. Built in 1940 (Shōwa 15) through offerings from parishioners. * Second Torii: Relocated from the merged Kodama Shrine in 1881. * {{ill|Stone lanterns|ja|石灯籠|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Stone lanterns|qq=}}: Present on either side of the second torii (date unknown). They are rare four-legged Miyadachi-style lanterns. * Stele of {{ill|Konpira Gongen|ja|金毘羅大権現|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Gongen|qq=}} and {{ill|Akiba Gongen|ja|秋葉権現|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akiha Gongen|qq=}}: Positioned to the left of the main hall. == Auxiliary Shrines == According to sources. Parentheses indicate deities {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1120 }}: * Shinmei Shrine ([[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]]) * Suwa Shrine (Takeminakata) * Inari Shrine ([[:en:Toyouke-hime|Toyoukehime]]) * Asama Shrine ([[:en:Konohanasakuya-hime|Konohanasakuya-hime]]) * Mountain God Shrine ([[:en:Ōyamatsumi|Ōyamatsumi]]) == Festivals == * Spring Festival: Features flower dances, Okame dances, and [[:en:Lion dance|Shishi-mai]]. == Cultural Properties == * Sword marked "Made by Nagachika of Shinano Province": A sword by the Shinano swordsmith Nagachika (Tenbun–Keichō eras). Length: 2 shaku, 3 sun, 4 bu. Originally belonging to the Zakōji family, it was bought back through parishioners' donations and enshrined {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |pp=1120–1121 }}. == Access == {{Maplink2|frame=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-width=220|frame-height=240|text=Map. Shimodaira Station is in the lower right.}} ;[[Public Transport]] :The nearest station is [[:en:Shimodaira Station|Shimodaira Station]] on the [[:en:Iida Line|Iida Line]] operated by [[:en:Central Japan Railway Company|JR Central]]. It is 701 meters away in a straight line. == Notes == === Annotations === {{Reflist|group="Note"}} === References === {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|title=Upper Volume of the Takamori Town History|author=Takamori Town History Compilation Committee|publisher=Takamori Town History Publication Committee|year=1972|ref=harv}} == See Also == {{Commonscat}} * {{ill|Yamabuki Jin'ya|ja|山吹陣屋|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamabuki Jin'ya|qq=}} {{translated page|ja|泰山神社|version=98169647|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Takamori, Nagano]] [[Category:History of Takamori, Nagano]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] }} [[sn:Taisan Shrine]] [[ja:泰山神社]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:長野県高森町の建築物]] [[Category:長野県高森町の歴史]] {{Commons category|Taizan-jinja}}

Originally belonging to the Zakōji family, it was bought back through parishioners' donations and enshrined {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |pp=1120–1121 }}. == Access == {{Maplink2|frame=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-width=220|frame-height=240|text=Map. Shimodaira Station is in the lower right.}} ;[[Public Transport]] :The nearest station is [[:en:Shimodaira Station|Shimodaira Station]] on the [[:en:Iida Line|Iida Line]] operated by [[:en:Central Japan Railway Company|JR Central]]. It is 701 meters away in a straight line. == Notes == === Annotations === {{Reflist|group="Note"}} === References === {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|title=Upper Volume of the Takamori Town History|author=Takamori Town History Compilation Committee|publisher=Takamori Town History Publication Committee|year=1972|ref=harv}} == See Also == {{Commonscat}} * {{ill|Yamabuki Jin'ya|ja|山吹陣屋|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamabuki Jin'ya|qq=}} {{translated page|ja|泰山神社|version=98169647|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Takamori, Nagano]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] }} [[sn:Taisan Shrine]] [[ja:泰山神社]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:長野県高森町の建築物]] [[Category:長野県高森町の歴史]] {{Commons category|Taizan-jinja}}

revision.comment: Bot: remove deleted category History of Takamori, Nagano
revision.text:
{{Shrine |name = Taizan Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image =[[File:Taizanjinnja2.jpg|300px]]
Taizan Shrine's Torii Gate and Grand Shrine Inscription |location = [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]][[:en:Shimoina District, Nagano|Shimoina District, Nagano]][[:en:Takamori, Nagano|Takamori, Nagano]] Oaza Yamabuki Azamaruyama 1048{{Cite web|title=Shrine Introduction Iii Branch|url=https://www.nagano-jinjacho.jp/shibu/hani.htm|publisher=Nagano Shrine Association|accessdate=2021-12-04}} | Latitude = 35 | Lat_min = 34 | Lat_sec = 28.5 | Longitude = 137 | Lon_min = 53 | Lon_sec = 18.4 |ISO = JP-20 |Label_Position = right |Map = Japan Nagano |deity = [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Homuda Wake no Mikoto]]・{{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル}}・[[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]]・Kodama Hikonomikoto・{{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeminakata|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タケミナカタ}} {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1120 }} |Status = |consecration_year = [[:en:Kan'ei|Kan'ei]] Period {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1118 }} |Main_Hall = |Alias = |Festival = |Ritual = }} [[File:Taizanjinnja1.jpg|thumb|Main Hall]] '''Taizan Shrine''' (泰山神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|shrine]] located in [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]], [[:en:Shimoina District, Nagano|Shimoina District]], {{ill|Takamori, Nagano|ja|高森町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamori, Nagano|qq=}}{{Cite web|title=Mapion Directory Taizan Shrine|url=https://www.mapion.co.jp/phonebook/M06005/20403/ILSP0061155717_ipclm/|publisher=ONE COMPATH|accessdate=2021-12-04}}. On registry records, it is listed as "𣳾{{Refnest|group="Note"|"券" written with the top half as "氺".}}"{{Cite web|title=Information about Taizan Shrine|url=https://www.houjin-bangou.nta.go.jp/henkorireki-johoto.html?selHouzinNo=4100005010121|website=Corporate Number Publication Site|publisher=National Tax Agency|date=2015-10-05|accessdate=2021-12-04}}. == Deities == According to sources {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1120 }}: * [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Homuda Wake no Mikoto]] * {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル}} * [[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] * Kodama Hikonomikoto * {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeminakata|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タケミナカタ}} == History == The shrine was historically known as "Hachiman Shrine," "Ōmiya," or "Ōmiya Sansha," and was highly revered by the {{ill|Zakōji clan|ja|座光寺氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zakōji clan|qq=}} {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |pp=1118–1119 }}. After the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] period, it merged with other shrines in {{ill|Yamabuki Village|ja|山吹村|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamabuki Village|qq=}}, forming its current structure and name {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1120 }}. It is a shrine closely tied to the Yamabuki Kamihira, Yamabuki Naka, and Shimohei districts of Takamori{{Cite web|title=Taizan Shrine|url=https://yamabukiku.main.jp/taizan.html|publisher=Yamabuki Ward of Takamori Town|accessdate=2021-12-07}}. === Chronology === * [[:en:Kan'ei|Kan'ei]] Period: Shrine established (as recorded in the shrine's detailed ledger) {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1118 }}. * {{ill|An'ei|ja|安永|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/An'ei|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 安永 (元号)}} 1 (1772): Ōmiya Sansha was rebuilt and relocated {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1119 }}. * 1881 (Meiji 14): Kodama Shrine was merged and renamed "Yamabuki Shrine" {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1120 }}. * 1911 (Meiji 44): Suwa Shrine was merged, and the name was changed to the current Taizan Shrine {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1120 }}. * 2009 (Heisei 21), April 3: The shrine's main hall, kagura hall, office, and cultural facilities were destroyed by fire{{Cite news|title=Spring Festival at Taizan Shrine in Takamori Town|url=https://minamishinshu.jp/news/culture/高森町の泰山神社で春祭り.html|newspaper=Minamishinshu Shimbun|publisher=Minamishinshu Shimbunsha|date=2013-04-18|accessdate=2021-12-05}}. * 2012 (Heisei 24), June: The shrine was rebuilt. * 2015 (Heisei 27), October 5: Designated [[:en:Corporate Number|corporate number]] 4100005010121. == Grounds == According to sources {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |pp=1121–1122 }}: * [[:en:Garden|Garden]]: A garden with a pond is arranged at the front of the precincts. * [[:en:Sandō|Path]]: The garden path leads alongside the garden. A pair of stone lanterns (dated Man'ei era) stand here. * [[:en:Torii|Torii]]: Built in 1901 (Meiji 34), the torii is a stone Myōjin-style gate. * Grand Shrine Inscription Monument: Erected on April 10, 1923 (Taishō 12). Located to the right of the above torii, with inscriptions by [[:en:Kamio Mitsuomi|Kameo Mitsutomi]]. * [[:en:Komainu|Komainu]]: Positioned beyond the torii. Built in 1940 (Shōwa 15) through offerings from parishioners. * Second Torii: Relocated from the merged Kodama Shrine in 1881. * {{ill|Stone lanterns|ja|石灯籠|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Stone lanterns|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 灯籠}}: Present on either side of the second torii (date unknown). They are rare four-legged Miyadachi-style lanterns. * Stele of {{ill|Konpira Gongen|ja|金毘羅大権現|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Gongen|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 金毘羅権現}} and {{ill|Akiba Gongen|ja|秋葉権現|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akiha Gongen|qq=}}: Positioned to the left of the main hall. == Auxiliary Shrines == According to sources. Parentheses indicate deities {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1120 }}: * Shinmei Shrine ([[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]]) * Suwa Shrine (Takeminakata) * Inari Shrine ([[:en:Toyouke-hime|Toyoukehime]]) * Asama Shrine ([[:en:Konohanasakuya-hime|Konohanasakuya-hime]]) * Mountain God Shrine ([[:en:Ōyamatsumi|Ōyamatsumi]]) == Festivals == * Spring Festival: Features flower dances, Okame dances, and [[:en:Lion dance|Shishi-mai]]. == Cultural Properties == * Sword marked "Made by Nagachika of Shinano Province": A sword by the Shinano swordsmith Nagachika (Tenbun–Keichō eras). Length: 2 shaku, 3 sun, 4 bu. Originally belonging to the Zakōji family, it was bought back through parishioners' donations and enshrined {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |pp=1120–1121 }}. == Access == {{Maplink2|frame=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-width=220|frame-height=240|text=Map. Shimodaira Station is in the lower right.}} ;[[Public Transport]] :The nearest station is [[:en:Shimodaira Station|Shimodaira Station]] on the [[:en:Iida Line|Iida Line]] operated by [[:en:Central Japan Railway Company|JR Central]]. It is 701 meters away in a straight line. == Notes == === Annotations === {{Reflist|group="Note"}} === References === {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|title=Upper Volume of the Takamori Town History|author=Takamori Town History Compilation Committee|publisher=Takamori Town History Publication Committee|year=1972|ref=harv}} == See Also == {{Commonscat}} * {{ill|Yamabuki Jin'ya|ja|山吹陣屋|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamabuki Jin'ya|qq=}} {{translated page|ja|泰山神社|version=98169647|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] }} [[sn:Taisan Shrine]] [[ja:泰山神社]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:長野県高森町の建築物]] [[Category:長野県高森町の歴史]] {{Commons category|Taizan-jinja}}

Taizan Shrine's Torii Gate and Grand Shrine Inscription |location = [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]][[:en:Shimoina District, Nagano|Shimoina District, Nagano]][[:en:Takamori, Nagano|Takamori, Nagano]] Oaza Yamabuki Azamaruyama 1048{{Cite web|title=Shrine Introduction Iii Branch|url=https://www.nagano-jinjacho.jp/shibu/hani.htm|publisher=Nagano Shrine Association|accessdate=2021-12-04}} | Latitude = 35 | Lat_min = 34 | Lat_sec = 28.5 | Longitude = 137 | Lon_min = 53 | Lon_sec = 18.4 |ISO = JP-20 |Label_Position = right |Map = Japan Nagano |deity = [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Homuda Wake no Mikoto]]・{{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル}}・[[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]]・Kodama Hikonomikoto・{{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeminakata|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タケミナカタ}} {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1120 }} |Status = |consecration_year = [[:en:Kan'ei|Kan'ei]] Period {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1118 }} |Main_Hall = |Alias = |Festival = |Ritual = }} [[File:Taizanjinnja1.jpg|thumb|Main Hall]] '''Taizan Shrine''' (泰山神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|shrine]] located in [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]], [[:en:Shimoina District, Nagano|Shimoina District]], {{ill|Takamori, Nagano|ja|高森町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamori, Nagano|qq=}}{{Cite web|title=Mapion Directory Taizan Shrine|url=https://www.mapion.co.jp/phonebook/M06005/20403/ILSP0061155717_ipclm/|publisher=ONE COMPATH|accessdate=2021-12-04}}. On registry records, it is listed as "𣳾{{Refnest|group="Note"|"券" written with the top half as "氺".}}"{{Cite web|title=Information about Taizan Shrine|url=https://www.houjin-bangou.nta.go.jp/henkorireki-johoto.html?selHouzinNo=4100005010121|website=Corporate Number Publication Site|publisher=National Tax Agency|date=2015-10-05|accessdate=2021-12-04}}. == Deities == According to sources {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1120 }}: * [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Homuda Wake no Mikoto]] * {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル}} * [[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] * Kodama Hikonomikoto * {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeminakata|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タケミナカタ}} == History == The shrine was historically known as "Hachiman Shrine," "Ōmiya," or "Ōmiya Sansha," and was highly revered by the {{ill|Zakōji clan|ja|座光寺氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zakōji clan|qq=}} {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |pp=1118–1119 }}. After the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] period, it merged with other shrines in {{ill|Yamabuki Village|ja|山吹村|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamabuki Village|qq=}}, forming its current structure and name {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1120 }}. It is a shrine closely tied to the Yamabuki Kamihira, Yamabuki Naka, and Shimohei districts of Takamori{{Cite web|title=Taizan Shrine|url=https://yamabukiku.main.jp/taizan.html|publisher=Yamabuki Ward of Takamori Town|accessdate=2021-12-07}}. === Chronology === * [[:en:Kan'ei|Kan'ei]] Period: Shrine established (as recorded in the shrine's detailed ledger) {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1118 }}. * {{ill|An'ei|ja|安永|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/An'ei|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 安永 (元号)}} 1 (1772): Ōmiya Sansha was rebuilt and relocated {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1119 }}. * 1881 (Meiji 14): Kodama Shrine was merged and renamed "Yamabuki Shrine" {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1120 }}. * 1911 (Meiji 44): Suwa Shrine was merged, and the name was changed to the current Taizan Shrine {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1120 }}. * 2009 (Heisei 21), April 3: The shrine's main hall, kagura hall, office, and cultural facilities were destroyed by fire{{Cite news|title=Spring Festival at Taizan Shrine in Takamori Town|url=https://minamishinshu.jp/news/culture/高森町の泰山神社で春祭り.html|newspaper=Minamishinshu Shimbun|publisher=Minamishinshu Shimbunsha|date=2013-04-18|accessdate=2021-12-05}}. * 2012 (Heisei 24), June: The shrine was rebuilt. * 2015 (Heisei 27), October 5: Designated [[:en:Corporate Number|corporate number]] 4100005010121. == Grounds == According to sources {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |pp=1121–1122 }}: * [[:en:Garden|Garden]]: A garden with a pond is arranged at the front of the precincts. * [[:en:Sandō|Path]]: The garden path leads alongside the garden. A pair of stone lanterns (dated Man'ei era) stand here. * [[:en:Torii|Torii]]: Built in 1901 (Meiji 34), the torii is a stone Myōjin-style gate. * Grand Shrine Inscription Monument: Erected on April 10, 1923 (Taishō 12). Located to the right of the above torii, with inscriptions by [[:en:Kamio Mitsuomi|Kameo Mitsutomi]]. * [[:en:Komainu|Komainu]]: Positioned beyond the torii. Built in 1940 (Shōwa 15) through offerings from parishioners. * Second Torii: Relocated from the merged Kodama Shrine in 1881. * {{ill|Stone lanterns|ja|石灯籠|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Stone lanterns|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 灯籠}}: Present on either side of the second torii (date unknown). They are rare four-legged Miyadachi-style lanterns. * Stele of {{ill|Konpira Gongen|ja|金毘羅大権現|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Gongen|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 金毘羅権現}} and {{ill|Akiba Gongen|ja|秋葉権現|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akiha Gongen|qq=}}: Positioned to the left of the main hall. == Auxiliary Shrines == According to sources. Parentheses indicate deities {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1120 }}: * Shinmei Shrine ([[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]]) * Suwa Shrine (Takeminakata) * Inari Shrine ([[:en:Toyouke-hime|Toyoukehime]]) * Asama Shrine ([[:en:Konohanasakuya-hime|Konohanasakuya-hime]]) * Mountain God Shrine ([[:en:Ōyamatsumi|Ōyamatsumi]]) == Festivals == * Spring Festival: Features flower dances, Okame dances, and [[:en:Lion dance|Shishi-mai]]. == Cultural Properties == * Sword marked "Made by Nagachika of Shinano Province": A sword by the Shinano swordsmith Nagachika (Tenbun–Keichō eras). Length: 2 shaku, 3 sun, 4 bu. Originally belonging to the Zakōji family, it was bought back through parishioners' donations and enshrined {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |pp=1120–1121 }}. == Access == {{Maplink2|frame=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-width=220|frame-height=240|text=Map. Shimodaira Station is in the lower right.}} ;[[Public Transport]] :The nearest station is [[:en:Shimodaira Station|Shimodaira Station]] on the [[:en:Iida Line|Iida Line]] operated by [[:en:Central Japan Railway Company|JR Central]]. It is 701 meters away in a straight line. == Notes == === Annotations === {{Reflist|group="Note"}} === References === {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|title=Upper Volume of the Takamori Town History|author=Takamori Town History Compilation Committee|publisher=Takamori Town History Publication Committee|year=1972|ref=harv}} == See Also == {{Commonscat}} * {{ill|Yamabuki Jin'ya|ja|山吹陣屋|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamabuki Jin'ya|qq=}} {{translated page|ja|泰山神社|version=98169647|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 5]] }} [[sn:Taisan Shrine]] [[ja:泰山神社]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:長野県高森町の建築物]] [[Category:長野県高森の Municipal History]] {{Commons category|Taizan-jinja}}

It is 701 meters away in a straight line. == Notes == === Annotations === {{Reflist|group="Note"}} === References === {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|title=Upper Volume of the Takamori Town History|author=Takamori Town History Compilation Committee|publisher=Takamori Town History Publication Committee|year=1972|ref=harv}} == See Also == {{Commonscat}} * {{ill|Yamabuki Jin'ya|ja|山吹陣屋|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamabuki Jin'ya|qq=}} {{translated page|ja|泰山神社|version=98169647|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 5]] }} [[sn:Taisan Shrine]] [[ja:泰山神社]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Architecture of Takamori Town, Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal History of Takamori, Nagano Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Taizan-jinja}}

revision.comment: Bot: convert local link → enwiki interwiki
revision.text:
{{Shrine |name = Taizan Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image =[[File:Taizanjinnja2.jpg|300px]]
Taizan Shrine's Torii Gate and Grand Shrine Inscription |location = [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]][[:en:Shimoina District, Nagano|Shimoina District, Nagano]][[:en:Takamori, Nagano|Takamori, Nagano]] Oaza Yamabuki Azamaruyama 1048{{Cite web|title=Shrine Introduction Iii Branch|url=https://www.nagano-jinjacho.jp/shibu/hani.htm|publisher=Nagano Shrine Association|accessdate=2021-12-04}} | Latitude = 35 | Lat_min = 34 | Lat_sec = 28.5 | Longitude = 137 | Lon_min = 53 | Lon_sec = 18.4 |ISO = JP-20 |Label_Position = right |Map = Japan Nagano |deity = [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Homuda Wake no Mikoto]]・{{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル}}・[[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]]・Kodama Hikonomikoto・{{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeminakata|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タケミナカタ}} {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1120 }} |Status = |consecration_year = [[:en:Kan'ei|Kan'ei]] Period {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1118 }} |Main_Hall = |Alias = |Festival = |Ritual = }} [[File:Taizanjinnja1.jpg|thumb|Main Hall]] '''Taizan Shrine''' (泰山神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|shrine]] located in [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]], [[:en:Shimoina District, Nagano|Shimoina District]], {{ill|Takamori, Nagano|ja|高森町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamori, Nagano|qq=}}{{Cite web|title=Mapion Directory Taizan Shrine|url=https://www.mapion.co.jp/phonebook/M06005/20403/ILSP0061155717_ipclm/|publisher=ONE COMPATH|accessdate=2021-12-04}}. On registry records, it is listed as "𣳾{{Refnest|group="Note"|"券" written with the top half as "氺".}}"{{Cite web|title=Information about Taizan Shrine|url=https://www.houjin-bangou.nta.go.jp/henkorireki-johoto.html?selHouzinNo=4100005010121|website=Corporate Number Publication Site|publisher=National Tax Agency|date=2015-10-05|accessdate=2021-12-04}}. == Deities == According to sources {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1120 }}: * [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Homuda Wake no Mikoto]] * {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル}} * [[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] * Kodama Hikonomikoto * {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeminakata|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タケミナカタ}} == History == The shrine was historically known as "Hachiman Shrine," "Ōmiya," or "Ōmiya Sansha," and was highly revered by the {{ill|Zakōji clan|ja|座光寺氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zakōji clan|qq=}} {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |pp=1118–1119 }}. After the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] period, it merged with other shrines in {{ill|Yamabuki Village|ja|山吹村|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamabuki Village|qq=}}, forming its current structure and name {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1120 }}. It is a shrine closely tied to the Yamabuki Kamihira, Yamabuki Naka, and Shimohei districts of Takamori{{Cite web|title=Taizan Shrine|url=https://yamabukiku.main.jp/taizan.html|publisher=Yamabuki Ward of Takamori Town|accessdate=2021-12-07}}. === Chronology === * [[:en:Kan'ei|Kan'ei]] Period: Shrine established (as recorded in the shrine's detailed ledger) {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1118 }}. * {{ill|An'ei|ja|安永|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/An'ei|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 安永 (元号)}} 1 (1772): Ōmiya Sansha was rebuilt and relocated {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1119 }}. * 1881 (Meiji 14): Kodama Shrine was merged and renamed "Yamabuki Shrine" {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1120 }}. * 1911 (Meiji 44): Suwa Shrine was merged, and the name was changed to the current Taizan Shrine {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1120 }}. * 2009 (Heisei 21), April 3: The shrine's main hall, kagura hall, office, and cultural facilities were destroyed by fire{{Cite news|title=Spring Festival at Taizan Shrine in Takamori Town|url=https://minamishinshu.jp/news/culture/高森町の泰山神社で春祭り.html|newspaper=Minamishinshu Shimbun|publisher=Minamishinshu Shimbunsha|date=2013-04-18|accessdate=2021-12-05}}. * 2012 (Heisei 24), June: The shrine was rebuilt. * 2015 (Heisei 27), October 5: Designated [[:en:Corporate Number|corporate number]] 4100005010121. == Grounds == According to sources {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |pp=1121–1122 }}: * [[:en:Garden|Garden]]: A garden with a pond is arranged at the front of the precincts. * [[:en:Sandō|Path]]: The garden path leads alongside the garden. A pair of stone lanterns (dated Man'ei era) stand here. * [[:en:Torii|Torii]]: Built in 1901 (Meiji 34), the torii is a stone Myōjin-style gate. * Grand Shrine Inscription Monument: Erected on April 10, 1923 (Taishō 12). Located to the right of the above torii, with inscriptions by [[:en:Kamio Mitsuomi|Kameo Mitsutomi]]. * [[:en:Komainu|Komainu]]: Positioned beyond the torii. Built in 1940 (Shōwa 15) through offerings from parishioners. * Second Torii: Relocated from the merged Kodama Shrine in 1881. * {{ill|Stone lanterns|ja|石灯籠|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Stone lanterns|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 灯籠}}: Present on either side of the second torii (date unknown). They are rare four-legged Miyadachi-style lanterns. * Stele of {{ill|Konpira Gongen|ja|金毘羅大権現|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Gongen|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 金毘羅権現}} and {{ill|Akiba Gongen|ja|秋葉権現|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akiha Gongen|qq=}}: Positioned to the left of the main hall. == Auxiliary Shrines == According to sources. Parentheses indicate deities {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1120 }}: * Shinmei Shrine ([[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]]) * Suwa Shrine (Takeminakata) * Inari Shrine ([[:en:Toyouke-hime|Toyoukehime]]) * Asama Shrine ([[:en:Konohanasakuya-hime|Konohanasakuya-hime]]) * Mountain God Shrine ([[:en:Ōyamatsumi|Ōyamatsumi]]) == Festivals == * Spring Festival: Features flower dances, Okame dances, and [[:en:Lion dance|Shishi-mai]]. == Cultural Properties == * Sword marked "Made by Nagachika of Shinano Province": A sword by the Shinano swordsmith Nagachika (Tenbun–Keichō eras). Length: 2 shaku, 3 sun, 4 bu. Originally belonging to the Zakōji family, it was bought back through parishioners' donations and enshrined {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |pp=1120–1121 }}. == Access == {{Maplink2|frame=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-width=220|frame-height=240|text=Map. Shimodaira Station is in the lower right.}} ;[[:en:Public Transport|Public Transport]] :The nearest station is [[:en:Shimodaira Station|Shimodaira Station]] on the [[:en:Iida Line|Iida Line]] operated by [[:en:Central Japan Railway Company|JR Central]]. It is 701 meters away in a straight line. == Notes == === Annotations === {{Reflist|group="Note"}} === References === {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|title=Upper Volume of the Takamori Town History|author=Takamori Town History Compilation Committee|publisher=Takamori Town History Publication Committee|year=1972|ref=harv}} == See Also == {{Commonscat}} * {{ill|Yamabuki Jin'ya|ja|山吹陣屋|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamabuki Jin'ya|qq=}} {{translated page|ja|泰山神社|version=98169647|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 5]] }} [[sn:Taisan Shrine]] [[ja:泰山神社]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Architecture of Takamori Town, Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal History of Takamori, Nagano Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Taizan-jinja}}

On registry records, it is listed as "𣳾{{Refnest|group="Note"|"券" written with the top half as "氺".}}"{{Cite web|title=Information about Taizan Shrine|url=https://www.houjin-bangou.nta.go.jp/henkorireki-johoto.html?selHouzinNo=4100005010121|website=Corporate Number Publication Site|publisher=National Tax Agency|date=2015-10-05|accessdate=2021-12-04}}. == Deities == According to sources {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1120 }}: * [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Homuda Wake no Mikoto]] * {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル}} * [[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] * Kodama Hikonomikoto * {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeminakata|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タケミナカタ}} == History == The shrine was historically known as "Hachiman Shrine," "Ōmiya," or "Ōmiya Sansha," and was highly revered by the {{ill|Zakōji clan|ja|座光寺氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zakōji clan|qq=}} {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |pp=1118–1119 }}. After the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] period, it merged with other shrines in {{ill|Yamabuki Village|ja|山吹村|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamabuki Village|qq=}}, forming its current structure and name {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1120 }}. It is a shrine closely tied to the Yamabuki Kamihira, Yamabuki Naka, and Shimohei districts of Takamori{{Cite web|title=Taizan Shrine|url=https://yamabukiku.main.jp/taizan.html|publisher=Yamabuki Ward of Takamori Town|accessdate=2021-12-07}}. === Chronology === * [[:en:Kan'ei|Kan'ei]] Period: Shrine established (as recorded in the shrine's detailed ledger) {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1118 }}. * {{ill|An'ei|ja|安永|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/An'ei|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 安永 (元号)}} 1 (1772): Ōmiya Sansha was rebuilt and relocated {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1119 }}. * 1881 (Meiji 14): Kodama Shrine was merged and renamed "Yamabuki Shrine" {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1120 }}. * 1911 (Meiji 44): Suwa Shrine was merged, and the name was changed to the current Taizan Shrine {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1120 }}. * 2009 (Heisei 21), April 3: The shrine's main hall, kagura hall, office, and cultural facilities were destroyed by fire{{Cite news|title=Spring Festival at Taizan Shrine in Takamori Town|url=https://minamishinshu.jp/news/culture/高森町の泰山神社で春祭り.html|newspaper=Minamishinshu Shimbun|publisher=Minamishinshu Shimbunsha|date=2013-04-18|accessdate=2021-12-05}}. * 2012 (Heisei 24), June: The shrine was rebuilt. * 2015 (Heisei 27), October 5: Designated [[:en:Corporate Number|corporate number]] 4100005010121. == Grounds == According to sources {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |pp=1121–1122 }}: * [[:en:Garden|Garden]]: A garden with a pond is arranged at the front of the precincts. * [[:en:Sandō|Path]]: The garden path leads alongside the garden. A pair of stone lanterns (dated Man'ei era) stand here. * [[:en:Torii|Torii]]: Built in 1901 (Meiji 34), the torii is a stone Myōjin-style gate. * Grand Shrine Inscription Monument: Erected on April 10, 1923 (Taishō 12). Located to the right of the above torii, with inscriptions by [[:en:Kamio Mitsuomi|Kameo Mitsutomi]]. * [[:en:Komainu|Komainu]]: Positioned beyond the torii. Built in 1940 (Shōwa 15) through offerings from parishioners. * Second Torii: Relocated from the merged Kodama Shrine in 1881. * {{ill|Stone lanterns|ja|石灯籠|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Stone lanterns|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 灯籠}}: Present on either side of the second torii (date unknown). They are rare four-legged Miyadachi-style lanterns. * Stele of {{ill|Konpira Gongen|ja|金毘羅大権現|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Gongen|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 金毘羅権現}} and {{ill|Akiba Gongen|ja|秋葉権現|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akiha Gongen|qq=}}: Positioned to the left of the main hall. == Auxiliary Shrines == According to sources. Parentheses indicate deities {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1120 }}: * Shinmei Shrine ([[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]]) * Suwa Shrine (Takeminakata) * Inari Shrine ([[:en:Toyouke-hime|Toyoukehime]]) * Asama Shrine ([[:en:Konohanasakuya-hime|Konohanasakuya-hime]]) * Mountain God Shrine ([[:en:Ōyamatsumi|Ōyamatsumi]]) == Festivals == * Spring Festival: Features flower dances, Okame dances, and [[:en:Lion dance|Shishi-mai]]. == Cultural Properties == * Sword marked "Made by Nagachika of Shinano Province": A sword by the Shinano swordsmith Nagachika (Tenbun–Keichō eras). Length: 2 shaku, 3 sun, 4 bu. Originally belonging to the Zakōji family, it was bought back through parishioners' donations and enshrined {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |pp=1120–1121 }}. == Access == {{Maplink2|frame=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-width=220|frame-height=240|text=Map. Shimodaira Station is in the lower right.}} ;[[:en:Public Transport|Public Transport]] :The nearest station is [[:en:Shimodaira Station|Shimodaira Station]] on the [[:en:Iida Line|Iida Line]] operated by [[:en:Central Japan Railway Company|JR Central]]. It is 701 meters away in a straight line. == Notes == === Annotations === {{Reflist|group="Note"}} === References === {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|title=Upper Volume of the Takamori Town History|author=Takamori Town History Compilation Committee|publisher=Takamori Town History Publication Committee|year=1972|ref=harv}} == See Also == {{Commonscat}} * {{ill|Yamabuki Jin'ya|ja|山吹陣屋|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamabuki Jin'ya|qq=}} {{translated page|ja|泰山神社|version=98169647|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 5]] }} [[sn:Taisan Shrine]] [[ja:泰山神社]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Architecture of Takamori Town, Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal History of Takamori, Nagano Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Taizan-jinja}}

Mikoto]]・{{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato {{ill|Takamori|qid=Q1203136}} |coordinates = {{Coord|35.5745878|N|137.8884515|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} [[File:Taizanjinnja1.jpg|thumb|Main Hall]] '''Taizan Shrine''' (泰山神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|shrine]] located in [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]], [[:en:Shimoina District, Nagano|Shimoina District]], {{ill|Takamori, Nagano|ja|高森町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamori, Nagano|qq=|1=Takamorimachi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q31469}}<ref name="mapion">{{Cite web|title=Mapion Directory Taizan Shrine|url=https://www.mapion.co.jp/phonebook/M06005/20403/ILSP0061155717_ipclm/|publisher=ONE COMPATH|accessdate=2021-12-04}}</ref>. On registry records, it is listed as "𣳾{{Refnest|group="Note"|"券" written with the top half as "氺".}}"<ref name="houjin-bangou">{{Cite web|title=Information about Taizan Shrine|url=https://www.houjin-bangou.nta.go.jp/henkorireki-johoto.html?selHouzinNo=4100005010121|website=Corporate Number Publication Site|publisher=National Tax Agency|date=2015-10-05|accessdate=2021-12-04}}</ref>.
== Deities ==
According to sources {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1120 }}: * [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Homuda Wake no Mikoto]] * {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}} * [[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] * Kodama Hikonomikoto * {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeminakata|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タケミナカタ}}
== History ==
The shrine was historically known as "Hachiman Shrine," "Ōmiya," or "Ōmiya Sansha," and was highly revered by the {{ill|Zakōji clan|ja|座光寺氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zakōji clan|qq=|1=Zakōji clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109364687}} {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |pp=1118–1119 }}. After the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] period, it merged with other shrines in {{ill|Yamabuki Village|ja|山吹村|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamabuki Village|qq=|1=Yamabuki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17175082}}, forming its current structure and name {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1120 }}. It is a shrine closely tied to the Yamabuki Kamihira, Yamabuki Naka, and Shimohei districts of Takamori<ref name="yamabukiku">{{Cite web|title=Taizan Shrine|url=https://yamabukiku.main.jp/taizan.html|publisher=Yamabuki Ward of Takamori Town|accessdate=2021-12-07}}</ref>.
=== Chronology === * [[:en:Kan'ei|Kan'ei]] Period: Shrine established (as recorded in the shrine's detailed ledger) {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1118 }}. * {{ill|An'ei|ja|安永|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/An'ei|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 安永 (元号)|1=An'ei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1193910}} 1 (1772): Ōmiya Sansha was rebuilt and relocated {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1119 }}. * 1881 (Meiji 14): Kodama Shrine was merged and renamed "Yamabuki Shrine" {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1120 }}. * 1911 (Meiji 44): Suwa Shrine was merged, and the name was changed to the current Taizan Shrine {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1120 }}. * 2009 (Heisei 21), April 3: The shrine's main hall, kagura hall, office, and cultural facilities were destroyed by fire<ref name="minamishinshu_2013-04-18">{{Cite news|title=Spring Festival at Taizan Shrine in Takamori Town|url=https://minamishinshu.jp/news/culture/高森町の泰山神社で春祭り.html|newspaper=Minamishinshu Shimbun|publisher=Minamishinshu Shimbunsha|date=2013-04-18|accessdate=2021-12-05}}</ref>. * 2012 (Heisei 24), June: The shrine was rebuilt<ref name="minamishinshu_2013-04-18" />. * 2015 (Heisei 27), October 5: Designated [[:en:Corporate Number|corporate number]] 4100005010121<ref name="houjin-bangou" />.
== Grounds ==
According to sources {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |pp=1121–1122 }}: * [[:en:Garden|Garden]]: A garden with a pond is arranged at the front of the precincts. * [[:en:Sandō|Path]]: The garden path leads alongside the garden. A pair of stone lanterns (dated Man'ei era) stand here. * [[:en:Torii|Torii]]: Built in 1901 (Meiji 34), the torii is a stone Myōjin-style gate. * Grand Shrine Inscription Monument: Erected on April 10, 1923 (Taishō 12). Located to the right of the above torii, with inscriptions by [[:en:Kamio Mitsuomi|Kameo Mitsutomi]]. * [[:en:Komainu|Komainu]]: Positioned beyond the torii. Built in 1940 (Shōwa 15) through offerings from parishioners. * Second Torii: Relocated from the merged Kodama Shrine in 1881. * {{ill|Stone lanterns|ja|石灯籠|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Stone lanterns|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 灯籠|1=Stone lantern|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q12012294}}: Present on either side of the second torii (date unknown). They are rare four-legged Miyadachi-style lanterns. * Stele of {{ill|Konpira Gongen|ja|金毘羅大権現|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Gongen|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 金毘羅権現}} and {{ill|Akiba Gongen|ja|秋葉権現|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akiha Gongen|qq=}}: Positioned to the left of the main hall.
== Auxiliary Shrines ==
According to sources. Parentheses indicate deities {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1120 }}: * Shinmei Shrine ([[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]]) * Suwa Shrine (Takeminakata) * Inari Shrine ([[:en:Toyouke-hime|Toyoukehime]]) * Asama Shrine ([[:en:Konohanasakuya-hime|Konohanasakuya-hime]]) * Mountain God Shrine ([[:en:Ōyamatsumi|Ōyamatsumi]])
== Festivals == * Spring Festival: Features flower dances, Okame dances, and [[:en:Lion dance|Shishi-mai]]<ref name="yamabukiku" />.
== Cultural Properties == * Sword marked "Made by Nagachika of Shinano Province": A sword by the Shinano swordsmith Nagachika (Tenbun–Keichō eras). Length: 2 shaku, 3 sun, 4 bu. Originally belonging to the Zakōji family, it was bought back through parishioners' donations and enshrined {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |pp=1120–1121 }}.
== Access == {{Maplink2|frame=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-width=220|frame-height=240|text=Map. Shimodaira Station is in the lower right.}} ;[[:en:Public Transport|Public Transport]] :The nearest station is [[:en:Shimodaira Station|Shimodaira Station]] on the [[:en:Iida Line|Iida Line]] operated by [[:en:Central Japan Railway Company|JR Central]]. It is 701 meters away in a straight line<ref name="mapion" />.
== Notes ==

== Grounds ==
According to sources {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |pp=1121–1122 }}: * [[:en:Garden|Garden]]: A garden with a pond is arranged at the front of the precincts. * [[:en:Sandō|Path]]: The garden path leads alongside the garden. A pair of stone lanterns (dated Man'ei era) stand here. * [[:en:Torii|Torii]]: Built in 1901 (Meiji 34), the torii is a stone Myōjin-style gate. * Grand Shrine Inscription Monument: Erected on April 10, 1923 (Taishō 12). Located to the right of the above torii, with inscriptions by [[:en:Kamio Mitsuomi|Kameo Mitsutomi]]. * [[:en:Komainu|Komainu]]: Positioned beyond the torii. Built in 1940 (Shōwa 15) through offerings from parishioners. * Second Torii: Relocated from the merged Kodama Shrine in 1881. * {{ill|Stone lanterns|ja|石灯籠|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Stone lanterns|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 灯籠|1=Stone lantern|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q12012294}}: Present on either side of the second torii (date unknown). They are rare four-legged Miyadachi-style lanterns. * Stele of {{ill|Konpira Gongen|ja|金毘羅大権現|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Gongen|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 金毘羅権現}} and {{ill|Akiba Gongen|ja|秋葉権現|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akiha Gongen|qq=}}: Positioned to the left of the main hall.
== Auxiliary Shrines ==
According to sources. Parentheses indicate deities {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1120 }}: * Shinmei Shrine ([[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]]) * Suwa Shrine (Takeminakata) * Inari Shrine ([[:en:Toyouke-hime|Toyoukehime]]) * Asama Shrine ([[:en:Konohanasakuya-hime|Konohanasakuya-hime]]) * Mountain God Shrine ([[:en:Ōyamatsumi|Ōyamatsumi]])
== Festivals == * Spring Festival: Features flower dances, Okame dances, and [[:en:Lion dance|Shishi-mai]]<ref name="yamabukiku" />.
== Cultural Properties == * Sword marked "Made by Nagachika of Shinano Province": A sword by the Shinano swordsmith Nagachika (Tenbun–Keichō eras). Length: 2 shaku, 3 sun, 4 bu. Originally belonging to the Zakōji family, it was bought back through parishioners' donations and enshrined {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |pp=1120–1121 }}.
== Access == {{Maplink2|frame=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-width=220|frame-height=240|text=Map. Shimodaira Station is in the lower right.}} ;[[:en:Public Transport|Public Transport]] :The nearest station is [[:en:Shimodaira Station|Shimodaira Station]] on the [[:en:Iida Line|Iida Line]] operated by [[:en:Central Japan Railway Company|JR Central]]. It is 701 meters away in a straight line<ref name="mapion" />.

== Deities ==
According to sources {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1120 }}: * [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Homuda Wake no Mikoto]] * {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}} * [[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] * Kodama Hikonomikoto * {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeminakata|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タケミナカタ}}
== History ==
The shrine was historically known as "Hachiman Shrine," "Ōmiya," or "Ōmiya Sansha," and was highly revered by the {{ill|Zakōji clan|ja|座光寺氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zakōji clan|qq=|1=Zakōji clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109364687}} {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |pp=1118–1119 }}. After the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] period, it merged with other shrines in {{ill|Yamabuki Village|ja|山吹村|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamabuki Village|qq=|1=Yamabuki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17175082}}, forming its current structure and name {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1120 }}. It is a shrine closely tied to the Yamabuki Kamihira, Yamabuki Naka, and Shimohei districts of Takamori<ref name="yamabukiku">{{Cite web|title=Taizan Shrine|url=https://yamabukiku.main.jp/taizan.html|publisher=Yamabuki Ward of Takamori Town|accessdate=2021-12-07}}</ref>.
=== Chronology === * [[:en:Kan'ei|Kan'ei]] Period: Shrine established (as recorded in the shrine's detailed ledger) {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1118 }}. * {{ill|An'ei|ja|安永|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/An'ei|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 安永 (元号)|1=An'ei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1193910}} 1 (1772): Ōmiya Sansha was rebuilt and relocated {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1119 }}. * 1881 (Meiji 14): Kodama Shrine was merged and renamed "Yamabuki Shrine" {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1120 }}. * 1911 (Meiji 44): Suwa Shrine was merged, and the name was changed to the current Taizan Shrine {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1120 }}. * 2009 (Heisei 21), April 3: The shrine's main hall, kagura hall, office, and cultural facilities were destroyed by fire<ref name="minamishinshu_2013-04-18">{{Cite news|title=Spring Festival at Taizan Shrine in Takamori Town|url=https://minamishinshu.jp/news/culture/高森町の泰山神社で春祭り.html|newspaper=Minamishinshu Shimbun|publisher=Minamishinshu Shimbunsha|date=2013-04-18|accessdate=2021-12-05}}</ref>. * 2012 (Heisei 24), June: The shrine was rebuilt<ref name="minamishinshu_2013-04-18" />. * 2015 (Heisei 27), October 5: Designated [[:en:Corporate Number|corporate number]] 4100005010121<ref name="houjin-bangou" />.
== Grounds ==
According to sources {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |pp=1121–1122 }}: * [[:en:Garden|Garden]]: A garden with a pond is arranged at the front of the precincts. * [[:en:Sandō|Path]]: The garden path leads alongside the garden. A pair of stone lanterns (dated Man'ei era) stand here. * [[:en:Torii|Torii]]: Built in 1901 (Meiji 34), the torii is a stone Myōjin-style gate. * Grand Shrine Inscription Monument: Erected on April 10, 1923 (Taishō 12). Located to the right of the above torii, with inscriptions by [[:en:Kamio Mitsuomi|Kameo Mitsutomi]]. * [[:en:Komainu|Komainu]]: Positioned beyond the torii. Built in 1940 (Shōwa 15) through offerings from parishioners. * Second Torii: Relocated from the merged Kodama Shrine in 1881. * {{ill|Stone lanterns|ja|石灯籠|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Stone lanterns|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 灯籠|1=Stone lantern|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q12012294}}: Present on either side of the second torii (date unknown). They are rare four-legged Miyadachi-style lanterns. * Stele of {{ill|Konpira Gongen|ja|金毘羅大権現|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Gongen|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 金毘羅権現}} and {{ill|Akiba Gongen|ja|秋葉権現|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akiha Gongen|qq=}}: Positioned to the left of the main hall.
