{{ill|Takamori|WD=Q1203136}} |coordinates = {{Coord|35.5745878|N|137.8884515|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} [[File:Taizanjinnja1.jpg|thumb|Main Hall]] '''Taizan Shrine''' (泰山神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|shrine]] located in [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]], [[:en:Shimoina District, Nagano|Shimoina District]], {{ill|Takamori, Nagano|ja|高森町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamori, Nagano|1=Takamorimachi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q31469}}<ref name="mapion">{{Cite web|title=Mapion Directory Taizan Shrine|url=https://www.mapion.co.jp/phonebook/M06005/20403/ILSP0061155717_ipclm/|publisher=ONE COMPATH|accessdate=2021-12-04}}</ref>. On registry records, it is listed as "𣳾{{Refnest|group="Note"|"券" written with the top half as "氺".}}"<ref name="houjin-bangou">{{Cite web|title=Information about Taizan Shrine|url=https://www.houjin-bangou.nta.go.jp/henkorireki-johoto.html?selHouzinNo=4100005010121|website=Corporate Number Publication Site|publisher=National Tax Agency|date=2015-10-05|accessdate=2021-12-04}}</ref>.
== Deities ==
According to sources {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1120 }}: * [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Homuda Wake no Mikoto]] * {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}} * [[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] * Kodama Hikonomikoto * {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeminakata|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タケミナカタ}}
== History ==
The shrine was historically known as "Hachiman Shrine," "Ōmiya," or "Ōmiya Sansha," and was highly revered by the {{ill|Zakōji clan|ja|座光寺氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zakōji clan|1=Zakōji clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109364687}} {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |pp=1118–1119 }}. After the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] period, it merged with other shrines in {{ill|Yamabuki Village|ja|山吹村|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamabuki Village|1=Yamabuki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17175082}}, forming its current structure and name {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1120 }}. It is a shrine closely tied to the Yamabuki Kamihira, Yamabuki Naka, and Shimohei districts of Takamori<ref name="yamabukiku">{{Cite web|title=Taizan Shrine|url=https://yamabukiku.main.jp/taizan.html|publisher=Yamabuki Ward of Takamori Town|accessdate=2021-12-07}}</ref>.
=== Chronology === * [[:en:Kan'ei|Kan'ei]] Period: Shrine established (as recorded in the shrine's detailed ledger) {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1118 }}. * {{ill|An'ei|ja|安永|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/An'ei|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 安永 (元号)|1=An'ei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1193910}} 1 (1772): Ōmiya Sansha was rebuilt and relocated {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1119 }}. * 1881 (Meiji 14): Kodama Shrine was merged and renamed "Yamabuki Shrine" {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1120 }}. * 1911 (Meiji 44): Suwa Shrine was merged, and the name was changed to the current Taizan Shrine {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1120 }}. * 2009 (Heisei 21), April 3: The shrine's main hall, kagura hall, office, and cultural facilities were destroyed by fire<ref name="minamishinshu_2013-04-18">{{Cite news|title=Spring Festival at Taizan Shrine in Takamori Town|url=https://minamishinshu.jp/news/culture/高森町の泰山神社で春祭り.html|newspaper=Minamishinshu Shimbun|publisher=Minamishinshu Shimbunsha|date=2013-04-18|accessdate=2021-12-05}}</ref>. * 2012 (Heisei 24), June: The shrine was rebuilt<ref name="minamishinshu_2013-04-18" />. * 2015 (Heisei 27), October 5: Designated [[:en:Corporate Number|corporate number]] 4100005010121<ref name="houjin-bangou" />.
== Grounds ==
According to sources {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |pp=1121–1122 }}: * [[:en:Garden|Garden]]: A garden with a pond is arranged at the front of the precincts. * [[:en:Sandō|Path]]: The garden path leads alongside the garden. A pair of stone lanterns (dated Man'ei era) stand here. * [[:en:Torii|Torii]]: Built in 1901 (Meiji 34), the torii is a stone Myōjin-style gate. * Grand Shrine Inscription Monument: Erected on April 10, 1923 (Taishō 12). Located to the right of the above torii, with inscriptions by [[:en:Kamio Mitsuomi|Kameo Mitsutomi]]. * [[:en:Komainu|Komainu]]: Positioned beyond the torii. Built in 1940 (Shōwa 15) through offerings from parishioners. * Second Torii: Relocated from the merged Kodama Shrine in 1881. * {{ill|Stone lanterns|ja|石灯籠|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Stone lanterns|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 灯籠|1=Stone lantern|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q12012294}}: Present on either side of the second torii (date unknown). They are rare four-legged Miyadachi-style lanterns. * Stele of {{ill|Konpira Gongen|ja|金毘羅大権現|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Gongen|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 金毘羅権現}} and {{ill|Akiba Gongen|ja|秋葉権現|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akiha Gongen|qq=}}: Positioned to the left of the main hall.
== Auxiliary Shrines ==
According to sources. Parentheses indicate deities {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1120 }}: * Shinmei Shrine ([[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]]) * Suwa Shrine (Takeminakata) * Inari Shrine ([[:en:Toyouke-hime|Toyoukehime]]) * Asama Shrine ([[:en:Konohanasakuya-hime|Konohanasakuya-hime]]) * Mountain God Shrine ([[:en:Ōyamatsumi|Ōyamatsumi]])
== Festivals == * Spring Festival: Features flower dances, Okame dances, and [[:en:Lion dance|Shishi-mai]]<ref name="yamabukiku" />.
== Cultural Properties == * Sword marked "Made by Nagachika of Shinano Province": A sword by the Shinano swordsmith Nagachika (Tenbun–Keichō eras). Length: 2 shaku, 3 sun, 4 bu. Originally belonging to the Zakōji family, it was bought back through parishioners' donations and enshrined {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |pp=1120–1121 }}.
== Access == {{Maplink2|frame=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-width=220|frame-height=240|text=Map. Shimodaira Station is in the lower right.}} ;[[:en:Public Transport|Public Transport]] :The nearest station is [[:en:Shimodaira Station|Shimodaira Station]] on the [[:en:Iida Line|Iida Line]] operated by [[:en:Central Japan Railway Company|JR Central]]. It is 701 meters away in a straight line<ref name="mapion" />.

The shrine was historically known as "Hachiman Shrine," "Ōmiya," or "Ōmiya Sansha," and was highly revered by the {{ill|Zakōji clan|ja|座光寺氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zakōji clan|1=Zakōji clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109364687}} {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |pp=1118–1119 }}. Nagano|ja|高森町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamori, Nagano|1=Takamorimachi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q31469}}<ref name="mapion">{{Cite web|title=Mapion Directory Taizan Shrine|url=https://www.mapion.co.jp/phonebook/M06005/20403/ILSP0061155717_ipclm/|publisher=ONE COMPATH|accessdate=2021-12-04}}</ref>. On registry records, it is listed as "𣳾{{Refnest|group="Note"|"券" written with the top half as "氺".}}"<ref name="houjin-bangou">{{Cite web|title=Information about Taizan Shrine|url=https://www.houjin-bangou.nta.go.jp/henkorireki-johoto.html?selHouzinNo=4100005010121|website=Corporate Number Publication Site|publisher=National Tax Agency|date=2015-10-05|accessdate=2021-12-04}}</ref>.
== Deities ==
According to sources {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1120 }}: * [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Homuda Wake no Mikoto]] * {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}} * [[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] * Kodama Hikonomikoto * {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeminakata|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タケミナカタ}}
== History ==
The shrine was historically known as "Hachiman Shrine," "Ōmiya," or "Ōmiya Sansha," and was highly revered by the {{ill|Zakōji clan|ja|座光寺氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zakōji clan|1=Zakōji clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109364687}} {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |pp=1118–1119 }}. After the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] period, it merged with other shrines in {{ill|Yamabuki Village|ja|山吹村|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamabuki Village|1=Yamabuki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17175082}}, forming its current structure and name {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1120 }}. It is a shrine closely tied to the Yamabuki Kamihira, Yamabuki Naka, and Shimohei districts of Takamori<ref name="yamabukiku">{{Cite web|title=Taizan Shrine|url=https://yamabukiku.main.jp/taizan.html|publisher=Yamabuki Ward of Takamori Town|accessdate=2021-12-07}}</ref>.
=== Chronology === * [[:en:Kan'ei|Kan'ei]] Period: Shrine established (as recorded in the shrine's detailed ledger) {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1118 }}. * {{ill|An'ei|ja|安永|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/An'ei|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 安永 (元号)|1=An'ei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1193910}} 1 (1772): Ōmiya Sansha was rebuilt and relocated {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1119 }}. * 1881 (Meiji 14): Kodama Shrine was merged and renamed "Yamabuki Shrine" {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1120 }}. * 1911 (Meiji 44): Suwa Shrine was merged, and the name was changed to the current Taizan Shrine {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1120 }}. * 2009 (Heisei 21), April 3: The shrine's main hall, kagura hall, office, and cultural facilities were destroyed by fire<ref name="minamishinshu_2013-04-18">{{Cite news|title=Spring Festival at Taizan Shrine in Takamori Town|url=https://minamishinshu.jp/news/culture/高森町の泰山神社で春祭り.html|newspaper=Minamishinshu Shimbun|publisher=Minamishinshu Shimbunsha|date=2013-04-18|accessdate=2021-12-05}}</ref>. * 2012 (Heisei 24), June: The shrine was rebuilt<ref name="minamishinshu_2013-04-18" />. * 2015 (Heisei 27), October 5: Designated [[:en:Corporate Number|corporate number]] 4100005010121<ref name="houjin-bangou" />.
== Grounds ==
According to sources {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |pp=1121–1122 }}: * [[:en:Garden|Garden]]: A garden with a pond is arranged at the front of the precincts. * [[:en:Sandō|Path]]: The garden path leads alongside the garden. A pair of stone lanterns (dated Man'ei era) stand here. * [[:en:Torii|Torii]]: Built in 1901 (Meiji 34), the torii is a stone Myōjin-style gate. * Grand Shrine Inscription Monument: Erected on April 10, 1923 (Taishō 12). Located to the right of the above torii, with inscriptions by [[:en:Kamio Mitsuomi|Kameo Mitsutomi]]. * [[:en:Komainu|Komainu]]: Positioned beyond the torii. Built in 1940 (Shōwa 15) through offerings from parishioners. * Second Torii: Relocated from the merged Kodama Shrine in 1881. * {{ill|Stone lanterns|ja|石灯籠|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Stone lanterns|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 灯籠|1=Stone lantern|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q12012294}}: Present on either side of the second torii (date unknown). They are rare four-legged Miyadachi-style lanterns. * Stele of {{ill|Konpira Gongen|ja|金毘羅大権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Gongen|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 金毘羅権現}} and {{ill|Akiba Gongen|ja|秋葉権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akiha Gongen|qq=}}: Positioned to the left of the main hall.
== Auxiliary Shrines ==
According to sources. Parentheses indicate deities {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1120 }}: * Shinmei Shrine ([[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]]) * Suwa Shrine (Takeminakata) * Inari Shrine ([[:en:Toyouke-hime|Toyoukehime]]) * Asama Shrine ([[:en:Konohanasakuya-hime|Konohanasakuya-hime]]) * Mountain God Shrine ([[:en:Ōyamatsumi|Ōyamatsumi]])
== Festivals == * Spring Festival: Features flower dances, Okame dances, and [[:en:Lion dance|Shishi-mai]]<ref name="yamabukiku" />.
== Cultural Properties == * Sword marked "Made by Nagachika of Shinano Province": A sword by the Shinano swordsmith Nagachika (Tenbun–Keichō eras). Length: 2 shaku, 3 sun, 4 bu. Originally belonging to the Zakōji family, it was bought back through parishioners' donations and enshrined {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |pp=1120–1121 }}.
== Access == {{Maplink2|frame=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-width=220|frame-height=240|text=Map. Shimodaira Station is in the lower right.}} ;[[:en:Public Transport|Public Transport]] :The nearest station is [[:en:Shimodaira Station|Shimodaira Station]] on the [[:en:Iida Line|Iida Line]] operated by [[:en:Central Japan Railway Company|JR Central]]. It is 701 meters away in a straight line<ref name="mapion" />.

The shrine was historically known as "Hachiman Shrine," "Ōmiya," or "Ōmiya Sansha," and was highly revered by the {{ill|Zakōji clan|ja|座光寺氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zakōji clan|1=Zakōji clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109364687}} {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |pp=1118–1119 }}. It is a shrine closely tied to the Yamabuki Kamihira, Yamabuki Naka, and Shimohei districts of Takamori<ref name="yamabukiku">{{Cite web|title=Taizan Shrine|url=https://yamabukiku.main.jp/taizan.html|publisher=Yamabuki Ward of Takamori Town|accessdate=2021-12-07}}</ref>.

Located to the right of the above torii, with inscriptions by [[:en:Kamio Mitsuomi|Kameo Mitsutomi]]. * [[:en:Komainu|Komainu]]: Positioned beyond the torii. It is a shrine closely tied to the Yamabuki Kamihira, Yamabuki Naka, and Shimohei districts of Takamori<ref name="yamabukiku">{{Cite web|title=Taizan Shrine|url=https://yamabukiku.main.jp/taizan.html|publisher=Yamabuki Ward of Takamori Town|accessdate=2021-12-07}}</ref>.
=== Chronology === * [[:en:Kan'ei|Kan'ei]] Period: Shrine established (as recorded in the shrine's detailed ledger) {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1118 }}. * {{ill|An'ei|ja|安永|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/An'ei|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 安永 (元号)|1=An'ei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1193910}} 1 (1772): Ōmiya Sansha was rebuilt and relocated {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1119 }}. * 1881 (Meiji 14): Kodama Shrine was merged and renamed "Yamabuki Shrine" {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1120 }}. * 1911 (Meiji 44): Suwa Shrine was merged, and the name was changed to the current Taizan Shrine {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1120 }}. * 2009 (Heisei 21), April 3: The shrine's main hall, kagura hall, office, and cultural facilities were destroyed by fire<ref name="minamishinshu_2013-04-18">{{Cite news|title=Spring Festival at Taizan Shrine in Takamori Town|url=https://minamishinshu.jp/news/culture/高森町の泰山神社で春祭り.html|newspaper=Minamishinshu Shimbun|publisher=Minamishinshu Shimbunsha|date=2013-04-18|accessdate=2021-12-05}}</ref>. * 2012 (Heisei 24), June: The shrine was rebuilt<ref name="minamishinshu_2013-04-18" />. * 2015 (Heisei 27), October 5: Designated [[:en:Corporate Number|corporate number]] 4100005010121<ref name="houjin-bangou" />.
== Grounds ==
According to sources {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |pp=1121–1122 }}: * [[:en:Garden|Garden]]: A garden with a pond is arranged at the front of the precincts. * [[:en:Sandō|Path]]: The garden path leads alongside the garden. A pair of stone lanterns (dated Man'ei era) stand here. * [[:en:Torii|Torii]]: Built in 1901 (Meiji 34), the torii is a stone Myōjin-style gate. * Grand Shrine Inscription Monument: Erected on April 10, 1923 (Taishō 12). Located to the right of the above torii, with inscriptions by [[:en:Kamio Mitsuomi|Kameo Mitsutomi]]. * [[:en:Komainu|Komainu]]: Positioned beyond the torii. Built in 1940 (Shōwa 15) through offerings from parishioners. * Second Torii: Relocated from the merged Kodama Shrine in 1881. * {{ill|Stone lanterns|ja|石灯籠|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Stone lanterns|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 灯籠|1=Stone lantern|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q12012294}}: Present on either side of the second torii (date unknown). They are rare four-legged Miyadachi-style lanterns. * Stele of {{ill|Konpira Gongen|ja|金毘羅大権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Gongen|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 金毘羅権現}} and {{ill|Akiba Gongen|ja|秋葉権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akiha Gongen|qq=}}: Positioned to the left of the main hall.
== Auxiliary Shrines ==
According to sources. Parentheses indicate deities {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1120 }}: * Shinmei Shrine ([[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]]) * Suwa Shrine (Takeminakata) * Inari Shrine ([[:en:Toyouke-hime|Toyoukehime]]) * Asama Shrine ([[:en:Konohanasakuya-hime|Konohanasakuya-hime]]) * Mountain God Shrine ([[:en:Ōyamatsumi|Ōyamatsumi]])
== Festivals == * Spring Festival: Features flower dances, Okame dances, and [[:en:Lion dance|Shishi-mai]]<ref name="yamabukiku" />.
== Cultural Properties == * Sword marked "Made by Nagachika of Shinano Province": A sword by the Shinano swordsmith Nagachika (Tenbun–Keichō eras). Length: 2 shaku, 3 sun, 4 bu. Originally belonging to the Zakōji family, it was bought back through parishioners' donations and enshrined {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |pp=1120–1121 }}.
== Access == {{Maplink2|frame=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-width=220|frame-height=240|text=Map. Shimodaira Station is in the lower right.}} ;[[:en:Public Transport|Public Transport]] :The nearest station is [[:en:Shimodaira Station|Shimodaira Station]] on the [[:en:Iida Line|Iida Line]] operated by [[:en:Central Japan Railway Company|JR Central]]. It is 701 meters away in a straight line<ref name="mapion" />.

== History ==
The shrine was historically known as "Hachiman Shrine," "Ōmiya," or "Ōmiya Sansha," and was highly revered by the {{ill|Zakōji clan|ja|座光寺氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zakōji clan|1=Zakōji clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109364687}} {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |pp=1118–1119 }}. After the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] period, it merged with other shrines in {{ill|Yamabuki Village|ja|山吹村|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamabuki Village|1=Yamabuki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17175082}}, forming its current structure and name {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1120 }}. It is a shrine closely tied to the Yamabuki Kamihira, Yamabuki Naka, and Shimohei districts of Takamori<ref name="yamabukiku">{{Cite web|title=Taizan Shrine|url=https://yamabukiku.main.jp/taizan.html|publisher=Yamabuki Ward of Takamori Town|accessdate=2021-12-07}}</ref>.

A pair of stone lanterns (dated Man'ei era) stand here. * [[:en:Torii|Torii]]: Built in 1901 (Meiji 34), the torii is a stone Myōjin-style gate. * Grand Shrine Inscription Monument: Erected on April 10, 1923 (Taishō 12). Located to the right of the above torii, with inscriptions by [[:en:Kamio Mitsuomi|Kameo Mitsutomi]]. * [[:en:Komainu|Komainu]]: Positioned beyond the torii. Built in 1940 (Shōwa 15) through offerings from parishioners. * Second Torii: Relocated from the merged Kodama Shrine in 1881. * {{ill|Stone lanterns|ja|石灯籠|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Stone lanterns|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 灯籠|1=Stone lantern|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q12012294}}: Present on either side of the second torii (date unknown). According to sources. Parentheses indicate deities {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1120 }}: * Shinmei Shrine ([[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]]) * Suwa Shrine (Takeminakata) * Inari Shrine ([[:en:Toyouke-hime|Toyoukehime]]) * Asama Shrine ([[:en:Konohanasakuya-hime|Konohanasakuya-hime]]) * Mountain God Shrine ([[:en:Ōyamatsumi|Ōyamatsumi]])
== Festivals == * Spring Festival: Features flower dances, Okame dances, and [[:en:Lion dance|Shishi-mai]]<ref name="yamabukiku" />.
== Cultural Properties == * Sword marked "Made by Nagachika of Shinano Province": A sword by the Shinano swordsmith Nagachika (Tenbun–Keichō eras). Length: 2 shaku, 3 sun, 4 bu. Originally belonging to the Zakōji family, it was bought back through parishioners' donations and enshrined {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |pp=1120–1121 }}.
== Access == {{Maplink2|frame=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-width=220|frame-height=240|text=Map. Shimodaira Station is in the lower right.}} ;[[:en:Public Transport|Public Transport]] :The nearest station is [[:en:Shimodaira Station|Shimodaira Station]] on the [[:en:Iida Line|Iida Line]] operated by [[:en:Central Japan Railway Company|JR Central]]. It is 701 meters away in a straight line<ref name="mapion" />.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Taizan-jinja]][[ja:泰山神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q109917862]] Wikidata name: Taizan-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:42, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Taizan Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image =File:Taizanjinnja2.jpg |location = [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]][[:en:Shimoina District, Nagano|Shimoina District, Nagano]][[:en:Takamori, Nagano|Takamori, Nagano]] Oaza Yamabuki Azamaruyama 1048<ref>{{Cite web|title=Shrine Introduction Iii Branch|url=https://www.nagano-jinjacho.jp/shibu/hani.htm|publisher=Nagano Shrine Association|accessdate=2021-12-04}}</ref> | Latitude = 35 | Lat_min = 34 | Lat_sec = 28.5 | Longitude = 137 | Lon_min = 53 | Lon_sec = 18.4 |ISO = JP-20 |Label_Position = right |Map = Japan Nagano |deity = [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Homuda Wake no Mikoto]]・{{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}}・[[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]]・Kodama Hikonomikoto・{{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeminakata|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タケミナカタ}} {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1120 }} |Status = |consecration_year = [[:en:Kan'ei|Kan'ei]] Period {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1118 }} |Main_Hall = |Alias = |Festival = |Ritual = |location = {{ill|Takamori|WD=Q1203136}} |coordinates = {{Coord|35.5745878|N|137.8884515|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} [[File:Taizanjinnja1.jpg|thumb|Main Hall]] '''Taizan Shrine''' (泰山神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|shrine]] located in [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]], [[:en:Shimoina District, Nagano|Shimoina District]], {{ill|Takamori, Nagano|ja|高森町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamori, Nagano|1=Takamorimachi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q31469}}<ref name="mapion">{{Cite web|title=Mapion Directory Taizan Shrine|url=https://www.mapion.co.jp/phonebook/M06005/20403/ILSP0061155717_ipclm/|publisher=ONE COMPATH|accessdate=2021-12-04}}</ref>. On registry records, it is listed as "𣳾{{Refnest|group="Note"|"券" written with the top half as "氺".}}"<ref name="houjin-bangou">{{Cite web|title=Information about Taizan Shrine|url=https://www.houjin-bangou.nta.go.jp/henkorireki-johoto.html?selHouzinNo=4100005010121|website=Corporate Number Publication Site|publisher=National Tax Agency|date=2015-10-05|accessdate=2021-12-04}}</ref>.
== Deities ==
According to sources {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1120 }}: * [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Homuda Wake no Mikoto]] * {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}} * [[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] * Kodama Hikonomikoto * {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeminakata|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タケミナカタ}}
== History ==
The shrine was historically known as "Hachiman Shrine," "Ōmiya," or "Ōmiya Sansha," and was highly revered by the {{ill|Zakōji clan|ja|座光寺氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zakōji clan|1=Zakōji clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109364687}} {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |pp=1118–1119 }}. After the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] period, it merged with other shrines in {{ill|Yamabuki Village|ja|山吹村|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamabuki Village|1=Yamabuki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17175082}}, forming its current structure and name {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1120 }}. It is a shrine closely tied to the Yamabuki Kamihira, Yamabuki Naka, and Shimohei districts of Takamori<ref name="yamabukiku">{{Cite web|title=Taizan Shrine|url=https://yamabukiku.main.jp/taizan.html|publisher=Yamabuki Ward of Takamori Town|accessdate=2021-12-07}}</ref>.
=== Chronology === * [[:en:Kan'ei|Kan'ei]] Period: Shrine established (as recorded in the shrine's detailed ledger) {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1118 }}. * {{ill|An'ei|ja|安永|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/An'ei|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 安永 (元号)|1=An'ei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1193910}} 1 (1772): Ōmiya Sansha was rebuilt and relocated {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1119 }}. * 1881 (Meiji 14): Kodama Shrine was merged and renamed "Yamabuki Shrine" {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1120 }}. * 1911 (Meiji 44): Suwa Shrine was merged, and the name was changed to the current Taizan Shrine {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1120 }}. * 2009 (Heisei 21), April 3: The shrine's main hall, kagura hall, office, and cultural facilities were destroyed by fire<ref name="minamishinshu_2013-04-18">{{Cite news|title=Spring Festival at Taizan Shrine in Takamori Town|url=https://minamishinshu.jp/news/culture/高森町の泰山神社で春祭り.html|newspaper=Minamishinshu Shimbun|publisher=Minamishinshu Shimbunsha|date=2013-04-18|accessdate=2021-12-05}}</ref>. * 2012 (Heisei 24), June: The shrine was rebuilt<ref name="minamishinshu_2013-04-18" />. * 2015 (Heisei 27), October 5: Designated [[:en:Corporate Number|corporate number]] 4100005010121<ref name="houjin-bangou" />.
== Grounds ==
According to sources {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |pp=1121–1122 }}: * [[:en:Garden|Garden]]: A garden with a pond is arranged at the front of the precincts. * [[:en:Sandō|Path]]: The garden path leads alongside the garden. A pair of stone lanterns (dated Man'ei era) stand here. * [[:en:Torii|Torii]]: Built in 1901 (Meiji 34), the torii is a stone Myōjin-style gate. * Grand Shrine Inscription Monument: Erected on April 10, 1923 (Taishō 12). Located to the right of the above torii, with inscriptions by [[:en:Kamio Mitsuomi|Kameo Mitsutomi]]. * [[:en:Komainu|Komainu]]: Positioned beyond the torii. Built in 1940 (Shōwa 15) through offerings from parishioners. * Second Torii: Relocated from the merged Kodama Shrine in 1881. * {{ill|Stone lanterns|ja|石灯籠|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Stone lanterns|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 灯籠|1=Stone lantern|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q12012294}}: Present on either side of the second torii (date unknown). They are rare four-legged Miyadachi-style lanterns. * Stele of {{ill|Konpira Gongen|ja|金毘羅大権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Gongen|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 金毘羅権現}} and {{ill|Akiba Gongen|ja|秋葉権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akiha Gongen|qq=}}: Positioned to the left of the main hall.
== Auxiliary Shrines ==
According to sources. Parentheses indicate deities {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1120 }}: * Shinmei Shrine ([[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]]) * Suwa Shrine (Takeminakata) * Inari Shrine ([[:en:Toyouke-hime|Toyoukehime]]) * Asama Shrine ([[:en:Konohanasakuya-hime|Konohanasakuya-hime]]) * Mountain God Shrine ([[:en:Ōyamatsumi|Ōyamatsumi]])
== Festivals == * Spring Festival: Features flower dances, Okame dances, and [[:en:Lion dance|Shishi-mai]]<ref name="yamabukiku" />.
== Cultural Properties == * Sword marked "Made by Nagachika of Shinano Province": A sword by the Shinano swordsmith Nagachika (Tenbun–Keichō eras). Length: 2 shaku, 3 sun, 4 bu. Originally belonging to the Zakōji family, it was bought back through parishioners' donations and enshrined {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |pp=1120–1121 }}.
== Access == {{Maplink2|frame=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-width=220|frame-height=240|text=Map. Shimodaira Station is in the lower right.}} ;[[:en:Public Transport|Public Transport]] :The nearest station is [[:en:Shimodaira Station|Shimodaira Station]] on the [[:en:Iida Line|Iida Line]] operated by [[:en:Central Japan Railway Company|JR Central]]. It is 701 meters away in a straight line<ref name="mapion" />.

The shrine was historically known as "Hachiman Shrine," "Ōmiya," or "Ōmiya Sansha," and was highly revered by the {{ill|Zakōji clan|ja|座光寺氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zakōji clan|1=Zakōji clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109364687}} {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |pp=1118–1119 }}. | Lat_min = 34 | Lat_sec = 28.5 | Longitude = 137 | Lon_min = 53 | Lon_sec = 18.4 |ISO = JP-20 |Label_Position = right |Map = Japan Nagano |deity = [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Homuda Wake no Mikoto]]・{{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}}・[[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]]・Kodama Hikonomikoto・{{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeminakata|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タケミナカタ}} {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1120 }} |Status = |consecration_year = [[:en:Kan'ei|Kan'ei]] Period {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1118 }} |Main_Hall = |Alias = |Festival = |Ritual = |location = {{ill|Takamori|WD=Q1203136}} |coordinates = {{Coord|35.5745878|N|137.8884515|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} [[File:Taizanjinnja1.jpg|thumb|Main Hall]] '''Taizan Shrine''' (泰山神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|shrine]] located in [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]], [[:en:Shimoina District, Nagano|Shimoina District]], {{ill|Takamori, Nagano|ja|高森町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamori, Nagano|1=Takamorimachi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q31469}}<ref name="mapion">{{Cite web|title=Mapion Directory Taizan Shrine|url=https://www.mapion.co.jp/phonebook/M06005/20403/ILSP0061155717_ipclm/|publisher=ONE COMPATH|accessdate=2021-12-04}}</ref>. On registry records, it is listed as "𣳾{{Refnest|group="Note"|"券" written with the top half as "氺".}}"<ref name="houjin-bangou">{{Cite web|title=Information about Taizan Shrine|url=https://www.houjin-bangou.nta.go.jp/henkorireki-johoto.html?selHouzinNo=4100005010121|website=Corporate Number Publication Site|publisher=National Tax Agency|date=2015-10-05|accessdate=2021-12-04}}</ref>.
== Deities ==
According to sources {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1120 }}: * [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Homuda Wake no Mikoto]] * {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}} * [[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] * Kodama Hikonomikoto * {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeminakata|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タケミナカタ}}
== History ==
The shrine was historically known as "Hachiman Shrine," "Ōmiya," or "Ōmiya Sansha," and was highly revered by the {{ill|Zakōji clan|ja|座光寺氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zakōji clan|1=Zakōji clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109364687}} {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |pp=1118–1119 }}. After the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] period, it merged with other shrines in {{ill|Yamabuki Village|ja|山吹村|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamabuki Village|1=Yamabuki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17175082}}, forming its current structure and name {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1120 }}. It is a shrine closely tied to the Yamabuki Kamihira, Yamabuki Naka, and Shimohei districts of Takamori<ref name="yamabukiku">{{Cite web|title=Taizan Shrine|url=https://yamabukiku.main.jp/taizan.html|publisher=Yamabuki Ward of Takamori Town|accessdate=2021-12-07}}</ref>.
=== Chronology === * [[:en:Kan'ei|Kan'ei]] Period: Shrine established (as recorded in the shrine's detailed ledger) {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1118 }}. * {{ill|An'ei|ja|安永|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/An'ei|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 安永 (元号)|1=An'ei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1193910}} 1 (1772): Ōmiya Sansha was rebuilt and relocated {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1119 }}. * 1881 (Meiji 14): Kodama Shrine was merged and renamed "Yamabuki Shrine" {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1120 }}. * 1911 (Meiji 44): Suwa Shrine was merged, and the name was changed to the current Taizan Shrine {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1120 }}. * 2009 (Heisei 21), April 3: The shrine's main hall, kagura hall, office, and cultural facilities were destroyed by fire<ref name="minamishinshu_2013-04-18">{{Cite news|title=Spring Festival at Taizan Shrine in Takamori Town|url=https://minamishinshu.jp/news/culture/高森町の泰山神社で春祭り.html|newspaper=Minamishinshu Shimbun|publisher=Minamishinshu Shimbunsha|date=2013-04-18|accessdate=2021-12-05}}</ref>. * 2012 (Heisei 24), June: The shrine was rebuilt<ref name="minamishinshu_2013-04-18" />. * 2015 (Heisei 27), October 5: Designated [[:en:Corporate Number|corporate number]] 4100005010121<ref name="houjin-bangou" />.
== Grounds ==
According to sources {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |pp=1121–1122 }}: * [[:en:Garden|Garden]]: A garden with a pond is arranged at the front of the precincts. * [[:en:Sandō|Path]]: The garden path leads alongside the garden. A pair of stone lanterns (dated Man'ei era) stand here. * [[:en:Torii|Torii]]: Built in 1901 (Meiji 34), the torii is a stone Myōjin-style gate. * Grand Shrine Inscription Monument: Erected on April 10, 1923 (Taishō 12). Located to the right of the above torii, with inscriptions by [[:en:Kamio Mitsuomi|Kameo Mitsutomi]]. * [[:en:Komainu|Komainu]]: Positioned beyond the torii. Built in 1940 (Shōwa 15) through offerings from parishioners. * Second Torii: Relocated from the merged Kodama Shrine in 1881. * {{ill|Stone lanterns|ja|石灯籠|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Stone lanterns|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 灯籠|1=Stone lantern|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q12012294}}: Present on either side of the second torii (date unknown). They are rare four-legged Miyadachi-style lanterns. * Stele of {{ill|Konpira Gongen|ja|金毘羅大権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Gongen|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 金毘羅権現}} and {{ill|Akiba Gongen|ja|秋葉権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akiha Gongen|qq=}}: Positioned to the left of the main hall.
== Auxiliary Shrines ==
According to sources. Parentheses indicate deities {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1120 }}: * Shinmei Shrine ([[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]]) * Suwa Shrine (Takeminakata) * Inari Shrine ([[:en:Toyouke-hime|Toyoukehime]]) * Asama Shrine ([[:en:Konohanasakuya-hime|Konohanasakuya-hime]]) * Mountain God Shrine ([[:en:Ōyamatsumi|Ōyamatsumi]])
== Festivals == * Spring Festival: Features flower dances, Okame dances, and [[:en:Lion dance|Shishi-mai]]<ref name="yamabukiku" />.
== Cultural Properties == * Sword marked "Made by Nagachika of Shinano Province": A sword by the Shinano swordsmith Nagachika (Tenbun–Keichō eras). Length: 2 shaku, 3 sun, 4 bu. Originally belonging to the Zakōji family, it was bought back through parishioners' donations and enshrined {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |pp=1120–1121 }}.
== Access == {{Maplink2|frame=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-width=220|frame-height=240|text=Map. Shimodaira Station is in the lower right.}} ;[[:en:Public Transport|Public Transport]] :The nearest station is [[:en:Shimodaira Station|Shimodaira Station]] on the [[:en:Iida Line|Iida Line]] operated by [[:en:Central Japan Railway Company|JR Central]]. It is 701 meters away in a straight line<ref name="mapion" />.

== Deities ==
According to sources {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1120 }}: * [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Homuda Wake no Mikoto]] * {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}} * [[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] * Kodama Hikonomikoto * {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeminakata|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タケミナカタ}}
== History ==
The shrine was historically known as "Hachiman Shrine," "Ōmiya," or "Ōmiya Sansha," and was highly revered by the {{ill|Zakōji clan|ja|座光寺氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zakōji clan|1=Zakōji clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109364687}} {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |pp=1118–1119 }}. After the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] period, it merged with other shrines in {{ill|Yamabuki Village|ja|山吹村|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamabuki Village|1=Yamabuki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17175082}}, forming its current structure and name {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1120 }}. It is a shrine closely tied to the Yamabuki Kamihira, Yamabuki Naka, and Shimohei districts of Takamori<ref name="yamabukiku">{{Cite web|title=Taizan Shrine|url=https://yamabukiku.main.jp/taizan.html|publisher=Yamabuki Ward of Takamori Town|accessdate=2021-12-07}}</ref>.
=== Chronology === * [[:en:Kan'ei|Kan'ei]] Period: Shrine established (as recorded in the shrine's detailed ledger) {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1118 }}. * {{ill|An'ei|ja|安永|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/An'ei|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 安永 (元号)|1=An'ei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1193910}} 1 (1772): Ōmiya Sansha was rebuilt and relocated {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1119 }}. * 1881 (Meiji 14): Kodama Shrine was merged and renamed "Yamabuki Shrine" {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1120 }}. * 1911 (Meiji 44): Suwa Shrine was merged, and the name was changed to the current Taizan Shrine {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1120 }}. * 2009 (Heisei 21), April 3: The shrine's main hall, kagura hall, office, and cultural facilities were destroyed by fire<ref name="minamishinshu_2013-04-18">{{Cite news|title=Spring Festival at Taizan Shrine in Takamori Town|url=https://minamishinshu.jp/news/culture/高森町の泰山神社で春祭り.html|newspaper=Minamishinshu Shimbun|publisher=Minamishinshu Shimbunsha|date=2013-04-18|accessdate=2021-12-05}}</ref>. * 2012 (Heisei 24), June: The shrine was rebuilt<ref name="minamishinshu_2013-04-18" />. * 2015 (Heisei 27), October 5: Designated [[:en:Corporate Number|corporate number]] 4100005010121<ref name="houjin-bangou" />.
== Grounds ==
According to sources {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |pp=1121–1122 }}: * [[:en:Garden|Garden]]: A garden with a pond is arranged at the front of the precincts. * [[:en:Sandō|Path]]: The garden path leads alongside the garden. A pair of stone lanterns (dated Man'ei era) stand here. * [[:en:Torii|Torii]]: Built in 1901 (Meiji 34), the torii is a stone Myōjin-style gate. * Grand Shrine Inscription Monument: Erected on April 10, 1923 (Taishō 12). Located to the right of the above torii, with inscriptions by [[:en:Kamio Mitsuomi|Kameo Mitsutomi]]. * [[:en:Komainu|Komainu]]: Positioned beyond the torii. Built in 1940 (Shōwa 15) through offerings from parishioners. * Second Torii: Relocated from the merged Kodama Shrine in 1881. * {{ill|Stone lanterns|ja|石灯籠|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Stone lanterns|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 灯籠|1=Stone lantern|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q12012294}}: Present on either side of the second torii (date unknown). They are rare four-legged Miyadachi-style lanterns. * Stele of {{ill|Konpira Gongen|ja|金毘羅大権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Gongen|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 金毘羅権現}} and {{ill|Akiba Gongen|ja|秋葉権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akiha Gongen|qq=}}: Positioned to the left of the main hall.
== Auxiliary Shrines ==
According to sources. Parentheses indicate deities {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1120 }}: * Shinmei Shrine ([[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]]) * Suwa Shrine (Takeminakata) * Inari Shrine ([[:en:Toyouke-hime|Toyoukehime]]) * Asama Shrine ([[:en:Konohanasakuya-hime|Konohanasakuya-hime]]) * Mountain God Shrine ([[:en:Ōyamatsumi|Ōyamatsumi]])
== Festivals == * Spring Festival: Features flower dances, Okame dances, and [[:en:Lion dance|Shishi-mai]]<ref name="yamabukiku" />.
== Cultural Properties == * Sword marked "Made by Nagachika of Shinano Province": A sword by the Shinano swordsmith Nagachika (Tenbun–Keichō eras). Length: 2 shaku, 3 sun, 4 bu. Originally belonging to the Zakōji family, it was bought back through parishioners' donations and enshrined {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |pp=1120–1121 }}.
== Access == {{Maplink2|frame=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-width=220|frame-height=240|text=Map. Shimodaira Station is in the lower right.}} ;[[:en:Public Transport|Public Transport]] :The nearest station is [[:en:Shimodaira Station|Shimodaira Station]] on the [[:en:Iida Line|Iida Line]] operated by [[:en:Central Japan Railway Company|JR Central]]. It is 701 meters away in a straight line<ref name="mapion" />.

After the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] period, it merged with other shrines in {{ill|Yamabuki Village|ja|山吹村|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamabuki Village|1=Yamabuki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17175082}}, forming its current structure and name {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1120 }}. It is a shrine closely tied to the Yamabuki Kamihira, Yamabuki Naka, and Shimohei districts of Takamori<ref name="yamabukiku">{{Cite web|title=Taizan Shrine|url=https://yamabukiku.main.jp/taizan.html|publisher=Yamabuki Ward of Takamori Town|accessdate=2021-12-07}}</ref>.
=== Chronology === * [[:en:Kan'ei|Kan'ei]] Period: Shrine established (as recorded in the shrine's detailed ledger) {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1118 }}. * {{ill|An'ei|ja|安永|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/An'ei|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 安永 (元号)|1=An'ei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1193910}} 1 (1772): Ōmiya Sansha was rebuilt and relocated {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1119 }}. * 1881 (Meiji 14): Kodama Shrine was merged and renamed "Yamabuki Shrine" {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1120 }}. * 1911 (Meiji 44): Suwa Shrine was merged, and the name was changed to the current Taizan Shrine {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1120 }}. * 2009 (Heisei 21), April 3: The shrine's main hall, kagura hall, office, and cultural facilities were destroyed by fire<ref name="minamishinshu_2013-04-18">{{Cite news|title=Spring Festival at Taizan Shrine in Takamori Town|url=https://minamishinshu.jp/news/culture/高森町の泰山神社で春祭り.html|newspaper=Minamishinshu Shimbun|publisher=Minamishinshu Shimbunsha|date=2013-04-18|accessdate=2021-12-05}}</ref>. * 2012 (Heisei 24), June: The shrine was rebuilt<ref name="minamishinshu_2013-04-18" />. * 2015 (Heisei 27), October 5: Designated [[:en:Corporate Number|corporate number]] 4100005010121<ref name="houjin-bangou" />.
== Grounds ==
According to sources {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |pp=1121–1122 }}: * [[:en:Garden|Garden]]: A garden with a pond is arranged at the front of the precincts. * [[:en:Sandō|Path]]: The garden path leads alongside the garden. A pair of stone lanterns (dated Man'ei era) stand here. * [[:en:Torii|Torii]]: Built in 1901 (Meiji 34), the torii is a stone Myōjin-style gate. * Grand Shrine Inscription Monument: Erected on April 10, 1923 (Taishō 12). Located to the right of the above torii, with inscriptions by [[:en:Kamio Mitsuomi|Kameo Mitsutomi]]. * [[:en:Komainu|Komainu]]: Positioned beyond the torii. Built in 1940 (Shōwa 15) through offerings from parishioners. * Second Torii: Relocated from the merged Kodama Shrine in 1881. * {{ill|Stone lanterns|ja|石灯籠|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Stone lanterns|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 灯籠|1=Stone lantern|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q12012294}}: Present on either side of the second torii (date unknown). They are rare four-legged Miyadachi-style lanterns. * Stele of {{ill|Konpira Gongen|ja|金毘羅大権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Gongen|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 金毘羅権現}} and {{ill|Akiba Gongen|ja|秋葉権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akiha Gongen|qq=}}: Positioned to the left of the main hall.
== Auxiliary Shrines ==
According to sources. Parentheses indicate deities {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1120 }}: * Shinmei Shrine ([[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]]) * Suwa Shrine (Takeminakata) * Inari Shrine ([[:en:Toyouke-hime|Toyoukehime]]) * Asama Shrine ([[:en:Konohanasakuya-hime|Konohanasakuya-hime]]) * Mountain God Shrine ([[:en:Ōyamatsumi|Ōyamatsumi]])
== Festivals == * Spring Festival: Features flower dances, Okame dances, and [[:en:Lion dance|Shishi-mai]]<ref name="yamabukiku" />.
== Cultural Properties == * Sword marked "Made by Nagachika of Shinano Province": A sword by the Shinano swordsmith Nagachika (Tenbun–Keichō eras). Length: 2 shaku, 3 sun, 4 bu. Originally belonging to the Zakōji family, it was bought back through parishioners' donations and enshrined {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |pp=1120–1121 }}.

It is 701 meters away in a straight line<ref name="mapion" />.

[[File:Taizanjinnja1.jpg|thumb|Main Hall]] '''Taizan Shrine''' (泰山神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|shrine]] located in [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]], [[:en:Shimoina District, Nagano|Shimoina == History ==
The shrine was historically known as "Hachiman Shrine," "Ōmiya," or "Ōmiya Sansha," and was highly revered by the {{ill|Zakōji clan|ja|座光寺氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zakōji clan|1=Zakōji clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109364687}} {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |pp=1118–1119 }}. After the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] period, it merged with other shrines in {{ill|Yamabuki Village|ja|山吹村|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamabuki Village|1=Yamabuki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17175082}}, forming its current structure and name {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1120 }}. It is a shrine closely tied to the Yamabuki Kamihira, Yamabuki Naka, and Shimohei districts of Takamori<ref name="yamabukiku">{{Cite web|title=Taizan Shrine|url=https://yamabukiku.main.jp/taizan.html|publisher=Yamabuki Ward of Takamori Town|accessdate=2021-12-07}}</ref>.

Located to the right of the above torii, with inscriptions by [[:en:Kamio Mitsuomi|Kameo Mitsutomi]]. * [[:en:Komainu|Komainu]]: Positioned beyond the torii. Built in 1940 (Shōwa 15) through offerings from parishioners. * Second Torii: Relocated from the merged Kodama Shrine in 1881. * {{ill|Stone lanterns|ja|石灯籠|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Stone lanterns|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 灯籠|1=Stone lantern|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q12012294}}: Present on either side of the second torii (date unknown). They are rare four-legged Miyadachi-style lanterns. * Stele of {{ill|Konpira Gongen|ja|金毘羅大権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Gongen|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 金毘羅権現}} and {{ill|Akiba Gongen|ja|秋葉権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akiha Gongen|qq=}}: Positioned to the left of the main hall.
== Auxiliary Shrines ==
According to sources. Parentheses indicate deities {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1120 }}: * Shinmei Shrine ([[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]]) * Suwa Shrine (Takeminakata) * Inari Shrine ([[:en:Toyouke-hime|Toyoukehime]]) * Asama Shrine ([[:en:Konohanasakuya-hime|Konohanasakuya-hime]]) * Mountain God Shrine ([[:en:Ōyamatsumi|Ōyamatsumi]])
== Festivals == * Spring Festival: Features flower dances, Okame dances, and [[:en:Lion dance|Shishi-mai]]<ref name="yamabukiku" />.
== Cultural Properties == * Sword marked "Made by Nagachika of Shinano Province": A sword by the Shinano swordsmith Nagachika (Tenbun–Keichō eras). Length: 2 shaku, 3 sun, 4 bu. Originally belonging to the Zakōji family, it was bought back through parishioners' donations and enshrined {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |pp=1120–1121 }}.
== Access == {{Maplink2|frame=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-width=220|frame-height=240|text=Map. Shimodaira Station is in the lower right.}} ;[[:en:Public Transport|Public Transport]] :The nearest station is [[:en:Shimodaira Station|Shimodaira Station]] on the [[:en:Iida Line|Iida Line]] operated by [[:en:Central Japan Railway Company|JR Central]]. It is 701 meters away in a straight line<ref name="mapion" />.
== Notes ==

=== References === {{Reflist}}

wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Taizan-jinja]][[ja:泰山神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q109917862]] Wikidata name: Taizan-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:42, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Taizan Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image =File:Taizanjinnja2.jpg |location = {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|en|Nagano Prefecture|WD=Q127877|lt=Nagano Prefecture}}{{ill|Shimoina District, Nagano|en|Shimoina District, Nagano|WD=Q726999|lt=Shimoina District, Nagano}}{{ill|Takamori, Nagano|en|Takamori, Nagano|WD=Q1203136|lt=Takamori, Nagano}} Oaza Yamabuki Azamaruyama 1048<ref>{{Cite web|title=Shrine Introduction Iii Branch|url=https://www.nagano-jinjacho.jp/shibu/hani.htm|publisher=Nagano Shrine Association|accessdate=2021-12-04}}</ref> | Latitude = 35 | Lat_min = 34 | Lat_sec = 28.5 | Longitude = 137 | Lon_min = 53 | Lon_sec = 18.4 |ISO = JP-20 |Label_Position = right |Map = Japan Nagano |deity = {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Homuda Wake no Mikoto}}・{{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}}・{{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|en|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}・Kodama Hikonomikoto・{{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeminakata|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タケミナカタ}} {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1120 }} |Status = |consecration_year = {{ill|Kan'ei|en|Kan'ei|WD=Q1192913|lt=Kan'ei}} Period {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1118 }} |Main_Hall = |Alias = |Festival = |Ritual = |location = {{ill|Takamori|WD=Q1203136}} |coordinates = {{Coord|35.5745878|N|137.8884515|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} [[File:Taizanjinnja1.jpg|thumb|Main Hall]] '''Taizan Shrine''' (泰山神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=shrine}} located in {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|en|Nagano Prefecture|WD=Q127877|lt=Nagano Prefecture}}, {{ill|Shimoina District, Nagano|en|Shimoina District, Nagano|WD=Q726999|lt=Shimoina District}}, {{ill|Takamori, Nagano|ja|高森町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamori, Nagano|1=Takamorimachi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q31469}}<ref name="mapion">{{Cite web|title=Mapion Directory Taizan Shrine|url=https://www.mapion.co.jp/phonebook/M06005/20403/ILSP0061155717_ipclm/|publisher=ONE COMPATH|accessdate=2021-12-04}}</ref>. On registry records, it is listed as "𣳾{{Refnest|group="Note"|"券" written with the top half as "氺".}}"<ref name="houjin-bangou">{{Cite web|title=Information about Taizan Shrine|url=https://www.houjin-bangou.nta.go.jp/henkorireki-johoto.html?selHouzinNo=4100005010121|website=Corporate Number Publication Site|publisher=National Tax Agency|date=2015-10-05|accessdate=2021-12-04}}</ref>.
== Deities ==
According to sources {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1120 }}: * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Homuda Wake no Mikoto}} * {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}} * {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|en|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} * Kodama Hikonomikoto * {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeminakata|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タケミナカタ}}
== History ==
The shrine was historically known as "Hachiman Shrine," "Ōmiya," or "Ōmiya Sansha," and was highly revered by the {{ill|Zakōji clan|ja|座光寺氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zakōji clan|1=Zakōji clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109364687}} {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |pp=1118–1119 }}. After the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}} period, it merged with other shrines in {{ill|Yamabuki Village|ja|山吹村|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamabuki Village|1=Yamabuki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17175082}}, forming its current structure and name {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1120 }}. It is a shrine closely tied to the Yamabuki Kamihira, Yamabuki Naka, and Shimohei districts of Takamori<ref name="yamabukiku">{{Cite web|title=Taizan Shrine|url=https://yamabukiku.main.jp/taizan.html|publisher=Yamabuki Ward of Takamori Town|accessdate=2021-12-07}}</ref>.
=== Chronology === * {{ill|Kan'ei|en|Kan'ei|WD=Q1192913|lt=Kan'ei}} Period: Shrine established (as recorded in the shrine's detailed ledger) {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1118 }}. * {{ill|An'ei|ja|安永|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/An'ei|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 安永 (元号)|1=An'ei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1193910}} 1 (1772): Ōmiya Sansha was rebuilt and relocated {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1119 }}. * 1881 (Meiji 14): Kodama Shrine was merged and renamed "Yamabuki Shrine" {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1120 }}. * 1911 (Meiji 44): Suwa Shrine was merged, and the name was changed to the current Taizan Shrine {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1120 }}. * 2009 (Heisei 21), April 3: The shrine's main hall, kagura hall, office, and cultural facilities were destroyed by fire<ref name="minamishinshu_2013-04-18">{{Cite news|title=Spring Festival at Taizan Shrine in Takamori Town|url=https://minamishinshu.jp/news/culture/高森町の泰山神社で春祭り.html|newspaper=Minamishinshu Shimbun|publisher=Minamishinshu Shimbunsha|date=2013-04-18|accessdate=2021-12-05}}</ref>. * 2012 (Heisei 24), June: The shrine was rebuilt<ref name="minamishinshu_2013-04-18" />. * 2015 (Heisei 27), October 5: Designated {{ill|Corporate Number|en|Corporate Number|WD=Q18535356|lt=corporate number}} 4100005010121<ref name="houjin-bangou" />.
== Grounds ==
According to sources {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |pp=1121–1122 }}: * {{ill|Garden|en|Garden|WD=Q1107656|lt=Garden}}: A garden with a pond is arranged at the front of the precincts. * {{ill|Sandō|en|Sandō|WD=Q667783|lt=Path}}: The garden path leads alongside the garden. A pair of stone lanterns (dated Man'ei era) stand here. * {{ill|Torii|en|Torii|WD=Q234731|lt=Torii}}: Built in 1901 (Meiji 34), the torii is a stone Myōjin-style gate. * Grand Shrine Inscription Monument: Erected on April 10, 1923 (Taishō 12). Located to the right of the above torii, with inscriptions by {{ill|Kamio Mitsuomi|en|Kamio Mitsuomi|WD=Q853895|lt=Kameo Mitsutomi}}. * {{ill|Komainu|en|Komainu|WD=Q20155846|lt=Komainu}}: Positioned beyond the torii. Built in 1940 (Shōwa 15) through offerings from parishioners. * Second Torii: Relocated from the merged Kodama Shrine in 1881. * {{ill|Stone lanterns|ja|石灯籠|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Stone lanterns|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 灯籠|1=Stone lantern|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q12012294}}: Present on either side of the second torii (date unknown). They are rare four-legged Miyadachi-style lanterns. * Stele of {{ill|Konpira Gongen|ja|金毘羅大権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Gongen|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 金毘羅権現}} and {{ill|Akiba Gongen|ja|秋葉権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akiha Gongen|qq=}}: Positioned to the left of the main hall.
== Auxiliary Shrines ==
According to sources. Parentheses indicate deities {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1120 }}: * Shinmei Shrine ({{ill|Amaterasu|en|Amaterasu|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami}}) * Suwa Shrine (Takeminakata) * Inari Shrine ({{ill|Toyouke-hime|en|Toyouke-hime|WD=Q2983724|lt=Toyoukehime}}) * Asama Shrine ({{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|en|Konohanasakuya-hime|WD=Q1781862|lt=Konohanasakuya-hime}}) * Mountain God Shrine ({{ill|Ōyamatsumi|en|Ōyamatsumi|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi}})
== Festivals == * Spring Festival: Features flower dances, Okame dances, and {{ill|Lion dance|en|Lion dance|WD=Q713866|lt=Shishi-mai}}<ref name="yamabukiku" />.
== Cultural Properties == * Sword marked "Made by Nagachika of Shinano Province": A sword by the Shinano swordsmith Nagachika (Tenbun–Keichō eras). Length: 2 shaku, 3 sun, 4 bu. Originally belonging to the Zakōji family, it was bought back through parishioners' donations and enshrined {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |pp=1120–1121 }}.
== Access == {{Maplink2|frame=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-width=220|frame-height=240|text=Map. Shimodaira Station is in the lower right.}} ;[[:en:Public Transport|Public Transport]] :The nearest station is {{ill|Shimodaira Station|en|Shimodaira Station|WD=Q988164|lt=Shimodaira Station}} on the {{ill|Iida Line|en|Iida Line|WD=Q771305|lt=Iida Line}} operated by {{ill|Central Japan Railway Company|en|Central Japan Railway Company|WD=Q513679|lt=JR Central}}. It is 701 meters away in a straight line<ref name="mapion" />.
== Notes ==
=== Annotations === {{Reflist|group="Note"}}
=== References === {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == * {{Cite book|title=Upper Volume of the Takamori Town History|author=Takamori Town History Compilation Committee|publisher=Takamori Town History Publication Committee|year=1972|ref=harv}} == See Also == {{Commonscat}} * {{ill|Yamabuki Jin'ya|ja|山吹陣屋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamabuki Jin'ya|1=Yamabuki Jin'ya|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q48760371}}

== Auxiliary Shrines ==
According to sources. Parentheses indicate deities {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1120 }}: * Shinmei Shrine ({{ill|Amaterasu|en|Amaterasu|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami}}) * Suwa Shrine (Takeminakata) * Inari Shrine ({{ill|Toyouke-hime|en|Toyouke-hime|WD=Q2983724|lt=Toyoukehime}}) * Asama Shrine ({{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|en|Konohanasakuya-hime|WD=Q1781862|lt=Konohanasakuya-hime}}) * Mountain God Shrine ({{ill|Ōyamatsumi|en|Ōyamatsumi|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi}})
== Festivals == * Spring Festival: Features flower dances, Okame dances, and {{ill|Lion dance|en|Lion dance|WD=Q713866|lt=Shishi-mai}}<ref name="yamabukiku" />.
== Cultural Properties == * Sword marked "Made by Nagachika of Shinano Province": A sword by the Shinano swordsmith Nagachika (Tenbun–Keichō eras). Length: 2 shaku, 3 sun, 4 bu. Originally belonging to the Zakōji family, it was bought back through parishioners' donations and enshrined {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |pp=1120–1121 }}.
== Access == {{Maplink2|frame=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-width=220|frame-height=240|text=Map. Shimodaira Station is in the lower right.}} ;[[:en:Public Transport|Public Transport]] :The nearest station is {{ill|Shimodaira Station|en|Shimodaira Station|WD=Q988164|lt=Shimodaira Station}} on the {{ill|Iida Line|en|Iida Line|WD=Q771305|lt=Iida Line}} operated by {{ill|Central Japan Railway Company|en|Central Japan Railway Company|WD=Q513679|lt=JR Central}}. It is 701 meters away in a straight line<ref name="mapion" />.

{{ill|Nagano Prefecture|en|Nagano Prefecture|WD=Q127877|lt=Nagano Prefecture}}{{ill|Shimoina District, Nagano|en|Shimoina District, Nagano|WD=Q726999|lt=Shimoina District, Nagano}}{{ill|Takamori, 53 | Lon_sec = 18.4 |ISO = JP-20 |Label_Position = right |Map = Japan Nagano |deity = {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Homuda Wake no Mikoto}}・{{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|comment=changed title based on Hikonomikoto・{{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeminakata|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タケミナカタ}} {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1120 }} |Status = |consecration_year = {{ill|Kan'ei|en|Kan'ei|WD=Q1192913|lt=Kan'ei}} Period {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1118 }} |Main_Hall = |Alias = |Festival = |Ritual = |location = {{ill|Takamori|WD=Q1203136}} |coordinates = {{Coord|35.5745878|N|137.8884515|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} [[File:Taizanjinnja1.jpg|thumb|Main Hall]] '''Taizan Shrine''' (泰山神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=shrine}} located in {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|en|Nagano Prefecture|WD=Q127877|lt=Nagano Prefecture}}, {{ill|Shimoina District, Nagano|en|Shimoina District, Nagano|WD=Q726999|lt=Shimoina District}}, {{ill|Takamori, Nagano|ja|高森町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamori, Nagano|1=Takamorimachi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q31469}}<ref name="mapion">{{Cite web|title=Mapion Directory Taizan Shrine|url=https://www.mapion.co.jp/phonebook/M06005/20403/ILSP0061155717_ipclm/|publisher=ONE COMPATH|accessdate=2021-12-04}}</ref>. On registry records, it is listed as "𣳾{{Refnest|group="Note"|"券" written with the top half as "氺".}}"<ref name="houjin-bangou">{{Cite web|title=Information about Taizan Shrine|url=https://www.houjin-bangou.nta.go.jp/henkorireki-johoto.html?selHouzinNo=4100005010121|website=Corporate Number Publication Site|publisher=National Tax Agency|date=2015-10-05|accessdate=2021-12-04}}</ref>.
== Deities ==
According to sources {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1120 }}: * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Homuda Wake no Mikoto}} * {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}} * {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|en|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} * Kodama Hikonomikoto * {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeminakata|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タケミナカタ}}
== History ==
The shrine was historically known as "Hachiman Shrine," "Ōmiya," or "Ōmiya Sansha," and was highly revered by the {{ill|Zakōji clan|ja|座光寺氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zakōji clan|1=Zakōji clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109364687}} {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |pp=1118–1119 }}. After the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}} period, it merged with other shrines in {{ill|Yamabuki Village|ja|山吹村|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamabuki Village|1=Yamabuki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17175082}}, forming its current structure and name {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1120 }}. It is a shrine closely tied to the Yamabuki Kamihira, Yamabuki Naka, and Shimohei districts of Takamori<ref name="yamabukiku">{{Cite web|title=Taizan Shrine|url=https://yamabukiku.main.jp/taizan.html|publisher=Yamabuki Ward of Takamori Town|accessdate=2021-12-07}}</ref>.
=== Chronology === * {{ill|Kan'ei|en|Kan'ei|WD=Q1192913|lt=Kan'ei}} Period: Shrine established (as recorded in the shrine's detailed ledger) {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1118 }}. * {{ill|An'ei|ja|安永|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/An'ei|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 安永 (元号)|1=An'ei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1193910}} 1 (1772): Ōmiya Sansha was rebuilt and relocated {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1119 }}. * 1881 (Meiji 14): Kodama Shrine was merged and renamed "Yamabuki Shrine" {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1120 }}. * 1911 (Meiji 44): Suwa Shrine was merged, and the name was changed to the current Taizan Shrine {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1120 }}. * 2009 (Heisei 21), April 3: The shrine's main hall, kagura hall, office, and cultural facilities were destroyed by fire<ref name="minamishinshu_2013-04-18">{{Cite news|title=Spring Festival at Taizan Shrine in Takamori Town|url=https://minamishinshu.jp/news/culture/高森町の泰山神社で春祭り.html|newspaper=Minamishinshu Shimbun|publisher=Minamishinshu Shimbunsha|date=2013-04-18|accessdate=2021-12-05}}</ref>. * 2012 (Heisei 24), June: The shrine was rebuilt<ref name="minamishinshu_2013-04-18" />. * 2015 (Heisei 27), October 5: Designated {{ill|Corporate Number|en|Corporate Number|WD=Q18535356|lt=corporate number}} 4100005010121<ref name="houjin-bangou" />.
== Grounds ==
According to sources {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |pp=1121–1122 }}: * {{ill|Garden|en|Garden|WD=Q1107656|lt=Garden}}: A garden with a pond is arranged at the front of the precincts. * {{ill|Sandō|en|Sandō|WD=Q667783|lt=Path}}: The garden path leads alongside the garden. A pair of stone lanterns (dated Man'ei era) stand here. * {{ill|Torii|en|Torii|WD=Q234731|lt=Torii}}: Built in 1901 (Meiji 34), the torii is a stone Myōjin-style gate. * Grand Shrine Inscription Monument: Erected on April 10, 1923 (Taishō 12). Located to the right of the above torii, with inscriptions by {{ill|Kamio Mitsuomi|en|Kamio Mitsuomi|WD=Q853895|lt=Kameo Mitsutomi}}. * {{ill|Komainu|en|Komainu|WD=Q20155846|lt=Komainu}}: Positioned beyond the torii. Built in 1940 (Shōwa 15) through offerings from parishioners. * Second Torii: Relocated from the merged Kodama Shrine in 1881. * {{ill|Stone lanterns|ja|石灯籠|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Stone lanterns|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 灯籠|1=Stone lantern|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q12012294}}: Present on either side of the second torii (date unknown). They are rare four-legged Miyadachi-style lanterns. * Stele of {{ill|Konpira Gongen|ja|金毘羅大権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Gongen|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 金毘羅権現}} and {{ill|Akiba Gongen|ja|秋葉権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akiha Gongen|qq=}}: Positioned to the left of the main hall.
== Auxiliary Shrines ==
According to sources. Parentheses indicate deities {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1120 }}: * Shinmei Shrine ({{ill|Amaterasu|en|Amaterasu|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami}}) * Suwa Shrine (Takeminakata) * Inari Shrine ({{ill|Toyouke-hime|en|Toyouke-hime|WD=Q2983724|lt=Toyoukehime}}) * Asama Shrine ({{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|en|Konohanasakuya-hime|WD=Q1781862|lt=Konohanasakuya-hime}}) * Mountain God Shrine ({{ill|Ōyamatsumi|en|Ōyamatsumi|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi}})
== Festivals == * Spring Festival: Features flower dances, Okame dances, and {{ill|Lion dance|en|Lion dance|WD=Q713866|lt=Shishi-mai}}<ref name="yamabukiku" />.
== Cultural Properties == * Sword marked "Made by Nagachika of Shinano Province": A sword by the Shinano swordsmith Nagachika (Tenbun–Keichō eras). Length: 2 shaku, 3 sun, 4 bu. Originally belonging to the Zakōji family, it was bought back through parishioners' donations and enshrined {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |pp=1120–1121 }}.
== Access == {{Maplink2|frame=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-width=220|frame-height=240|text=Map. Shimodaira Station is in the lower right.}} ;[[:en:Public Transport|Public Transport]] :The nearest station is {{ill|Shimodaira Station|en|Shimodaira Station|WD=Q988164|lt=Shimodaira Station}} on the {{ill|Iida Line|en|Iida Line|WD=Q771305|lt=Iida Line}} operated by {{ill|Central Japan Railway Company|en|Central Japan Railway Company|WD=Q513679|lt=JR Central}}. It is 701 meters away in a straight line<ref name="mapion" />.

Originally belonging to the Zakōji family, it was bought back through parishioners' donations and enshrined {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |pp=1120–1121 }}.
== Access == {{Maplink2|frame=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-width=220|frame-height=240|text=Map. Shimodaira Station is in the lower right.}} ;[[:en:Public Transport|Public Transport]] :The nearest station is {{ill|Shimodaira Station|en|Shimodaira Station|WD=Q988164|lt=Shimodaira Station}} on the {{ill|Iida Line|en|Iida Line|WD=Q771305|lt=Iida Line}} operated by {{ill|Central Japan Railway Company|en|Central Japan Railway Company|WD=Q513679|lt=JR Central}}. It is 701 meters away in a straight line<ref name="mapion" />.

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Taizan Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image =File:Taizanjinnja2.jpg |location = {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|en|Nagano Prefecture|WD=Q127877|lt=Nagano Prefecture}}{{ill|Shimoina District, Nagano|en|Shimoina District, Nagano|WD=Q726999|lt=Shimoina District, Nagano}}{{ill|Takamori, Nagano|en|Takamori, Nagano|WD=Q1203136|lt=Takamori, Nagano}} Oaza Yamabuki Azamaruyama 1048<ref>{{Cite web|title=Shrine Introduction Iii Branch|url=https://www.nagano-jinjacho.jp/shibu/hani.htm|publisher=Nagano Shrine Association|accessdate=2021-12-04}}</ref> | Latitude = 35 | Lat_min = 34 | Lat_sec = 28.5 | Longitude = 137 | Lon_min = 53 | Lon_sec = 18.4 |ISO = JP-20 |Label_Position = right |Map = Japan Nagano |deity = {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Homuda Wake no Mikoto}}・{{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}}・{{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|en|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}・Kodama Hikonomikoto・{{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeminakata|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タケミナカタ}} {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1120 }} |Status = |consecration_year = {{ill|Kan'ei|en|Kan'ei|WD=Q1192913|lt=Kan'ei}} Period {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1118 }} |Main_Hall = |Alias = |Festival = |Ritual = |location = {{ill|Takamori|WD=Q1203136}} |coordinates = {{Coord|35.5745878|N|137.8884515|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} [[File:Taizanjinnja1.jpg|thumb|Main Hall]] '''Taizan Shrine''' (泰山神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=shrine}} located in {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|en|Nagano Prefecture|WD=Q127877|lt=Nagano Prefecture}}, {{ill|Shimoina District, Nagano|en|Shimoina District, Nagano|WD=Q726999|lt=Shimoina District}}, {{ill|Takamori, Nagano|ja|高森町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamori, Nagano|1=Takamorimachi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q31469}}<ref name="mapion">{{Cite web|title=Mapion Directory Taizan Shrine|url=https://www.mapion.co.jp/phonebook/M06005/20403/ILSP0061155717_ipclm/|publisher=ONE COMPATH|accessdate=2021-12-04}}</ref>. On registry records, it is listed as "𣳾{{Refnest|group="Note"|"券" written with the top half as "氺".}}"<ref name="houjin-bangou">{{Cite web|title=Information about Taizan Shrine|url=https://www.houjin-bangou.nta.go.jp/henkorireki-johoto.html?selHouzinNo=4100005010121|website=Corporate Number Publication Site|publisher=National Tax Agency|date=2015-10-05|accessdate=2021-12-04}}</ref>.
== Deities ==
According to sources {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1120 }}: * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Homuda Wake no Mikoto}} * {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}} * {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|en|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} * Kodama Hikonomikoto * {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeminakata|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タケミナカタ}}
== History ==
The shrine was historically known as "Hachiman Shrine," "Ōmiya," or "Ōmiya Sansha," and was highly revered by the {{ill|Zakōji clan|ja|座光寺氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zakōji clan|1=Zakōji clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109364687}} {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |pp=1118–1119 }}. After the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}} period, it merged with other shrines in {{ill|Yamabuki Village|ja|山吹村|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamabuki Village|1=Yamabuki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17175082}}, forming its current structure and name {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1120 }}. It is a shrine closely tied to the Yamabuki Kamihira, Yamabuki Naka, and Shimohei districts of Takamori<ref name="yamabukiku">{{Cite web|title=Taizan Shrine|url=https://yamabukiku.main.jp/taizan.html|publisher=Yamabuki Ward of Takamori Town|accessdate=2021-12-07}}</ref>.
=== Chronology === * {{ill|Kan'ei|en|Kan'ei|WD=Q1192913|lt=Kan'ei}} Period: Shrine established (as recorded in the shrine's detailed ledger) {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1118 }}. * {{ill|An'ei|ja|安永|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/An'ei|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 安永 (元号)|1=An'ei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1193910}} 1 (1772): Ōmiya Sansha was rebuilt and relocated {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1119 }}. * 1881 (Meiji 14): Kodama Shrine was merged and renamed "Yamabuki Shrine" {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1120 }}. * 1911 (Meiji 44): Suwa Shrine was merged, and the name was changed to the current Taizan Shrine {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1120 }}. * 2009 (Heisei 21), April 3: The shrine's main hall, kagura hall, office, and cultural facilities were destroyed by fire<ref name="minamishinshu_2013-04-18">{{Cite news|title=Spring Festival at Taizan Shrine in Takamori Town|url=https://minamishinshu.jp/news/culture/高森町の泰山神社で春祭り.html|newspaper=Minamishinshu Shimbun|publisher=Minamishinshu Shimbunsha|date=2013-04-18|accessdate=2021-12-05}}</ref>. * 2012 (Heisei 24), June: The shrine was rebuilt<ref name="minamishinshu_2013-04-18" />. * 2015 (Heisei 27), October 5: Designated {{ill|Corporate Number|en|Corporate Number|WD=Q18535356|lt=corporate number}} 4100005010121<ref name="houjin-bangou" />.
== Grounds ==
According to sources {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |pp=1121–1122 }}: * {{ill|Garden|en|Garden|WD=Q1107656|lt=Garden}}: A garden with a pond is arranged at the front of the precincts. * {{ill|Sandō|en|Sandō|WD=Q667783|lt=Path}}: The garden path leads alongside the garden. A pair of stone lanterns (dated Man'ei era) stand here. * {{ill|Torii|en|Torii|WD=Q234731|lt=Torii}}: Built in 1901 (Meiji 34), the torii is a stone Myōjin-style gate. * Grand Shrine Inscription Monument: Erected on April 10, 1923 (Taishō 12). Located to the right of the above torii, with inscriptions by {{ill|Kamio Mitsuomi|en|Kamio Mitsuomi|WD=Q853895|lt=Kameo Mitsutomi}}. * {{ill|Komainu|en|Komainu|WD=Q20155846|lt=Komainu}}: Positioned beyond the torii. Built in 1940 (Shōwa 15) through offerings from parishioners. * Second Torii: Relocated from the merged Kodama Shrine in 1881. * {{ill|Stone lanterns|ja|石灯籠|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Stone lanterns|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 灯籠|1=Stone lantern|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q12012294}}: Present on either side of the second torii (date unknown). They are rare four-legged Miyadachi-style lanterns. * Stele of {{ill|Konpira Gongen|ja|金毘羅大権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Gongen|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 金毘羅権現}} and {{ill|Akiba Gongen|ja|秋葉権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akiha Gongen|qq=}}: Positioned to the left of the main hall.
== Auxiliary Shrines ==
According to sources. Parentheses indicate deities {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |p=1120 }}: * Shinmei Shrine ({{ill|Amaterasu|en|Amaterasu|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami}}) * Suwa Shrine (Takeminakata) * Inari Shrine ({{ill|Toyouke-hime|en|Toyouke-hime|WD=Q2983724|lt=Toyoukehime}}) * Asama Shrine ({{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|en|Konohanasakuya-hime|WD=Q1781862|lt=Konohanasakuya-hime}}) * Mountain God Shrine ({{ill|Ōyamatsumi|en|Ōyamatsumi|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi}})
== Festivals == * Spring Festival: Features flower dances, Okame dances, and {{ill|Lion dance|en|Lion dance|WD=Q713866|lt=Shishi-mai}}<ref name="yamabukiku" />.
== Cultural Properties == * Sword marked "Made by Nagachika of Shinano Province": A sword by the Shinano swordsmith Nagachika (Tenbun–Keichō eras). Length: 2 shaku, 3 sun, 4 bu. Originally belonging to the Zakōji family, it was bought back through parishioners' donations and enshrined {{Sfn |Takamori Town History Compilation Committee |1972 |pp=1120–1121 }}.
== Access == {{Maplink2|frame=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-width=220|frame-height=240|text=Map. Shimodaira Station is in the lower right.}} ;[[:en:Public Transport|Public Transport]] :The nearest station is {{ill|Shimodaira Station|en|Shimodaira Station|WD=Q988164|lt=Shimodaira Station}} on the {{ill|Iida Line|en|Iida Line|WD=Q771305|lt=Iida Line}} operated by {{ill|Central Japan Railway Company|en|Central Japan Railway Company|WD=Q513679|lt=JR Central}}. It is 701 meters away in a straight line<ref name="mapion" />.
